Programme Highlights

The Doctor of Business Studies (DBS) programme is a high-level academic course that integrates theoretical depth with practical breadth. By fostering independent research capabilities, exploring cutting-edge business topics, and emphasizing a global and interdisciplinary perspective, the DBS programme provides comprehensive support for students transitioning from academic research to industry practice. It aims to equip students to become innovative scholars, outstanding managers, or strategic consultants in the business field.

Unique Features

Comprehensive Business Curriculum: The DBS programme allows students to gain expertise across multiple segments of business including accounting, ESG and sustainability, finance, HR management, supply chain management, digital business and risk management.
Efficient Pathway to Achieve Terminal Degree ： The DBS programme allows students to engage in both coursework and applied business research, enabling them to achieve terminal degrees in three years.
Diverse Student Body: The DBS programme draws a varied student cohort encompassing individuals at different stages of their careers, fostering a dynamic and enriching learning environment for all.
Dual Career Pathways: The DBS programme offers academic enrichment for ambitious individuals to thrive in both academic and industry settings.

Programme Intended Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of the programme, students will be able to:
Demonstrate advanced knowledge of theoretical frameworks, concepts, and principles related to various areas of business studies;
Apply advanced analytical techniques and tools and critical thinking skills in developing innovative solutions to business challenges and identifying new opportunities;
Evaluate and synthesise existing literature and contribute to the advancement of knowledge in business through scholarly inquiry by employing appropriate research methodologies and techniques;
Effectively communicate complex business concepts and research findings to both academic and non-academic audiences; and
Exhibit responsible and ethical leadership qualities, considering the impact of business activities on stakeholders and society.

Committees-Related Information

Programme and Curriculum Committee (PCC)
At the programme level, a Programme and Curriculum Committee (PCC) is set up for a taught postgraduate programme. The major responsibilities are:
To take charge of the degree programme including the design, implementation and review of the curricula, inter alia, the achievement of the intended programme learning outcomes.
To liaise with all Departments/Units/Divisions concerned on programme matters, such as course offerings for each academic year.
To liaise with the Registry/School of Graduate Studies on all academic matters, including review of academic regulations and examination matters.
To review admissions policy for the degree programme.
To deal with all other matters related to the degree programme.

Chairperson of the Faculty's RPSC Sub-com
All academic staff of the programme
At least one student representative
To be appointed by the Chairperson

Board of Examiners (BoE)
A Board of Examiners is set up for a taught postgraduate programme to oversee examination matters. The major duties are:
To maintain the academic standards of the programme at a level appropriate to the type of award.
To approve examination question papers after considering the views and recommendations of External Academic Advisers, where appropriate.
To maintain general supervision of the system of continuous assessment, where it applies.
To maintain the proper conduct of examinations.
To determine students’ examination results.
To assess the progress of students and to report its decisions, including its recommendations for award classification, to the Postgraduate Studies Committee.
To consider appeals referred to it by the Registrar.

Membership
Chairperson:
Members:

Secretary:

Programme Director
Dean of the Faculty of Business
Chairperson of the Faculty's RPSC Sub-com
All staff concerned with the setting and marking of any part of the examinations
To be appointed by the Chairperson

Staff-Student Consultation Committee (SSCC)
The Staff-Student Consultation Committee provides a platform for students to express their views on the programme and individual courses, and for the departments/academic units to solicit student feedback on specific aspects of the programme and courses for continuous improvement. The major responsibilities are:
To promote understanding between students and teaching staff.
To consider feedback from students regarding teaching, learning and course evaluation and issues of importance for enhancing teaching and learning quality.
To consider any other matters of concern to students.
To review and monitor the learning and teaching quality assurance mechanisms and processes within the programme.
To provide feedback to students on changes made to courses and teaching in response to the Course Teaching and Learning Evaluation exercise.
To formulate strategies in helping students to learn more effectively.

Membership
Chairperson:
Members:

Programme Director
At least two staff members teaching the programme in the current academic year
At least three student representative
To be appointed by the Chairperson

Advisory Board (AB)
An Advisory Board shall normally meet once a year to help plan and keep under review the following aspects of work including future developments:
The level and length of courses/programmes concerned in relation to local and regional needs;
The relevance of the courses/programme in relation to local and regional needs;
The prospects of local and regional employment for the graduates;
The adequacy of the equipment and other resources of the academic unit to fulfil local and regional needs;
The development of teaching and other activities carried out jointly by the academic unit and the appropriate sectors of the community;
The investigation, consultancy and other services given by the academic unit(s);
Keep under review the assistance which the community can give to further the objectives of the University in the subject concerned by way of practical training facilities, the provision of part-time teaching staff and equipment, the award of scholarships, student-fellowships, etc.

Home

Faculty of Business

Doctor of Business Studies (DBS)

Programme Overview

Programme Highlights

Explore More

Programme Overview

Programme Highlights
Programme Structure
Course Description
Committees-Related Information
Career Prospects

Programme Highlights

The Doctor of Business Studies (DBS) programme is a high-level academic course that integrates theoretical depth with practical breadth. By fostering independent research capabilities, exploring cutting-edge business topics, and emphasizing a global and interdisciplinary perspective, the DBS programme provides comprehensive support for students transitioning from academic research to industry practice. It aims to equip students to become innovative scholars, outstanding managers, or strategic consultants in the business field.

Unique Features

Comprehensive Business Curriculum: The DBS programme allows students to gain expertise across multiple segments of business including accounting, ESG and sustainability, finance, HR management, supply chain management, digital business and risk management.
Efficient Pathway to Achieve Terminal Degree ： The DBS programme allows students to engage in both coursework and applied business research, enabling them to achieve terminal degrees in three years.
Diverse Student Body: The DBS programme draws a varied student cohort encompassing individuals at different stages of their careers, fostering a dynamic and enriching learning environment for all.
Dual Career Pathways: The DBS programme offers academic enrichment for ambitious individuals to thrive in both academic and industry settings.

Programme Intended Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of the programme, students will be able to:
Demonstrate advanced knowledge of theoretical frameworks, concepts, and principles related to various areas of business studies;
Apply advanced analytical techniques and tools and critical thinking skills in developing innovative solutions to business challenges and identifying new opportunities;
Evaluate and synthesise existing literature and contribute to the advancement of knowledge in business through scholarly inquiry by employing appropriate research methodologies and techniques;
Effectively communicate complex business concepts and research findings to both academic and non-academic audiences; and
Exhibit responsible and ethical leadership qualities, considering the impact of business activities on stakeholders and society.

Admission Requirements

I: General Requirement :
An applicant should hold a Master's degree awarded by a recognized tertiary institution or have equivalent qualifications, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

II: English Language Proficiency Requirement:
An applicant whose degree is not granted by a tertiary institution in Hong Kong, or an English-speaking country should pass ONE of the tests below to meet the English language proficiency requirement:
• IELTS : minimum acceptable score of 6.0 or
• TOEFL : minimum acceptable score of 79 (internet-based test) or
• CET-6: a minimun score of 450 or above in College English Test Band 6 (CET-6), with proof of competency in Spoken English.

other equivalent English qualifications to be considered on a case-by-case basis.

III: Two Recommendation Forms
Click here

IV: Consent Form for Verifying Academic Awards
Click here

V: CQV Report if your degree is awarded by a Mainland university

NOTES：
Student Visa
The application requirements are the same for both local and non-local applicants. Non-local students studying in Hong Kong are required to apply for student visas or entry permit from the Hong Kong Immigration Department. Once admitted, the DBS Programme Office will help the students with the visa application.

Research Interest
All applicants are required to submit a Research Interest about 400-500 words in English.

Interview
Applicants will be required to attend an interview (via online) conducted by the Progamme Director of Doctor of Business Studies (DBS) Programmes Office and one more faculty member.

Message from Dean of Faculty of Business
About Lingnan Univeristy

Message from Dean of Faculty of Business

Prof. LENG Mingming

Welcome to the Doctor of Business Studies (DBS) Programme offered by the Faculty of Business of Lingnan University, accredited by the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB)! In today’s rapidly evolving and competitive business environment, the need for highly skilled professionals with advanced academic qualifications has never been more crucial. The Faculty of Business proudly introduces the new DBS programme, designed to empower ambitious individuals to advance their careers and make significant contributions to the realm of academia and industry alike.



Our DBS programme offers a comprehensive cutting-edge curriculum in business studies, equipping students with the knowledge, expertise, and research capability needed to thrive in both academic and industry settings. By nurturing adept business leaders capable of addressing contemporary challenges with innovative solutions, we aim to drive regional development and foster a culture of excellence.



We invite you to explore the DBS programme, which promises unparalleled academic rigor and a transformative scholarly journey that will elevate your future career prospects to new heights. Join us as we embark on this enlightening and enriching academic voyage together!



Professor LENG Mingming

Dean of the Faculty of Business

Lingnan University

Vision, Mission and Core Values

Home

Faculty of Business

Doctor of Business Studies (DBS)

About Us

About Lingnan Univeristy

Explore More

About Us

Message from Dean of Faculty of Business
About Lingnan Univeristy

At Lingnan, our commitment to quality education is realized through a comprehensive curriculum that builds upon pioneering research to shape the future. This educational approach is enriched by close staff-student relationships, vibrant residential campus life, diverse extra-curricular activities, proactive community service, extensive workplace experience, global alumni network and impact, as well as numerous global learning opportunities. We are evolving from a focus on liberal arts to a broader emphasis on quality education, ensuring that each element of our university experience contributes to the development of well-rounded, informed, and socially responsible individuals.

Vision
To excel as a leading Asian quality education university with international recognition, distinguished by outstanding teaching, learning, scholarship and community engagement.

Mission
Lingnan University is committed to
Providing quality whole-person education by combining the best of the Chinese and Western liberal arts traditions;

Nurturing students to achieve all-round excellence and imbuing them with its core values; and

Encouraging faculty and students to contribute to society through original research and knowledge transfer

Motto
Education for service

Core Values
A collegial community of learning and discovery for students and scholar-teachers
Lingnan aims to build a community of learning and discovery with collegial students and scholar-teachers who respect each other, keep an open mind, embrace diversity, appreciate different views, uphold academic freedom and freedom of expression, and accept responsibility for their words and deeds.

Whole-person cultivation and all-round development
Lingnan endeavours to cultivate students’ whole-person and all-round growth. It encourages students to pursue independent and critical thinking, creativity and innovation, excellent communication skills including a high level of literacy, social responsibility, personal virtue, cultural accomplishment and a passion for lifelong learning.

Community engagement and social responsibility
Lingnan encourages its members to care for others, to be responsible for their own actions, to serve the local community and beyond, and to make a positive impact for the betterment of humanity.

The Lingnan spirit
The Lingnan spirit, which has flourished since Lingnan’s founding in Canton (Guangzhou) in 1888 as Christian College in China, has served over the years to bond all Lingnanians together, and continues to inspire the University today. The Lingnan spirit is marked by passion, loyalty, perseverance, openness to different ideas and cultures, and a readiness to serve.

programmeName: Master of Accountancy
summary: This course introduces students to the fundamental concepts, techniques and applications of cost and management accounting. Topics include cost concepts, cost behavior, costing systems, cost determination and allocation, standard costing and variance analysis, relevant costs, and budgetary planning and performance evaluation. Methods and techniques of cost management in the service sector and in the manufacturing environment will be discussed. Accounting implications for management planning, control, decision making and performance evaluation will be emphasized and developed. Throughout this course, students will be expected to actively participate in class discussions including case analysis.

Topics covered include the financial reporting environment in Hong Kong, accounting standards for major assets, liabilities, and equity, business combinations and consolidation of financial statements, and disclosure and reporting.

The study of the taxation system in China will be the major focus of the course with an extensive coverage of the laws, regulations, and the prevalent practices of the major types of taxes such as Value Added tax, Consumption Tax, Individual Income Tax, and the Enterprise Income Tax.
ACT611 Comparative Legal Systems The course introduces the salient features of the legal system of the PRC and compares it with other major jurisdictions which have representative legal regimes.
Major legal systems in the world include the Common law, Civil law, Islamic law ( Sharia ) and Scandinavian systems.
Students should leave this course with the skills necessary to translate accounting and business problems into actionable proposals that they can competently present to various stakeholders.

Specifically, topics covered include basic corporate governance framework, the stakeholder perspective of corporate strategy, corporate social responsibility, corporate environmental considerations, ESG management techniques, ESG disclosure, and relevant case studies.
learningObjectives: Professional Recognition

Throughout this course, students will be expected to actively participate in class discussions including case analysis.

ACT614 Financial Regulatory Environments of Hong Kong and Mainland China This course aims to provide students with perspectives of how the financial regulatory environments of Hong Kong and Mainland China function.
Students will acquire a solid foundation in the fundamentals of the Python programming language and explore its diverse applications in various business domains.

By engaging in hands-on exercises and analysing real-world examples, students will cultivate a profound understanding of business theory and practice, while concurrently honing their analytical acumen to critically assess and challenge existing paradigms in business-related fields.
Students will also examine popular cloud computing platforms, with a focus on Alibaba Cloud as the primary platform for hands-on learning.
Throughout the course, students will learn how to build and manage cloud systems, optimise performance, and enhance security.
Upon completion of these five courses with cumulative GPA of 2.67 or above, students will be granted a Master of Accountancy degree, subject to the satisfaction of University graduation requirements.

careerDirections: The offering is subject to sufficient demand and faculty availability.) ACT501 Auditing This course examines the theory and practice of financial auditing and selected professional issues in auditing.

Coverage includes the audit engagement framework and concepts from planning the engagement to audit reporting, professional ethics, the role and responsibilities of auditors, agency theory, frauds and internal control, auditing procedures for major accounts and transaction cycles, audit reporting and lags, audit market and auditor switches.

ACT502 Business and Company Law This course introduces the characteristics of the Hong Kong legal system, the general principles of contract law, and the essential knowledge of consumer law and employment law.
ACT506 Managerial Finance This course provides an introduction to corporate finance and, in particular, financial decision making by firms and individuals.

ACT606 Forensic Accounting and Fraud Examination The course will include broad coverage of an array of related topics, including the sociology of white-collar crime; legal, regulatory, professional, and ethical issues; types of fraud involving business organizations; fraud prevention techniques; and fraud examination techniques.

The framework consists of four key components: (1) business environment and strategy analysis (industry and competitive advantage analysis), (2) accounting analysis (evaluation of earnings quality), (3) financial analysis (evaluation of financial position and performance), and (4) prospective analysis (the forecasting of future payoffs).
It explains, in the context of the current crisis of trust in the accountancy profession, the ethical expectations that business and society have of accountants.
The course then examines the conflicts of interest that may occur in practice in the accounting profession.
It discusses the nature, objectives and limitations of professional codes of ethics, focusing on that of the HKICPA.
A deep understanding of such big and sophisticated global financial market will benefit students in planning and managing their careers.
graduateOutcomeSummary: A master’s degree in accountancy opens the doors to job and career opportunities in a number of fields. We’ll give you the tools to take control of your career, putting you on an upward trajectory and giving you a head start to become an accounting professional. Some graduates have also pursued their careers in civil servants, education sector as well as other sizable companies / organizations in the Mainland China, Hong Kong or overseas. Awardees of the PGDA are eligible to apply for direct entry into the Institute’s QP, provided they hold a recognized first degree.

Course Descriptions

(Not all elective courses will be offered in a given year. The offering is subject to sufficient demand and faculty availability.)

ACT501 Auditing
This course examines the theory and practice of financial auditing and selected professional issues in auditing. Coverage includes the audit engagement framework and concepts from planning the engagement to audit reporting, professional ethics, the role and responsibilities of auditors, agency theory, frauds and internal control, auditing procedures for major accounts and transaction cycles, audit reporting and lags, audit market and auditor switches.

ACT502 Business and Company Law
This course introduces the characteristics of the Hong Kong legal system, the general principles of contract law, and the essential knowledge of consumer law and employment law. It also discusses the characteristics of different types of business organisations, including sole proprietorships, partnerships and companies. Detailed elaboration will be provided on the incorporation procedure, requirements for raising funds by share capital and loan financing, legal considerations in operating a company, and winding up procedure of a business registered pursuant to the new Companies Ordinance (Cap 622).

ACT503 Cost and Management Accounting
This course introduces students to the fundamental concepts, techniques and applications of cost and management accounting. Topics include cost concepts, cost behavior, costing systems, cost determination and allocation, standard costing and variance analysis, relevant costs, and budgetary planning and performance evaluation. Methods and techniques of cost management in the service sector and in the manufacturing environment will be discussed. Accounting implications for management planning, control, decision making and performance evaluation will be emphasized and developed. Throughout this course, students will be expected to actively participate in class discussions including case analysis.

ACT504 Financial Accounting and Reporting
This course deals with various aspects of financial accounting and reporting. Topics covered include the financial reporting environment in Hong Kong, accounting standards for major assets, liabilities, and equity, business combinations and consolidation of financial statements, and disclosure and reporting.

ACT505 Hong Kong Taxation and Tax Planning
This course covers the basic laws and practice of the Hong Kong taxation system, including salaries tax, property tax, profits tax, personal assessment, depreciation allowances, stamp duty, and tax administration. Additionally, the course will introduce problem solving with practical examples. Local tax cases will be discussed.

ACT506 Managerial Finance
This course provides an introduction to corporate finance and, in particular, financial decision making by firms and individuals. Financial management has been defined as the acquisition, management and financing of resources for firms. The emphasis is on financial decision making in capitalist economies although some techniques and concepts are also relevant for state-owned enterprises in socialist-market economies. Issues addressed include asset valuation models, investment decision making, analysis of risk, financial planning, working capital management, the cost of capital, and financial markets.

ACT601 Accounting Theory
This course critically discuss various theories underlying the economics of financial accounting and reporting and how they impact the regulation process, the measurement practice, the disclosure practices and decision process.

ACT602 Research Project in Accounting
This course allows students to bring together knowledge and skills acquired during their accounting studies and apply them to solve a real problemrelated to accounting. This course requires extensive investigation and culminates in a quality report with relevant findings and sound recommendations.

ACT604 China Taxation: An International Perspective
China is now the world’s second largest economy after years of unprecedented economic growth. An in-depth knowledge about the taxation system in Mainland China is therefore essential for any business student. This course will start with a brief coverage of the regulatory framework of tax reporting in China and its implications to the capital market. The study of the taxation system in China will be the major focus of the course with an extensive coverage of the laws, regulations, and the prevalent practices of the major types of taxes such as Value Added tax, Consumption Tax, Individual Income Tax, and the Enterprise Income Tax. The differences between taxation systems in China and those in Hong Kong and elsewhere will be discussed with an appreciation of the opportunities and challenges faced by enterprises in planning their tax affairs and managing tax risks in the global business environment. The course will also cover the current developments in the international tax arena and how China is responding to these changes.

ACT605 Contemporary Issues in Management Accounting
This course examines a variety of advanced management accounting concepts, principles, and techniques for manufacturing and service organizations. The course is composed of (1) cost analysis and joint product costing; (2) current and emerging issues in management accounting; and (3) international issues and comprehensive case studies. The advanced topics may include management accounting information, quality cost management, performance evaluation, the balanced scorecard, management accounting control systems, customer profitability and environmental cost management. International management accounting issues will also be discussed. Comprehensive cases selected for intensive studies are relevant to the business environment in different jurisdictions.

ACT606 Forensic Accounting and Fraud Examination
The course will include broad coverage of an array of related topics, including the sociology of white-collar crime; legal, regulatory, professional, and ethical issues; types of fraud involving business organizations; fraud prevention techniques; and fraud examination techniques. This course assumes you have working knowledge of basic accounting principles, auditing concepts and techniques, and transaction processing cycles and internal controls.

ACT607 Financial Statement Interpretation and Analysis
This course focuses on providing a framework for using financial statements data in a variety of business analysis and valuation contexts. The framework consists of four key components: (1) business environment and strategy analysis (industry and competitive advantage analysis), (2) accounting analysis (evaluation of earnings quality), (3) financial analysis (evaluation of financial position and performance), and (4) prospective analysis (the forecasting of future payoffs). The course places primary emphasis on accounting analysis and financial analysis.

ACT608 The Economic Law of China
The course introduces to students the salient features of the PRC Economic Law, including the principles of agency, litigation, arbitration, sole traders, partnership, main types of foreign investment, companies, securities, bankruptcy, contract, and bills.

ACT609 Advanced Auditing with AI Applications
This course provides an in-depth analysis of auditing issues and principles, introducing students to advanced auditing topics (e.g., audit sampling, auditing in computerized environments) and other types of attestation and assurance services. Additionally, the course integrates AI and data analytics applications in auditing, including AI-driven risk assessment, predictive analytics, and automation in audit procedures. The course incorporates real-world auditing cases to expose students to the complexities of auditing practice, particularly in an AI-driven financial environment.

ACT610 Accounting Ethics and Corporate Governance
This course introduces the main principles and concepts of accounting ethics and corporate governance, and discusses how the two are linked. It explains, in the context of the current crisis of trust in the accountancy profession, the ethical expectations that business and society have of accountants. It introduces normative theories of ethics and ethical decision models to the students. The course then examines the conflicts of interest that may occur in practice in the accounting profession. It discusses the nature, objectives and limitations of professional codes of ethics, focusing on that of the HKICPA.

ACT611 Comparative Legal Systems
The course introduces the salient features of the legal system of the PRC and compares it with other major jurisdictions which have representative legal regimes. Major legal systems in the world include the Common law, Civil law, Islamic law ( Sharia ) and Scandinavian systems.

ACT612 Data Analytics for Accounting
In this digital age, data has proliferated and businesses big and small, are increasingly place a high value on data analytics. Accountants nowadays need to possess the analytic mindset and technical data skills that enable them to provide value-added services and make better decisions. This course is intended to provide students with an understanding of data analytic thinking and terminology as well as hands-on experience with data analytics tools and techniques. Students should leave this course with the skills necessary to translate accounting and business problems into actionable proposals that they can competently present to various stakeholders.

ACT613 Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) Issues in Business and Accounting
This is an introductory course on environmental & social responsibilities and their relations with corporate governance and corporate disclosure. It enables students to identify, analyze, and evaluate ESG issues as an essential factor in corporate reporting and decision-making process. Specifically, topics covered include basic corporate governance framework, the stakeholder perspective of corporate strategy, corporate social responsibility, corporate environmental considerations, ESG management techniques, ESG disclosure, and relevant case studies. The teaching & learning process incorporates the evolving nature of ESG for business and accounting, as well as up-to-date information on ESG practices, regulations, and management. Students are expected to apply subject knowledge in researching current ESG issues faced by accountants and other administrators in their working environment. They are also required to actively participate in class discussions and to share their experiences and learn from each other.

ACT614 Financial Regulatory Environments of Hong Kong and Mainland China
This course aims to provide students with perspectives of how the financial regulatory environments of Hong Kong and Mainland China function. Students can deepen the understanding of financial markets and institutions, international financial centres, money supply process, central banking, currency board system, regulations of banking system and financial markets. The course will cover contemporary development and reform in global financial market as well as fundamental financial reform, opening-up and financial systems integration and challenges in China. Current issues and future directions of Financial Regulatory Environments of Hong Kong and Mainland will also be explored in the course. A deep understanding of such big and sophisticated global financial market will benefit students in planning and managing their careers.

FIN545 Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) Investing and Sustainable Finance
ESG knowledge and skills are critical for developing ESG thinking, which is embedded in almost all investment settings nowadays for the promotion of sustainability in capital market. This course introduces (1) the background, current developments and trends of ESG investing, (2) the participants, institutions, and various financial instruments in ESG marketplace, and (3) the concepts, framework, techniques, data and toolkits related to using ESG considerations for identifying and informing sustainable investment solutions.

ESG 503 Environmental Social and Governance (ESG) Accounting and Disclosure
ESG accounting and disclosure is an emerging field of accounting that provides a framework for developing measures of corporate environmental and social performance, assessing their reliability, reporting to external stakeholders, and assisting managers in strategic and operational decisions that affect environmental costs and risks. This course provides a comprehensive overview of the theory and practice of ESG accounting and disclosure. Major topics include an introduction to the background of sustainability accounting, the framework and regulation of ESG reporting, the external assurance of ESG reporting, assessment of ESG performance, and accounting for key sustainability issues, such as climate and water. Comprehensive cases selected for intensive studies are based on real-world examples of public firms’ ESG reports.

ACT 615 Introduction to Python Programming
This elective course offers a comprehensive introduction to Python programming tailored specifically for business students. The primary objective of this course is to empower students with essential knowledge and skills to effectively apply Python programming within the realm of business practice. Students will acquire a solid foundation in the fundamentals of the Python programming language and explore its diverse applications in various business domains. By engaging in hands-on exercises and analysing real-world examples, students will cultivate a profound understanding of business theory and practice, while concurrently honing their analytical acumen to critically assess and challenge existing paradigms in business-related fields.

ORM 545 Cloud-based Solutions for Digital Business Transformation
This course offers a comprehensive introduction to cloud computing and services, covering foundational topics such as basic architecture, cloud service and deployment models, and cloud solutions for digital business transformation. Students will also examine popular cloud computing platforms, with a focus on Alibaba Cloud as the primary platform for hands-on learning. Throughout the course, students will learn how to build and manage cloud systems, optimise performance, and enhance security. In addition, this curriculum includes an exploration of big data and artificial intelligence, providing insights into data processing and AI tools. Designed for IT professionals and enthusiasts, the course employs real-world examples and practical exercises using Alibaba Cloud. This approach ensures that learners gain valuable skills and are well-prepared for future technological advancements.

Programme Structure

The full-time MAcc is a credit-based taught postgraduate programme and consists of total 33 credits or total 11 courses.
In the first semester, students complete six required courses. An intermediate award, Postgraduate Diploma in Accountancy (PGDA), will be granted to students who successfully complete these six required courses with cumulative GPA of 2.67 or above, subject to the general requirements of the University. Moreover, a student is required to achieve a cumulative GPA of 2.50 or above to proceed to the next term. Students who cannot meet such GPA requirement will normally be put on academic probation in the following term or discontinued.
In the second semester, students complete five courses or 15 credits, consisting of one required course and four elective courses. Upon completion of these five courses with cumulative GPA of 2.67 or above, students will be granted a Master of Accountancy degree, subject to the satisfaction of University graduation requirements.
A compulsory seminar or workshop on business communication is arranged to strengthen student’s communication skills. This seminar or workshop is usually conducted by a seasoned professional during the term break. Besides, a mandatory professional ethics programme is also arranged to inculcate students an understanding of the role that professional accountants play in business and society and the ethical standards expected of them. This programme is usually conducted after the final examination of term 2 study.

Pre-entry Courses
The pre-entry programme is designed to help applicants with non-business degrees to get ready for their formal admission to the MAcc programme. It is normally offered during the period from mid-July to end of August.
The pre-entry programme includes seven courses as listed below:
1. Accounting
2. Economics
3. Information Systems Management
4. Intermediate Accounting
5. Management
6. Marketing
7. Statistics
Each of these pre-entry courses has 15 class contact hours and is non-credit bearing with a pass/fail result. The number of pre-entry courses required to take by an applicant will depend on his/her undergraduate study. Students may also satisfy the pre-entry programme requirements by taking equivalent bachelor level courses in other recognized tertiary programmes.

Programme Curriculum
First Semester (6 core courses, or 18 credits)
ACT 501 Auditing
ACT 502 Business and Company Law
ACT 503 Cost and Management Accounting
ACT 504 Financial Accounting and Reporting
ACT 505 Hong Kong Taxation and Tax Planning
ACT 506 Managerial Finance
Students need to complete the above six courses (18 credits) in the first semester with a cumulative GPA of 2.67 or above to receive PGDA intermediate award. The PGDA awardees are eligible to enroll in Hong Kong Institute of Certified Public Accountants’ Qualification Programme. However, the final admission decision is subject to the approval of the HKICPA.

Second Semester (5 elective courses, or 15 credits)
Elective Courses (choose any five)*
ACT 601 Accounting Theory
ACT 602 Research Project in Accounting
ACT 604 China Taxation: An International Perspective
ACT 605 Contemporary Issues in Management Accounting
ACT 606 Forensic Accounting and Fraud Examination
ACT 607 Financial Statement Interpretation and Analysis
ACT 608 The Economic Law of China
ACT 609 Advanced Auditing with AI Applications
ACT 610 Accounting Ethics and Corporate Governance
ACT 611 Comparative Legal Systems
ACT 612 Data Analytics for Accounting
ACT 613 Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) Issues in Business and Accounting
ACT 614 Financial Regulatory Environments of Hong Kong and Mainland China
FIN 545 Environmental Social and Governance (ESG) Investing and Sustainable Finance
ESG 503 Environmental Social and Governance (ESG) Accounting and Disclosure
ACT 615 Introduction to Python Programming
ORM 545 Cloud-based Solutions for Digital Business Transformation

* The offering of elective courses is subject to students' demand and faculty availability.

Admission Requirements

I: General Requirement
An applicant should hold a bachelor's degree awarded by a recognized tertiary institution or have equivalent qualifications, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

II: English Language Proficiency Requirement
An applicant whose degree is not granted by a tertiary institution in Hong Kong or an English-speaking country should pass ONE of the tests below to meet the English language proficiency requirement:
• a minimum score of 79 in TOEFL Internet-Based Test; or
• a band score of 6.0 or above in IELTS; or
• a minimum score of 450 or above in College English Test Band 6 (CET-6), with proof of competency in spoken English
other equivalent English qualifications to be considered on a case-by-case basis.

For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are conducted at a test centre and within the validity period, viz. 2 years , from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort.

For CET-6 results submitted for admission purposes, the University accepts only those obtained within the five-year validity period , counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort. Applicants must also demonstrate competency in spoken English through an oral interview conducted by the Programme and/or obtain grade B or above in the CET-6 Speaking English Test.

III: Two Recommendation Forms
Confidential Recommendation Form

IV: Consent Form for Verifying Academic Awards
Consent Form

V: CQV Report if your degree is awarded by a Mainland university (only applicable to graduates)

NOTES：
Student Visa
The application requirements are the same for both local and non-local applicants. Non-local students studying in Hong Kong are required to apply for student visas or entry permit from the Hong Kong Immigration Department. Details of the entry arrangement are set out in Immigration Department’s website: http://www.immd.gov.hk/eng/services/visas/study.html . Once admitted, the MAcc Programme Office will help the students with the visa application.
Pre-entry Programme
Applicants with a recognized bachelor’s degree but not in business-related disciplines will be required to pass a pre-entry programme prior to their formal admission to the MAcc Programme. The number of pre-entry courses required to take by an applicant will be based on his/her undergraduate study.
Admission Preference
Applicants will enjoy admission preference if they have relevant working experiences or have a professional qualification in the areas of accounting, auditing, taxation, and finance.
Interview
Applicants may be required to attend an interview (via online) conducted by the Director of Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office and one more faculty member.
Qualification Assessment Report
Applicants with degrees awarded by overseas universities through distance learning programmes or the completion of a curriculum of short duration or uncertain qualification may be required to provide an assessment report from the Hong Kong Council for Academic Accreditation and Vocational Qualifications (HKCAAVQ) on the level of qualification obtained.

Committee-Related Information

Committees of PGDA / MAcc

Postgraduate Diploma in Accountancy (PGDA) / Master of Accountancy (MAcc)
Programme and Curriculum Committee
Terms of reference
At the programme level, a Programme and Curriculum Committee (PCC) is set up for a taught postgraduate programme. The major responsibilities are:
a) To take charge of the degree programme including the design, implementation and review of the curricula, inter alia, the achievement of the intended programme learning outcomes.;
b) To liaise with all Departments/Units/Divisions concerned on programme matters, such as course offerings for each academic year.;
c) To liaise with Registry/School of Graduate Studies on all academic matters, including review of academic regulations and examination matters;
d) To review admissions policy for the degree programme;
e) To deal with all other matters related to the degree programme.

Membership
Chairman:
Members:

Secretary:

Programme Director
Head of Department of Accountancy
All teaching staff of the programme
At least one student representative
To be appointed by the Chairperson

Postgraduate Diploma in Accountancy (PGDA) / Master of Accountancy (MAcc)
Board of Examiners
Terms of reference
A Board of Examiners is set up for a taught postgraduate programme to oversee examination matters. The major duties are:
a) To maintain the academic standards of the programme at a level appropriate to the type of award.;
b) To approve examination question papers after considering the views and recommendations of External Academic Advisers, where appropriate;
c) To maintain general supervision of the system of continuous assessment, where it applies..;
d) To maintain the proper conduct of examinations;
e) To determine students' examination results;
f) To assess the progress of students and to report its decisions, including its recommendations for award classification, to the Postgraduate Studies Committee; and
g) To consider appeals referred to it by the Registrar.

Members:

Postgraduate Diploma in Accountancy (PGDA) / Master of Accountancy (MAcc)
Staff-Student Consultation Committee
Terms of reference
The Staff-Student Consultation Committee provides a platform for students to express their views on the programme and individual courses, and for the departments/academic units to solicit student feedback on specific aspects of the programme and courses for continuous improvement. The major responsibilities are:
a) To promote understanding between students and teaching staff.
b) To consider feedback from students regarding teaching, learning and course evaluation and issues of importance for enhancing teaching and learning quality.
c) To consider any other matters of concern to students.
d) To review and monitor the learning and teaching quality assurance mechanisms and processes within the programme.
e) To provide feedback to students on changes made to courses and teaching in response to the Course Teaching and Learning Evaluation exercise.
f) To formulate strategies in helping students to learn more effectively.
Chairman:
Members:

Secretary:

Programmes Director
At least two staff members teaching for the programme in the current academic year
At least three student representatives

To be appointed by the Chairman

Postgraduate Diploma in Accountancy (PGDA) / Master of Accountancy (MAcc)
Advisory Board
Terms of reference
An Advisory Board shall normally meet once a year to help plan and keep under review the following aspects of work including future developments:
a) level and length of courses/programme(s) concerned in relation to local and regional needs;
b) the relevance of the courses/programme(s) in relation to the local and regional needs;
c) the prospects of local and regional employment for graduates;
d) the adequacy of the equipment and other resources of the academic unit(s) to fulfil local and regional needs;
e) the development of teaching and other activities carried out jointly by the academic unit(s) and the appropriate sector of the community;
f) investigation consultancy and other services given by the academic unit(s); and
g) keep under review the assistance which the community can give to further the objectives of the University in the subject concerned by way of practical training facilities, the provision of part-time teaching staff and equipment, the award of scholarships and student-fellowships, etc..

Objectives and Mission

The MAcc programme is designed primarily for individuals who are or intend to be, working in the field of accountancy or a related field. The programme equips its graduates with an up-to-date, broad knowledge of most recent developments in accounting theory and practice from global and local perspective.

The programme enables students to:
demonstrate extensive, in-depth and current knowledge of accounting theory and practice in the international and local accountancy fields;

develop and enhance their analytical skills to be able to critically analyse and question existing knowledge and practice in accounting-related fields;

integrate and apply their knowledge of accountancy in problem solving and decision making in an ever-changing business environment;

have good communication skills;

be able to identify and demonstrate effective judgment of ethical issues in accounting practices.

Professional Recognition

HKICPA
The first half of our MAcc curriculum (i.e. the six required PGDA level courses in the first semester) has been accredited as an approved Conversion Programme under the Qualification Programme (QP) of the Hong Kong Institute of Certified Public Accountants (HKICPA). Awardees of the PGDA are eligible to apply for direct entry into the Institute’s QP, provided they hold a recognized first degree. Passing the QP is a pre-requisite for becoming a qualified accountant, CPA, in Hong Kong. Mutual examination paper exemption has also been arranged between the HKICPA and the Chinese Institute of Certified Public Accountants (CICPA). Graduates of MAcc Programme are eligible for exemption of up to ten Associate Level Modules of Qualification Programme (QP), subject to the final decision of the HKICPA.

ACCA
Certain subjects within our MAcc programme are entitled to corresponding exemptions from respective ACCA examination papers. You are advised to visit ACCA's website to find out further details about its exemption policy and qualification requirements.

ICMA
The Institute of Certified Management Accountants (ICMA, also known as CMA Australia) was officially incorporated in 1996 in Australia. MAcc graduates are exempted from the first 16 units of the ICMA professional examination and may apply for the Graduate Management Accountant (GMA) designation upon graduation. PGDA graduates are also exempted from taking 15 units of the ICMA examination.

CIMA
MAcc graduates are eligible for exemptions from the CIMA examinations at the Certificate Level and Operational Level, allowing them to start directly at the Management Level for the CIMA exam.

TIHK
MAcc graduates are eligible for exemptions from two papers of The Taxation Institute of Hong Kong’s Chartered Tax Adviser (CTA) Qualifying Examination.

AACSB Accredited Programme
AACSB International grants accreditation to business degree programmes worldwide. Found in 1916, AACSB is the longest serving global accrediting body for business schools that offer undergraduate, master and doctoral degrees in business. AACSB Accreditation is the hallmark of excellence in business education and has been earned by less than six percent of the world's business programmes. There are nearly 900 accredited business schools worldwide.
Lingnan University's Faculty of Business obtained its AACSB accreditation in 2011 and has maintained such status since then. MAcc programme, operating within the Faculty of Business, is an AACSB accredited programme.

Application Method

1. Application Period
The application period for applying for MAcc 2026-27 is 1 October 2025 to 30 June 2026 .
Admissions will be closed once the quota is reached.

2. Application Fee
A non-refundable and non-transferable application fee of HK$400 is charged for each application.

3. Application Procedures
Applicants can apply online.
For online application, please access the University’s online application system via the Internet at https://www.ln.edu.hk/admission/apply/ .

Supporting Documents
Applicants should scan and submit the following documents to the Online Application System for evaluation of their applications:
Identity documents (e.g. Identity card*, passport or other travel document)

Official Transcript (for academic qualifications of undergraduate and/or postgraduate studies):

An official final transcript for a completed programme

The latest transcript if the programme is not yet completed

An official English-translated copy must be attached if the transcript is not in English. The interpretation of grades (in English) is also required.

Degree Certificate/Graduation Certificate/Proof of Study (as appropriate)

Verification proof by third-party authorities (for a completed programme):

Online Verification Report of Higher Education Degree Certificate (中國高等教育學位在綫驗証報告), both Chinese and English versions, issued by the CHSI (中國高等教育學生信息網) for qualification(s) awarded by universities in Mainland China

Online Query of Overseas Credentials Evaluation Reports國(境)外學歷學位認証書 issued by the Chinese Service Center for Scholarly Exchange (CSCSE) (教育部留學服務中心) (applicable to Mainland applicants with qualifications awarded by overseas universities)

Proof of English language proficiency, e.g. IETLS, TOEFL Score Report (applicable to applicants whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent, is not from a tertiary institution where English is used as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the degree programme)

Personal statement

Reference letters (if any)

*Do not upload copy of HKID. Local students will be required to present HKID for verification upon registration.

No Dishonest and Bribery Practices
Any false or misleading information and/or document(s) provided in the application, or any dishonest conduct observed during the application process, e.g. plagiarism, employing surrogate examination-taker, etc., will result in disqualification of application for admission, revocation of admission offer or termination of studies at the University if admitted, and any fees paid will be forfeited.

Falsification of documents or the use of falsified documents is a very serious offence and is absolutely not tolerated by the University. According to the laws of Hong Kong, forgery is a serious criminal offence that a convicted offender may be liable to imprisonment for a maximum of 14 years.

Applicants or their family members and agents must not offer advantages to any staff or members of the University in relation to their admission applications or while having dealings of any kind with the University. These offers may constitute an offence under the Prevention of Bribery Ordinance (Cap.201) in Hong Kong. The University also prohibits its staff and members from soliciting or accepting such advantages.

4. Selection
All applicants for admission to the programme will be considered on the basis of academic and professional qualifications, work experience, suitability, and potential. Applicants may be required to attend an interview (via online) conducted by the Director of Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office and one more faculty member.
Applications are processed on a rolling basis until all places are filled. Early applications are strongly encouraged.

FAQs

Programme and Study

Q1. What is the duration of the Programme?

The normal duration for the Programme is one year full-time and the maximum period of study is three years. Teaching normally begins in late August and ends in May. Students who are required to attend the pre-entry course(s) will normally take the course(s) from late July– prior to the beginning of the new academic year.

Q2. When is the application period for next academic year?

The application generally begins on 1 October and closes on 30 June of the following year. Late application may be considered if spaces are still available.

Q3. What is the tuition fees per year?

The tuition fees for the academic year 2026-27 is HK$229,000 for local student and HK$315,000 for non-local student.

Q4. What is the commencement date of the MAcc classes?

Usually the commencement date of the MAcc classes is late August to early September.

Q5. What is the time and venue of classes?

The MAcc classes will normally be held on weekdays or Saturdays at Lingnan campus. For the pre-entry courses (which will normally be offered from mid-July to late August), class meeting times and venues will depend on student enrollment. A formal announcement will be made prior to the commencement of the courses.

Q6. What is the language of instruction for the courses?

English (unless otherwise indicated in the course descriptions).

Q7. What is the course structure?

The full-time MAcc is a credit-based programme. All courses in the programme structure are designed on the basis of three-credit units. The normal duration for the full-time MAcc programme is one year and the maximum period of study will be three years from the date of first registration.

Students are required to complete six required courses (congruent to the Postgraduate Diploma in Accountancy curriculum) in the first semester.

Upon successful completion of the 18 credits in the first semester, students are eligible to proceed to the second semester. Students are required to complete the five elective courses (15 credits) in the second semester. Upon completion of these five courses and achieve a cumulative GPA of 2.67 or above, students are granted Master of Accountancy degrees, subject to the satisfaction of University graduation requirements.

Application for Admission

Q8. What is the admission requirements for the MAcc programme?

To be eligible for entry into Master of Accountancy, applicants should hold a bachelor’s degree in business or equivalent from a recognized tertiary institution; or have other qualifications and experience, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis. Applicants with a bachelor’s degree in a non-business discipline from a recognized tertiary institution have to complete and pass a pre-entry programme prior to their formal admission to the MAcc programme. The number of pre-entry courses required to take by an applicant will depend on his/her undergraduate study. Applicants will enjoy admission preference if they have related working experiences or have professional qualification in accounting, auditing, taxation or finance.

Q9. What is the language requirement?

As teaching will be normally conducted in English, an applicant whose undergraduate degree is not from a tertiary institution in Hong Kong or an English-speaking country should meet the English Language Proficiency Requirement below:

- IELTS: minimum acceptable score of 6.0 or

- TOEFL: minimum acceptable score of 79 (internet-based test) or

- A minimum score of 450 or above in College English Test Band 6 (CET-6), with proof of competency in spoken English

other equivalent English qualifications to be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Applicants who have graduated from a university using English as a main teaching language do not normally need to present evidence of their language proficiency.

Q10. Can I update my TOEFL / IELTS and additional documents after my online submission?

Yes, after completion of your online submission and payment, you still can submit any additional supporting materials by email to: [email protected] and send the original copy of recommendation letters by post on or before the application deadline.

Q11. How to submit the application?

Applications to MAcc programme should be submitted via the University's Online Application System here .

Q12. Will I be required to attend an interview?

The selection process will begin as soon as we receive your completed application materials and confidential recommendation forms. Applicants may be required to attend an interview (via online) conducted by the Director of Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office and one more faculty member.

Others

Q13. I cannot process the online submission or e-Payment and the system has rejected my submission, why?

If you encounter a firewall issue, you may need to use another computer or a different network to try again. Alternatively, you might have selected the wrong "document type" when uploading your ID card/passport or transcript/certificate online. In that case, you should remove the files and re-upload them, ensuring you choose the correct document type.

For any questions, please email [email protected] .

Q14. How can I apply for the programme scholarship?

No separate scholarship applications are required. Students will be considered automatically for various types of the scholarships based on their academic merits and services.

Q15. Can I apply for accommodation in the University?

On-campus hostel places are not available for Self-funded taught postgraduate students.

Q16. How can I contact the MAcc Programme Office?

MAcc Programme Office

Lingnan University
Tuen Mun, Hong Kong

programmeName: Master of Science in Arts Technology and Business
summary: • The first interdisciplinary master's programme in Hong Kong that is taught in Chinese and focuses on both arts technology and business.
learningObjectives: • To develop students' understanding of business theories and concepts and their appreciation of art and technology innovations and their latest developments;
• To enable students to critically analyse opportunities and challenges in the arts and technology sector, develop problem-solving skills, and become effective arts managers and arts administrators;

• To provide students with the opportunity to be exposed to the broad spectrum of the arts community and to develop practical communication skills with people from all walks of life in the arts, technology and related fields;
• To nurture a new generation an ethical arts and business leaders who will contribute to a better and sustainable future for society.
• It aims to develop the next generation of arts managers and arts business leaders in the cultural and creative industries.
• Students will be able to develop a good understanding of best business practices and develop a deep appreciation of arts technology and business in this dynamic industry.
coreCourses: History of Chinese and Western Art
Accounting and Finance for Arts Industry
The Interaction of Arts and Sciences
Law in Arts and Intellectual Property
Strategic Innovation and Entrepreneurship for Arts Industry
Innovation and New Product Development for Arts and Creative Industry
Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) for Entrepreneurship

courseDescriptions.description: 本选修课程为商科学生提供全面的 Python 编程入门课程。本课程的主要目标是使学生掌握必要的知识和技能，以便在商业实践领域有效地应用 Python 编程。学生将打下坚实的 Python 编程语言基础，并探索其在各个商业领域的多种应用。通过进行实践练习和分析现实世界的例子，学生将对商业理论和实践有深刻的理解，同时磨练他们的分析敏锐度，以批判性地评估和挑战商业相关领域的现有范式。

courseDescriptions.courseName: ORM545 数字化业务转型的云解决方案
courseDescriptions.description: 本课程全面介绍云计算和服务，涵盖基础架构、云服务和部署模型以及面向数字化业务转型的云解决方案等基础主题。学生还将学习热门云计算平台，并重点关注阿里云作为实践学习的主要平台。在整个课程中，学生将学习如何构建和管理云系统、优化性能并增强安全性。此外，本课程还涵盖大数据和人工智能的探索，深入讲解数据处理和人工智能工具。本课程专为IT专业人士和爱好者设计，采用阿里云的真实案例和实践练习。这种教学方法确保学习者获得宝贵的技能，并为未来的技术进步做好充分准备。

Master of Science in Business Innovation and Entrepreneurship

Application Period:

1 Oct 2025 - 30 Jun 2026

---

Source: https://www.ln.edu.hk/fb/mscbie
Master of Science in Business Innovation and Entrepreneurship (MScBIE)

创新和创业是经济增长的主要动力，经济社会的可持续发展依赖于创新想法和企业家精神。近年来，政府非常重视对企业家和初创企业的支持，提出了大众创业, 万众创新的理念。特别是随着大湾区和一带一路的快速发展，更需创新与创业来助力建设创新发展，提高区域整体的国际竞争力。
为了满足创业和创新管理知识的需求，MScBIE商业创新与创业理学硕士应运而生。该课程由岭南大学商学院开办，是一年全日制硕士课程。课程旨在为学生提供创新和创业管理方面的全面知识，并为学生提供机会在Capstone项目中整合和应用所学知识。
作为亚洲首屈一指的博雅大学，提供优质教育是岭大的主要任务和核心价值之一。 岭南大学在最新公布的 「 2025年度泰晤士高等教育世界大学影响力排名 」 中，于联合国可持续发展目标 「 SDG4：优质教育 」 的排名跃升至全球第一，是首次有香港地区的高等院校于该项排名高踞全球榜首。同时，商学院已于2011年取得国际商管学院促进协会（AACSB）认证。

课程宣传页

岭南大学网上申请系统

对于新学年录取（即2026年9月入学），申请于2025年10月1日开始。本课程的录取将依申请的先后次序进行，有意申请者请及早提交申请表，额满即停止招生。

Programme and Curriculum Committee

Terms of Reference
At the programme level, a Programme Curriculum Committee (PCC) is set up for a taught postgraduate programme. The major responsibilities are:
To take charge of the degree programme, including the design, implementation and review of the curriculum, inter alia, the achievement of the intended student learning outcomes.
To liaise with all Departments/Units/Divisions concerned on programme matters, such as course offerings for each academic year.
To liaise with Registry/School of Graduate Studies (GS) on all academic matters, including review of academic regulations and examination matters.
To review the admissions policy for the degree programme.
To deal with all other matters related to the degree programme.

Faculty of Business

Master of Science in Business Innovation and Entrepreneurship (MScBIE)

课程特色

课程特色

全港首创同时涵盖创新和创业的课程

香港第一个同时针对商业创新和创业的硕士课程。涵盖创业所需的不同范畴的知识。帮助学生了解有助于创业创新及可持续性发展所应学习的概念和理论、掌握创新创业所需要的社会、法律和财务等各方面的知识和技能，同时也激发学生批判性地思考分析与创业创新相关的问题。创新和创业是协同并进的，我们注重把这两个概念结合起来，是本港首个同时涵盖创新和创业管理两方面内容的课程，填补了这一领域的空白。本课程是综合性和全方位的，是学生同时学习创新和创业知识独一无二的课程选择。课程由商学院开办， 涵盖多个学科的内容，让学生掌握多种商业知识并且有机会参与各类讲座及研讨会，了解行业最新发展。

Capstone项目

本课程设有Capstone 项目，为学生提供理论联系实际运用所学知识和技能的机会，帮助学生透过此项目实践如何为新企业制定发展计划，使学生掌握项目管理和谈判的技巧，了解如何拓展和维护客户关系的技能，这些都是企业家所需要的核心能力。在项目督导的指导下，学生会学到企业家各种实用的管理技能。
BIE 506 Capstone Project 之所以如此受欢迎和受重视，主要是其课程设置具有极强的实战性,解决的都是现实社会中存在的实际问题。

企业导师讲座+实地参访+实战演练的立体培养模式：
该课程的教学形式将基于各个项目导师的密切指导，并辅以一系列与项目相关的讲座和研讨会（如创新发展讲座，初创计划拓展讲座，分析技能，演讲技巧，咨询和项目管理技能等），课程中使用大量的案例研究，为学生提供各方面的实践培训，使学生能够在商业环境中培养创新思维，进行批判性思考。学生们将他们所学到的知识应用到实际的商业环境中，这有助于他们将创业理念转化成一个成功的初创企业。在项目督导的指导下，学生将以5-7人为一个团队，制定一个切实可行的创业计划。
通过项目导师的指导，学生将进行实地研究，为项目决策提供建议。重点强调业务创新和创业理论的“现实商业环境”应用进行管理分析，并根据项目的研究和分析结果向客户提供建议。课程的一个关键特征是学生将会在完成项目的过程中进行学习。 完成本课程后，学生将能够：
1.确定与业务问题相关的关键因素和问题 2.制定切实可行的商业计划和时间表来完成项目,以达至初创企业计划的拓展。 3.进行尖端项目，管理项目工作流程和客户关系： 4.使用项目管理和分析工具解决初创计划和项目发展中的关键问题 5.提高人际沟通技巧，并以简洁，易于接受和有说服力的方式呈现市场研究结果和创业思路。

（Capstone Project课程链接资讯）BIE AY24-25 课程收官：构筑商业创新的追光时代

普通话教学

学生背景多元化，可跨专业申请。授课语言，作业及考试均以普通话进行。 采用普通话授课可以让学生更好地了解中国市场，了解在大中华地区有关商业创新和创业的政策、法规和发展方向，并在有需要的情况下提供一些翻译材料以拓展相关领域的国际视野。用普通话教学也促使学生在学习中更好的建立自己的关系网。此外，使用普通话也方便邀请内地的专家、企业家和政府官员来举办研讨会，分享他们的经验，以及安排学生到内地参观、考察和交流。

自费游学活动

除了正常授课外，岭南大学商学院也给同学提供自费参加游学的机会。学生有机会参加海内外知名高校的学习交流活动，参观当地著名企业，与企业家和教授进行面对面交流。在此过程中，同学可以挖掘更多创新元素，了解更多创新创业模式，拓宽自己的国际视野。完成课程后，会获得学校颁发的结业证书。

（创新创业与设计思维工作坊）MScBIE新加坡游学之旅：点亮学术与视野的灯塔

---

课程结构

学生需于 1 学年（两学期）内完成 10 门课程，包括7门必修课和任意3门选修课，每门课程均为 3 学分。
AY2025/26 必修课程
BIE501 创新及创业战略（3 学分）
BIE502 创新与新产品开发（3 学分）
BIE503 全球企业家精神（3 学分）
BIE504 风险投资与私募股权（3 学分）
BIE505 创业法律与知识产权（3 学分）
BIE506 Capstone 项目（3 学分）
BIE507 大数据营销 （3 学分）

AY2025/26 选修课程
BIE602 创业意识和行动（3 学分）
BIE603 电子商务模式和初创企业（3 学分）
BIE604 企业家会计（3学分）
BIE605 创业的环境，社会及公司治理（3 学分）
ACT615 Python程式设计入门 （3 学分）
ORM545 数字化业务转型的云解决方案（3学分）

学费及奖学金

申请条件

申请条件
1.一般学历要求
申请者需持有认可大学的学士学位
2. 英语能力要求
除非持有香港或其他英语国家大学颁发的学位，其他申请人须符合以下要求:
• 雅思考试 (IELTS) 最低成绩达5.5 分；或
• 托福考试 (TOEFL) 最低成绩达 46 分 (网考)，家考托福将不予接受；或
• 全国大学英语六级考试（CET6）最低成绩达 430 分。
• 持有同等资格者，本课程会作个别考虑。
注意：
• 申请者所毕业之大学如以英语为授课语言，则无需证明英文能力。
• 学生入读后，需要完成大学提供的英语课程及活动以满足毕业要求及提升英语能力和国际化视野。
• 非商科背景的同学需要读预科课程，Economics和/或Marketing

课程描述

学习目标与成果

目标
课程为想在中国特别是大湾区发展自己的商业的人士所设立。
-让学生了解创业的技术、社会、法律及财务等方面的知识，并具备运用这些知识的能力;
-让学生了解创新和可持续发展的概念和理论，并批判性地分析与创新发展有关的问题;
-帮助学生制定创业计划;
-通过完成课程的capstone模块，为学生提供应用知识和技能的机会。

学习成果
理解创新和创业管理的概念以及创新的发展，特别是在中国的发展，完成课程后，学生应能:
-了解与创业组织管理和创新发展有关的理论和概念;
-应用所获得的知识，分析业务和管理中的问题及其对企业的影响，评价可能的解决办法和结果;
-撰写一份全面的商业计划书，具有足够的团队合作能力;
-识别、开发和评估香港本地和区域市场，特别是大湾区的业务和创新机会;
-评估创业的环境、可持续发展性、治理和道德责任。

Master of Science in Business Innovation and Entrepreneurship (MScBIE)

课程概览

课程描述

课程概览

学习目标与成果
课程结构
课程描述

课程描述

BIE 501 创新与创业战略

本课程旨在使研究生熟悉当代创业和创新战略的基本概念。学生需要整合并应用他们之前学到的知识来制定一个创新或新企业的战略规划。本课程还为研究生提供了理解、诊断和实施组织变革和创新的原理。其中包括审查企业家领导力在创造适合实施商业创新和变革战略的商业氛围担任的战略角色。

BIE 502 创新与新产品开发

本课程从管理学的角度说明学生深入了解企业的创新和新产品开发课题，阐述企业如何通过在新市场和现有市场的创新来创造价值和实现增长。学生将掌握创新和新产品开发过程中出现的概念、方法和工具，了解如何更好地控制成本和风险，检讨开发新产品的过程中公司面临的许多管理问题。本课程的教学将使用大量的案例研究，以帮助学生更好地了解创新和新产品开发过程中的成功经验与失败教训，最终提高创新成功的机会。

BIE 503 全球企业家精神

全球企业家精神是全球领导者的一种特质或特征。全球企业家是利用他们对全球的理解和联系来识别跨国和跨文化机会，并将其转化为新的价值创造倡议的专业人士。这个学分的课程帮助学生了解全球企业家如何发展和使用他们的技能和能力，以确定机会，让他们在全球舞台上竞争。它涵盖了实施全球创业战略和管理全球新企业的核心概念。这与当今许多怀有国际抱负的新企业有关，也与许多现有企业需要发展新企业以走向海外或迎接外国挑战的迫切需求有关，在这方面，本课程对于想要获得创业知识和技能的学生很有用。这些创业知识和技能可应用于全球性的企业，致力于国际化发展的中小企，或者试图利用这些知识和技能以应对国际上的新挑战的大公司。

BIE 504 风险投资与私募股权

本课程重点关注中小型和成长型公司的风险投资和私募股权投资的相关实用知识。本课程适合那些考虑从事风险投资和私募股权行业、商业和投资银行业的学生，以及那些期望创业和成为企业家的学生修读。本课程探讨的题目包括：创业公司融资，业务评估和估值，交易和谈判策略，以及投资退出策略等问题。并且本课程将涵盖一系列行业和相应的融资结构。

BIE 505 创业法律与知识产权

本课程向学生介绍创业法律和知识产权方面的基本概念，法律原则和规则等。内容包括现代商业类型 ( 例如独资企业、合伙企业和公司 ) 的各自特征及成立程序，企业的经营管理制度。资本的筹集和维护，董事和经理的职责，以及创业知识产权方面的法律和法规。在整个课程中，学生需要积极参与课堂讨论，特别是参与案例分析。

BIE 506 Capstone项目

本课程为所有学生提供与公司合作的尖端项目作为实践，引导学生对于复杂的商业环境能够有创新性和战略性的思维。学生将把他们学到的知识应用到现实商业环境中，又能启发和协助他们开展成功的初创计划。本课程为参与项目管理，咨询和客户沟通技能做好准备。在他们指定的项目导师的监督下，学生将以团队形式（ 6-7 人）进行管理研究和分析，以满足客户或公司的商业计划和业务需求、并为管理层提供决策的深入分析。

BIE 507 大数据营销

在大资料时代，市场分析在商业决策制定中越来越重要。大资料行销分析说明企业更好地了解他们的客户和竞争对手，提高行销决策的品质。本课程向学生介绍最先进的大资料和行销分析方法，示范如何将这些分析技能实际应用于业务决策，并提供营销资料所需的技巧，以制定有关营销策略的建议。这些技巧是通过讲课、作业、课堂练习以及小组真实资料项目。

BIE 602 创业意识和行动

本课程向学生介绍创业理论及其实践，强调应对日新月异变化外部环境的意识和态度。本课程侧重于探索创业机会的不同阶段，包括创业机会识别，可行性探索，商业模式创新等等。教学方法上将理论学习、现实案例和讲座嘉宾相结合，学生将围绕创业的成功，机遇和风险进行讨论与学习。学生还将发展创业实践技能，建立自己的创业计划，能够整合创业概念并与有经验的创业者进行探讨。

BIE 603 电子商务模式和初创企业

大数据兴起，全球各地普遍存在数据化的趋势，使电子商务成为21世纪其中一个财富和经济增长的核心驱动力。越来越多大学生在毕业后冒险地开创自己的企业，或参与扩大雇主的产品线，这些挑战让学生熟知相关的创业和创新理念，本课程旨在 帮助学生为这些挑战做好准备。课程将调查和分析电子商务模式和初创企业的案例，讨论其基本创新和相关策略。学生学习引发商业创意、制定商业计划以及向潜在投资者介绍的过程。

BIE 604 企业家会计

本课程向学生介绍企业创业者必须具备的至关重要的财务和管理会计的基本概念，会计技巧和应用。该课程的重点不在于会计的技术性，而在于更多地关注会计概念和工具的应用，以加强商业决策能力。课程主题包括会计原则和标准，基本会计等式和会计周期，四个基本财务报告，成本条款和概念，本量利分析，相关成本和决策，预算规划，税收策略和绩效评估。整个课程也会强调创业公司在会计方面面临的挑战和问题。学生应该积极参与课堂讨论和案例分析，并将学到的学科知识应用于团队项目的解决方案。

BIE 605 创业的环境，社会及公司治理

本课程是对创业的环境、社会责任及其同公司治理关系的概论。课程帮助学生发现、分析、并且评估企业决策过程中同 ESG 相关的核心问题。涵盖的具体课题包括公司治理基本框架，从利益相关者视角理解公司战略，企业社会责任 (CSR) ，企业对环境的考量， ESG 管理方式，及相关案例剖析。教学过程中因应 ESG 在创业中的不断发展，融入关于 ESG 实务、规则和管理的最新信息。学生应能够运用本课程的知识对管理层面临的实际 ESG 问题进行分析研讨。学生还应积极参与课堂讨论，并且分享经验、相互学习。

BIE 606 沟通与谈判

该研究生课程考察了各种各样的方法，用于分析和实践谈判以及创业者的相关问题。在不断变化的全球业务中，特别是对于创业者而言，谈判对于各自初创企业的 成功和生存至关重要，也是创业者日常生活中不可避免的因素。创业精神本质上是一种谈判科学。一个创业者的成功与否取决于他或她能够成功谈判商业交易。 谈判的艺术已经发展成为一门科学和一门学科领域。本课程探讨了许多创业背景下的谈判：比如简单的个人交易，合同谈判，交易，与同事和联合创始人的谈判，与 投资者和供货商的 谈判，解决初创企业组织内部和组织之间的利益冲突。作为战略 和战术层面的沟通技巧，对于成功的谈判至关重要，本课程还教导学生如何认识各 利益相关方的沟通期望，以及如何有效利用基本的沟通策略和技术来改善总体结 果。本课程还将探讨谈判中的道德问题。

ACT615 Python程式设计入门

本选修课程为商科学生提供全面的 Python 编程入门课程。本课程的主要目标是使学生掌握必要的知识和技能，以便在商业实践领域有效地应用 Python 编程。学生将打下坚实的 Python 编程语言基础，并探索其在各个商业领域的多种应用。通过进行实践练习和分析现实世界的例子，学生将对商业理论和实践有深刻的理解，同时磨练他们的分析敏锐度，以批判性地评估和挑战商业相关领域的现有范式。

ORM545 数字化业务转型的云解决方案

本课程全面介绍云计算和服务，涵盖基础架构、云服务和部署模型以及面向数字化业务转型的云解决方案等基础主题。学生还将学习热门云计算平台，并重点关注阿里云作为实践学习的主要平台。在整个课程中，学生将学习如何构建和管理云系统、优化性能并增强安全性。此外，本课程还涵盖大数据和人工智能的探索，深入讲解数据处理和人工智能工具。本课程专为IT专业人士和爱好者设计，采用阿里云的真实案例和实践练习。这种教学方法确保学习者获得宝贵的技能，并为未来的技术进步做好充分准备。

learningObjectives: Strengthen students’ abilities to understand and use the information technologies that are necessary for supply chain operations in an eBusiness environment;
Equip students with the management strategies for effectively using information technologies to achieve supply chain integration in the international and global context;
Familiarize students with managing online and mobile business operations that are involved in a supply chain;
Expose to students the benefits for an organization through incorporating the Internet into its supply chain operations;
Foster students’ abilities in identifying the business changes resulting from the use of information technologies for organizational practices in supply chains.

This programme focuses on the improvement of students’ management strategies and skills in supply chain operations in the eBusiness context, in addition to the education of information technologies. Accordingly, all teaching staffs in the programme will utilize a number of practical cases and managerial discussions in lecturing their courses. To help students understand the SCM-related issues in eBusiness, we will also encourage students to discuss important cases in class, and have interactive discussions with teachers after class in any convenient ways such as face-to-face talks, telephone conversations, email communications, and others, which are in line with the liberal arts education at Lingnan University.

The requirement for the award of the M.Sc. degree is the completion of a total of 30 credits, including 10 courses (6 required and 4 elective). The teaching pattern is normally a 3-hour lecture and/or seminar per week per course. The normal duration for the M.Sc. programme is one year full-time study, and a student could extend his or her study up to the maximum duration of three years, which is subject to the approval of the Programme Director and the Head of the Department. Students would normally complete four required courses and one elective course in the first term (August to December), and two required courses and three elective courses in the second term (January to May).

A student who has passed the public, professional, and other examinations recognized by the University, or has successfully completed a similar course at an appropriate level offered by another tertiary institution, may apply for course exemption. A maximum of two courses (6 credits) are allowed to be exempted. The student has to take another course(s) on the programme to fulfill a total of 30 credits in order to graduate.
According to the laws of Hong Kong, forgery is a serious criminal offence that a convicted offender may be liable to imprisonment for fourteen years.
It also aims to help students with or without IT background understand and utilize the IT for supply chain integration in the international and global context.

Admission Requirements
Financial Matters

Admission Requirements

General qualifications
Hold a bachelor's degree awarded by a recognized tertiary institution or have equivalent qualifications, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Language requirements
In addition to the above, an applicant whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain:
a minimum score of 79 in the Test of English as a Foreign Language (TOEFL) Internet-Based Test; or

a band score of 6.0 or above in the International English Language Testing System (IELTS); or

a minimum score of 450 or above in the College English Test Band 6 (CET-6).

Applicants who do not fulfill the above language requirements but have an equivalent score in a recognized test, or an equivalent qualification assessment to prove their language proficiency will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Declaration
Any false, unclear or misleading information and/or document(s) provided by applicants in their application will disqualify their applications or revoke their admission offers, and any fees paid, including down payment or deposit, will be forfeited. According to the laws of Hong Kong, forgery is a serious criminal offence that a convicted offender may be liable to imprisonment for fourteen years.
