Programme Overview

Programme Overview

eBusiness is mainly concerned with the use of information and communication technologies to facilitate information exchange, commercial transactions, and knowledge sharing among interdependent firms, which usually operate in a supply chain network. The natural connection between eBusiness and supply chain management (SCM) necessitates the M.Sc. Programme in eBusiness and Supply Chain Management, which is offered and managed by the Department of Operations and Risk Management, Faculty of Business, Lingnan University. The programme is mainly designed to educate students with information technology (IT) backgrounds about key management strategies and approaches in the eBusiness environment. It also aims to help students with or without IT background understand and utilize the IT for supply chain integration in the international and global context. Growing international focus on the management of supply chain operations in eBusiness and the emergency of related international and global issues indicate the importance and relevance of this programme.

Aims

The programme aims to:
Strengthen students’ abilities to understand and use the information technologies that are necessary for supply chain operations in an eBusiness environment;
Equip students with the management strategies for effectively using information technologies to achieve supply chain integration in the international and global context;
Familiarize students with managing online and mobile business operations that are involved in a supply chain;
Expose to students the benefits for an organization through incorporating the Internet into its supply chain operations;
Foster students’ abilities in identifying the business changes resulting from the use of information technologies for organizational practices in supply chains.

Learning Outcomes

This programme focuses on the improvement of students’ management strategies and skills in supply chain operations in the eBusiness context, in addition to the education of information technologies. Accordingly, all teaching staffs in the programme will utilize a number of practical cases and managerial discussions in lecturing their courses. To help students understand the SCM-related issues in eBusiness, we will also encourage students to discuss important cases in class, and have interactive discussions with teachers after class in any convenient ways such as face-to-face talks, telephone conversations, email communications, and others, which are in line with the liberal arts education at Lingnan University.

Programme Structure

The requirement for the award of the M.Sc. degree is the completion of a total of 30 credits, including 10 courses (6 required and 4 elective). The teaching pattern is normally a 3-hour lecture and/or seminar per week per course. The normal duration for the M.Sc. programme is one year full-time study, and a student could extend his or her study up to the maximum duration of three years, which is subject to the approval of the Programme Director and the Head of the Department. Students would normally complete four required courses and one elective course in the first term (August to December), and two required courses and three elective courses in the second term (January to May).

A student who has passed the public, professional, and other examinations recognized by the University, or has successfully completed a similar course at an appropriate level offered by another tertiary institution, may apply for course exemption. A maximum of two courses (6 credits) are allowed to be exempted. The student has to take another course(s) on the programme to fulfill a total of 30 credits in order to graduate.

Programme Curriculum

Required Courses
ORM501 Fundamentals of Electronic Business
ORM502 Supply Chain Management in eBusiness
ORM503 Accounting and Financial Technology
ORM504 Business Data Analytics
ORM505 Mobile Technology and Applications in eBusiness
ORM506 Best Practices in eBusiness and Supply Chain Management

Elective Courses
ORM507 Legal Issues for eBusiness
ORM508 Electronic Marketing
ORM509 E-Commerce Implementation: Planning, Platform Selection, and Execution
ORM510 Social Media for eBusiness
ORM511 Project Management with Software
ORM512 Service Operations Management
ORM513 Purchasing and Logistics Management for eBusiness
ORM514 Information Systems Management
ORM515 Business Decision Making with Software
CDS521 Foundation of Artificial Intelligence
CDS527 Big Data Analytics
ORM545 Cloud Computing for Decision Making
MIB602 Integrated Marketing Communications
ACT615 Introduction to Python Programming

The offering of elective courses is subject to sufficient demand and faculty availability.

Pre-entry Courses

Applicants with no or limited background in information systems management and statistics will be required to complete pre-entry courses in August prior to the start of this master programme. The number of pre-entry courses required to take by an applicant will depend on his/ her undergraduate study.

The pre-entry programme includes two courses as listed below:
Information Systems Management
Statistics

Progression

For progression, students are required to achieve a cumulative GPA of 2.50 or above in order to proceed to the next term. Students who cannot meet such GPA requirement will normally be put on academic probation in the following term or discontinued.

Graduation

To qualify for the award of the Master of Science in eBusiness and Supply Chain Management offered by Lingnan University, students must, where applicable:
Complete 10 courses (6 core courses and 4 elective courses) and obtain a minimum of 30 credits;
Achieve a minimum CGPA of 2.67; and
Fulfil the University exit requirement on English language competency, if applicable; and fulfil all University/programme requirements.

A student who fulfils all the requirements for graduation shall be awarded a Master of Science in eBusiness and Supply Chain Management degree.

Students will be awarded a Postgraduate Diploma in eBusiness and Supply Chain Management if they complete 4 required courses with a minimum CGPA of 2.33, and do not continue in the programme. Acceptance of an intermediate award implies an intention not to complete the final award.

Graduate Highlights

Ms. ZHUO Siying (2025 Graduate)
Senior project engineer; Siemens Ltd （ HK ）

As someone who returned to school after working for several years, I particularly cherish my learning experience at eBSCM. The courses were not only practical but also cutting-edge. Our professors shared the latest insights and business case studies in supply chain management and e-commerce, which allowed me to step beyond the confines of my daily work and gain a new understanding of the driving forces and direction of industry change.
I particularly want to highlight the Business Data Analytics and Project Management courses. The data analysis tools and project management methods I learned didn't end with the course completion. Instead, they continue to provide value in my current work—whether in optimizing processes, analyzing data, or promoting cross-departmental collaboration—making me more efficient and better organized.
Looking back, the time I spent studying in the eBSCM programme was not only an update of my knowledge but also a transformation of my thinking. For those who are pursuing careers in supply chain and e-commerce, I believe eBSCM is an excellent choice!

Ms. SHAN Baohui (2024 Graduate)
Import Business Administrator, China Courier Services Limited (HK)
I am a graduate student of eBSCM programme at Lingnan university. Time flies, and I have already left the campus, stepped into society, and started my career.
The one-year postgraduate study life at Lingnan University, Hong Kong, is a very precious experience in my life. The school provides rich academic resources and excellent teaching staff, allowing me to deeply study professional knowledge, broaden my horizons, and improve my research ability. In the classroom, the rigorous academic attitude and in-depth teaching methods of the teachers have benefited me a lot; Outside the classroom, the school also provided us with a variety of extracurricular activities and communication opportunities, allowing me to make friends from different regions and different fields.
I am currently working at China Courier Services Limited in Hong Kong, mainly responsible for the management of the company's import business. During my work, I have made full use of the knowledge and skills I learned at Lingnan University in Hong Kong and achieved good results. I know that all this is inseparable from the training and education of the school. Therefore, I would like to thank Lingnan University in Hong Kong again for providing me with such an excellent learning platform and development opportunities.
Finally, I wish Lingnan University would get better and better and cultivate more excellent talents to make greater contributions to society!

Ms.XU Haoning(2024 Graduate)
Business Analyst, IT Solution Ltd (Bank Of China (HK))
I think it is the right decision to choose Lingnan University and ebscm. In the liberal arts education of Lingnan University, I chose courses such as big data and AI to continue to improve my technology while learning courses related to e-commerce supply chain. The school attaches great importance to the all-round development of students. I participated in the SRES project on NLP, signed up for the career guidance to clarify the employment direction and revise my resume, which are of great help to my development.
The most important thing in this year is the education of the academy. I am very interested in business analysis. I understand technology and want to combine it with specific cases to get valuable results. I clearly remember that in Prof.YAN Aihua's first class, Business Data Analysis course, Professor Yan explained each case skillfully. I was excited by the algorithm behind these models. I was also deeply influenced by Prof. LENG Mingming. I realized that project management is a rigorous, scientific and systematic course, and any resource scheduling and extension need to pass accurate evaluation. Similar courses include Prof. LIANG Li Ping's Business Decision making course, Francis Wong's accounting course... Majors use the best resources to broaden students' horizons.
The liberal arts education in Lingnan University has improved my comprehensive ability and enriched my resume background, giving me an advantage in job hunting, and only then have I found a job opportunity. My gratitude is beyond words, and I sincerely wish my Alma mater a rich and fruitful tree and a fragrant year after year.

Mr. LI Guanzhi (2024 Graduate)
Supply Chain Controller, VidaXL International (HK) Limited

With a passion for supply chain and a strong desire to relocate my life and career to my dream city, Hong Kong, I am extremely proud to be a member of Lingnan University.

Here, I have met many qualified professors who teach effectively and patiently; I have enjoyed their engaging lectures which equipped me with the ability to think critically and work unitedly to solve supply chain-related problems. What's more, I got precious friendships with my classmates. I would like to say that I've benefited greatly from everybody around me during the past year. And those benefits all helped me to be outstanding in the job market, and my future.

Now as a graduate from LU, I've already started my career as the supply chain controller in a Dutch company based in Hong Kong. Whenever looking back on those days, I feel a sense of beauty and accomplishment. For me, the past year as a postgraduate student in the eBSCM programme has not only been a temporary terminal on my academic journey; but also, a fresh beginning of a new chapter in my life.

Ms. YANG Huijie (2024 Graduate)
Shanghai University of International Business and Economics, Industrial Economics, PHD
I am very grateful for the learning time in the MSceBSCM programme. It is during this learning experience that I have defined my future development direction and made me surer that I want to study the digital economy. During my application period, the professors and the teachers of the course group provided me with a lot of support. They encouraged me to pursue my goals and gave me suggestions on the direction and content of my research. Their help made me unforgettable! And, the content of the course is very novel and rich, which gives me a new insight into the current development of the world economy. The MSceBSCM is definitely a good choice!

Ms. DAI Yanyan (2024 Graduate)
Operation Assistant, Celestial Asia Securities Holdings Limited
What started as a “ let ’ s give it a try ” moment became an amazing journey when I participated in the National Sales Competition campus selection. Over five weeks, Professor Tang Cheng guided us through meticulously designed workshops, building a solid foundation that gave me the confidence to face any challenge during the national sales competition.
This year, I also had the opportunity to join a study tour in Singapore, where I immersed myself in the local culture, visited companies, and gained a deeper understanding of how business knowledge is applied on a global stage. This experience taught me that learning goes beyond textbooks — it ’ s about stepping out of your comfort zone time and time again. Each experience shapes a better version of myself, and it leaves me filled with excitement for the future! 🚀

Mr. MA Herui (2024 Graduate)
Delivery Engineer, Shenzhen SDMC technology co.,Ltd
Although I have already left the campus of Lingnan University, my study and living experiences there continue to have a profound impact on me. I still vividly remember how I prepared for the IELTS test with all my might, almost frantically, to successfully gain admission to Lingnan University. Fortunately, my efforts paid off, and I eventually became a student there—a feeling of accomplishment that remains unforgettable.
After an intense spring recruitment process, I was fortunate enough to join a well-known listed company in Shenzhen in a supply chain-related position. These achievements were made possible by the education and experiences I received during my time at Lingnan University.
At Lingnan University, I received not only strong humanistic care but also course content that greatly enriched my professional knowledge. For instance, project management course, Big Data Analytics, and supply chain management have all provided me with a unique advantage in my work.

Mr. WAN Yu (2023 Graduate)
Category Executive of DFI Retail Group
Reflecting on my tenure within the MSceBSCM Programme, I am grateful for the valuable experiences I gained during this academic voyage. With a background in e-commerce and retail, complemented by four years of practical experience, I found the courses related to supply chain and data analysis exceptionally relevant and advantageous. These courses offered in this Programme have equipped me with useful skills and comprehensive knowledge, which have been instrumental in my professional development. Specifically, they have given me a deeper understanding of supply chain optimization and the strategic use of data for informed decision-making. On a personal level, I noticed a significant boost in confidence within my professional domain. Whether I choose to return to Mainland China or pursue new opportunities in Hong Kong, I am optimistic about the promising prospects that await me. The MSceBSCM Programme has provided me with a robust foundation and equipped me with the essential skills to excel in a myriad of career paths.

Mr. WANG Chenglong (2023 Graduate)
Management Trainee at Sa Sa Dot Com Limited (Hong Kong)
My experience in the MSceBSCM Programme has been a life-changing one. It has not only shaped my innovative and practical skills but has also fostered my deep appreciation for the diverse perspectives gained through cross-cultural learning. Currently, as a Management Trainee at Sa Sa Dot Com Limited (Hong Kong), I am able to actively apply the skills and knowledge gained from the Programme. It is immensely rewarding to contribute to Sa Sa’s operations and witness the positive impact I bring to the company’s growth. I would like to share a thought with everyone: when you first embark on this journey, you might feel as though you have “nothing and nothing”. However, by the time you navigate through this enriching experience, you will realize that you have not only gained a wealth of knowledge but also built a beautiful kingdom of life experiences, as magical and fascinating as Disneyland.

Ms. YE Xiuxia (2023 Graduate)
Graduate Trainee of DSV Air&Sea Ltd.
I still remember vividly that I was so excited when I was admitted to MSceBSCM. This program is about my interests, and supply chain management is a promising industry. MSceBSCM provided students with lots of useful courses and seminars, where I learned a variety of the latest knowledge about supply chain management. Besides, some courses, such as Big Data Analysis and Business Decision Making, taught us how to use software to deal with business problems, which is beneficial to my career. I am sure that if you guys would like to develop your career in the supply chain industry, MSceBSCM is a good choice for you!

Mr. LIN Wenxin (2022 Graduate)
Sourcing Manager of the Procurement Qualification Department, Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd.
It is my great honour to study and grow in eBusiness and Supply Chain Management Programme. After studying this Programme, I have developed an intense interest in eBusiness. I was impressed by one of the courses – Supply Chain Management in eBusiness. The case studies presented in this course provided me with a deeper understanding of various industries and proved invaluable during interviews. I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to the teaching assistant and all the professors who offered valuable suggestions regarding my studies, personal life, and career choices. Currently, I am proud to hold the position of Sourcing Manager in the Procurement Qualification Department at Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd.

Application

Explore More

Admissions

Admission Requirements
Financial Matters
Application

Application

Application Period
From 1 October 2025 to 30 June 2026
Admissions will be closed once the quota is reached.

Application Method
Apply through the online application system at https://www.ln.edu.hk/admission/apply

Confidential Recommendation Form
The referee must be applicant’s current or former direct supervisor or teacher at university who should not be the applicant’s friends or relatives.
The applicant should complete Part A, then invite his/her referees to complete Part B. The referee should return this form to the Programme Office within a month after the applicant submitted the online application by: (1) mailing this form in a sealed envelope direct to MSceBSCM, Room 112/1, Simon and Eleanor Kwok Building, Lingnan University, 8 Castle Peak Road, Tuen Mun, Hong Kong, or (2) emailing this form to [email protected] via the email account stated in Part B Item 5 (we only accept forms sent by university or company email account).

Notes
The Programme team will start processing the application when all the application materials including the application fee are received.
Applicants may be required to attend an interview (face-to-face/online) conducted by the Programme Office.
Any false or misleading information given in the application will lead to disqualification of the applicant and any fees paid will be forfeited.

Financial Matters

Fees for Academic Year 2026-27

Application Fee:
HK$400

Full-time Tuition Fee:
HK$208,000 for local students
HK$238,000 for non-local students

Pre-entry Course Fee:
HK$3,000 for EACH pre-entry course

Notes:
All fees paid are non-refundable and non-transferable no matter the student completes the Programme or not.
A deposit of HK$600 is payable by all admitted students. The amount is used to cover the University’s graduation fee.

Scholarships

Scholarships might be considered for outstanding students on the basis of academic merit. All students admitted to the programme will automatically be the candidates for the scholarships.

Financial Assistance

Local students can apply for Extended Non-Means-Tested Loan Scheme offered by the HKSAR Government. For eligibility and details, please visit the Working Family and Student Financial Assistance Agency’s website.

Employment opportunities include listed companies, banks (commercial and investment banks), fund companies, asset management companies, accounting firms, think tanks, multinational companies, government units, public administration, non-governmental organizations (NGOs), social enterprises, consulting firms, etc.

Related positions include ESG analyst, ESG manager, ESG and sustainability officer, ESG consultant, risk management officer/consultant, chief sustainability officer (CSO), ESG strategy director, CSR director, ESG consulting consultant/manager, etc.
​ In addition, graduates of the Sustainable Technology Specialization can also work as clean technology researchers, environmental consultants, energy analysts, energy managers, etc.

Our programme has taken a significant step forward in fostering industry-academia collaboration by signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with CECEP Environmental Consulting Group Limited, a leading organization in ESG consultancy and green finance.
This partnership aims to provide MScESGM students with valuable internship and career opportunities, bridging the gap between academic learning and real-world application.
The role of an Advisory Board is to act as an interface between Government/industry/commerce/the community at large and the programme concerned.

admissionNotes: Admission Requirements Fees and Scholarships Admission Requirements Hold a bachelor's degree awarded by a recognized tertiary institution or have equivalent qualifications, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis. An applicant whose degree is not from a tertiary institution in Hong Kong or an English-speaking country should obtain a minimum score of 79 (internet-based test) in the Test of English as a Foreign Language (TOEFL), a band score of 6.0 or above in the International English Language Testing System (IELTS), or a minimum score of 450 or above in the College English Test Band 6 (CET-6). For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are conducted at a test centre and within the validity period, viz. 2 years, from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort. For CET6 qualification for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are within the validity period, viz. 5 years, from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort. For MScESGM applicants with no or limited background in accounting, economics, finance, and business in their bachelor’s studies will be required to pass either one or two 30-hour pre-entry courses, Accounting or Introduction to Business, Economics and Finance, based on the applicants’ undergraduate transcripts prior to the start of this programme. For MScESGM(ST) applicants with no or limited background in economics, finance, and business in their bachelor's studies will be required to pass a 30-hour pre-entry course, Introduction to Business, Economics and Finance, prior to the start of this programme. Department of Accountancy

Master of Science in Environmental Social and Governance Management

Course Description

Explore More

Programme Overview

Sustainable Technology Concentration
Objectives And Mission
Programme Structure
Course Description
Career Prospects
Student Activities
Committee Related Information

Course Description

Faculty members contribute to top-quality teaching for the programme. A brief introduction of each of the 23 courses is provided below：

ESG501 The Economics of Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG)

The Economics of Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) provides students with a foundation understanding and appreciation of the economics concerning ESG. The course helps students to master basic economic methods and use them to analyse ESG-related issues and find solutions. The course covers three areas of economic analysis: microeconomics, macroeconomics, and applied linear regressions and forecasting for practical analysis of ESG issues.

ESG502 Sustainable Financial Management

Sustainable Financial Management introduces sustainable corporate finance, particularly financial decision-making, emphasising sustainability. The focus is on financial decision-making in a market with increasing environmental, Social, and governance demand (ESG). Issues addressed include asset valuation models, investment decision-making, risk analysis, financial planning, working capital management, the cost of capital, and financial markets.

ESG503 ESG Accounting and Disclosure

ESG Accounting and Disclosure provides a framework for developing corporate environmental and social performance measures, assessing their reliability, reporting to external stakeholders, and assisting managers in strategic and operational decisions affecting environmental costs and risks. This course provides a comprehensive overview of the theory and practice of ESG accounting and disclosure. Major topics include the framework and regulation of ESG reporting, the external assurance of ESG reporting, assessment of ESG performance, and accounting for key sustainability issues, such as climate and water.

ESG504 Ethics, Law, and Policy for Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG)

Ethics, Law, and Policy for Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) introduces the main rules, principles and concepts of ethics, law, and policy in ESG practices. It evaluates the forces shaping environmental, social, and government policy making. By analysing the policy cycle and the basics of policy development and appraisal, students will better understand the influences that shape the ESG policies and practices locally and globally. The course will also cover the institutions, doctrines, and rules in the business and corporation content to equip students with the knowledge and skills to be ethical decisionmakers in the ESG domain.

ESG505 ESG Management and Strategies

ESG Management and Strategies provide the knowledge required to understand how business organisations work and how they can be managed to create economic and non-economic value for their key stakeholders: customers, employees, investors, business partners, and social communities. Students are expected to gain knowledge and tools to become purpose-driven business leaders, explore the different business models that companies can use to drive change, and explains why purpose-driven businesses are particularly well-positioned to tackle the world’s ESG challenges.

ESG511 Sustainability in Hong Kong and Mainland China

Sustainability in Hong Kong and Mainland China provides the students with an overall understanding of the regulations, guidelines, and measures of the sustainability framework and practices in Hong Kong and Mainland and pinpoints and benchmarks the best practices of ESG management internationally. The course covers sustainable developments in Hong Kong and Mainland China, the roles and importance of NGOs in sustainable development, the environmental problems in China, and sustainability innovation in the future. The course will focus on identifying and evaluating the challenges and issues firms face in integrating environmental, social, and governance factors in managing business organisations in Hong Kong and Mainland China. The students will think critically about the issues and challenges faced by various stakeholders in sustainable development. They engage in meaningful social actions that promote sustainable development locally and beyond.

ESG512 ESG Practices in Business

ESG Practices in Business describes key ESG issues and explains how stakeholders influence corporate ESG performance. It analyses ESG risks and opportunities and assesses ESG company performance using publicly available information. The course enables students to identify and evaluate ESG issues as an essential factor in corporate decision-making. The concourse contents are built on the basic framework for corporate governance, corporate social responsibility, and corporate environmental considerations.

ESG513 ESG Investing

ESG Investing emphasises the ESG mindset for almost all investment settings to promote sustainability in the capital market. The course introduces (1) the background, current developments and trends of ESG investing, (2) the participants, institutions, and various financial instruments in the ESG marketplace, and (3) the concepts, framework, techniques, data and toolkits related to using ESG considerations for identifying and informing sustainable investment solutions. The students will grasp ESG analysis and its incorporation into decision-making to ensure sound investment judgment.

ESG514 Green Innovation and Entrepreneurship

Green Innovation and Entrepreneurship provides the background and understanding of creating green products and services through invention, creativity and an entrepreneur’s mindset, focusing on the environmental industry. It articulates the technical and economic fundamentals of important and emerging sustainable technologies and explores entrepreneurial opportunities in green technology and business. The course demonstrates how the economic and technical performance of sustainable technologies can be measured and compared.

ESG515 ESG in Marketing

ESG in Marketing promotes a company's strategy's environmental, social and governance attributes to its investors, buyers and other stakeholders in alignment with corporate purpose and mission. The course introduces the concepts and principles of environmental, social and corporate governance-based marketing, its many applications and implications, and what it means to consumers and other significant audiences. The course aims to equip the students with principles of sustainability-based marketing, harnessing sustainable consumer behaviour, and closely examining sustainability marketing transformation possibilities and problems.

ESG516 ESG and Big Data

ESG and Big Data are designed to equip the students with a broad understanding of the concepts and techniques of the statistical analysis of ESG. The course aims to equip the students with an analytic mindset in approaching business problems. It provides the students with an understanding of the data analytics model called the IMPACT Cycle (Identify the question, Master the data, Perform test plan, Address and refine results, Communicate insights, and Track outcomes). The course's objectives are to equip the students with a deep appreciation of how data analytics and data management affect the quality of ESG and to develop their abilities to assess and analyse ESG performance.

ESG517 Sustainable Supply Chain Management

Sustainable Supply Chain Management is increasingly at the centre of business decision-making. The course introduces students to the role and importance of sustainable development in supply chain management. Students will have a solid grasp of measuring environmental impacts in supply chains, operational aspects of sustainable supply chains, business models and strategies in sustainable supply chains, and the social dimension of sustainable supply chains. With sustainability as a central core of learning, the course equips the students with the creative problem-solving skills to address the supply chain challenges many businesses face in the ESG content.

ESG521 Conventional Energy and Renewable Energy

Conventional Energy and Renewable Energy is a dynamic course that explores the multifaceted realm of energy production, with a strong emphasis on sustainable and renewable resources. This course provides a comprehensive overview of traditional energy sources, such as fossil fuels and nuclear energy, and their environmental impact. It then dives into the transformative potential of renewable energy technologies, including solar, wind, hydropower, and bioenergy. Students will examine the principles, applications, and policy implications of these green energy solutions. Through hands-on projects and real-world case studies, learners will develop a solid foundation in energy sustainability, equipping them to address the pressing global energy challenges of today and tomorrow.

ESG522 Carbon Neutrality Strategies

This course explores strategies for achieving carbon neutrality in various sectors, emphasizing the transition to sustainable practices and the mitigation of greenhouse gas emissions. Students will analyze case studies and develop practical carbon reduction plans.

ESG523 Clean Technology Innovation

Clean Technology Innovation is an interdisciplinary course at the forefront of sustainable development and environmental responsibility. This course explores cutting-edge clean technologies and innovations designed to address pressing global challenges related to pollution, resource depletion, and climate change. Students will examine the principles and practices of clean technology, renewable energy, waste reduction, and sustainable resource management. Through case studies, hands-on projects, and discussions with industry experts, learners will gain insights into the latest advancements and emerging trends in clean tech, equipping them to contribute to a more sustainable and environmentally friendly future

ESG531 Renewable Power Technologies

Renewable Power Technologies is an engaging and informative course designed to explore the diverse world of sustainable energy sources. In this course, students will explore the science, technology, and applications of renewable energy, including solar, wind, hydro, and geothermal power. Through a blend of theoretical insights and practical applications, students will gain a comprehensive understanding of the principles behind harnessing renewable resources to reduce carbon emissions and combat climate change. Topics covered include energy conversion systems, grid integration, policy frameworks, and emerging innovations in the field. Join us on a journey towards a cleaner and more sustainable energy
future.

ESG532 Energy Efficiency and Management

Energy Efficiency and Management is a comprehensive course designed to explore strategies, techniques, and best practices for optimizing energy consumption in various sectors. Students will study the principles of energy conservation, sustainable practices, and effective management of energy resources. The course emphasizes the critical role of energy efficiency in mitigating environmental impacts and reducing operational costs for businesses and organizations. Through case studies and practical exercises, students will gain valuable insights into energy auditing, retrofitting, and the integration of renewable energy sources. By the end of this course, students will be equipped with the knowledge and skills to contribute to sustainable energy practices and drive efficiency improvements in diverse settings.

ESG533 Green Logistics

Green Logistics is a course that explores the integration of environmentally sustainable practices within supply chain management and logistics. Students will examine strategies for minimizing the ecological footprint of transportation, distribution, and procurement processes. The course covers topics such as eco-friendly transportation modes, waste reduction, sustainable sourcing, and green packaging solutions. Through case studies and real-world examples, learners will gain practical insights into creating supply chains that prioritize environmental responsibility while maintaining efficiency and profitability. This course equips students with the knowledge and tools to contribute to environmentally conscious supply chain operations.

ESG534 Sustainable Power and Economic Assessment

Sustainable Power and Economic Assessment is a course that focuses on the evaluation, analysis, and integration of diverse energy technologies. Students will delve into the principles and methods of assessing energy sources, conversion processes, and their environmental, economic, and societal impacts. This course emphasizes critical thinking and decision-making skills, enabling learners to make informed choices regarding energy technology selection and implementation. Through case studies and practical exercises, students will gain hands-on experience in evaluating renewable and non-renewable energy technologies, optimizing energy systems, and contributing to sustainable energy solutions in a rapidly evolving global energy landscape.

ESG535 Sustainable Urban Energy Planning

Sustainable Urban Energy Planning explores the complex intersection of urbanization and energy consumption. Students will study the principles and practices of developing energy-efficient and environmentally friendly urban areas. This course emphasizes the integration of renewable energy sources, smart technologies, and sustainable design into city planning. Students will gain practical skills in evaluating and designing urban energy systems that contribute to a greener and more sustainable future for cities through case studies and projects. This course is ideal for those interested in urban development, environmental sustainability, and energy efficiency in urban areas.

ESG536 Green Finance and Investment

This course studiesthe dynamic and evolving field of green finance and sustainable investment. As the global economy increasingly recognizes the urgency of addressing environmental challenges, understanding the principles and practices of green finance becomes paramount. Students will explore the intersection of finance and sustainability, learning to evaluate and promote environmentally responsible investment opportunities. Topics include sustainable investment strategies, impact assessment, ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) criteria, green bonds, and the role of financial institutions in driving sustainable development. By analyzing case studies and real-world examples, students will gain the knowledge and skills needed to navigate the growing landscape of green finance and contribute to a more sustainable
future.

ACT615 Introduction to Python Programming

This course offers a comprehensive introduction to Python programming tailored specifically for business students. The primary objective of this course is to empower students with essential knowledge and skills to effectively apply Python programming within the realm of business practice. Students will acquire a solid foundation in the fundamentals of the Python programming language and explore its diverse applications in various business domains. By engaging in hands-on exercises and analysing real-world examples, students will cultivate a profound understanding of business theory and practice, while concurrently honing their analytical acumen to critically assess and challenge existing paradigms in business-related fields.

ORM545 Cloud-based Solutions for Digital Business Transformation

This course offers a comprehensive introduction to cloud computing and services, covering foundational topics such as basic architecture, cloud service and deployment models, and cloud solutions for digital business transformation. Students will also examine popular cloud computing platforms, with a focus on Alibaba Cloud as the primary platform for hands-on learning. Throughout the course, students will learn how to build and manage cloud systems, optimise performance, and enhance security. In addition, this curriculum includes an exploration of big data and artificial intelligence, providing insights into data processing and AI tools. Designed for IT professionals and enthusiasts, the course employs real-world examples and practical exercises using Alibaba Cloud. This approach ensures that learners gain valuable skills and are well-prepared for future technological advancements.

Programme Structure

The MScESGM Programme

The MScESGM programme consists of 15 courses, 5 core courses and 10 elective courses. Each course carries three credits. To graduate, students must complete 30 credits or 10 courses with a cumulative grade point average (GPA) of 2.67 or above. They must complete 5 core courses (15 credits) and choose any 5 from the elective courses offered (15 credits) in order to complete 30 credits for the programme.

The normal duration of the programme is one year on a full-time basis. The maximum study period to complete the programme is three years. There will be two terms. Term 1 typically starts in late August or early September and ends in December. Term 2 usually begins in January and ends in May. The pre-entry course will be offered in August before the normal term starts. The classes will be normally held on weekdays, but occasionally weekday evening or weekend classes will be held to accommodate the schedule of part-time instructors if any.

Core Courses
ESG501 The Economics of Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG)

ESG502 Sustainable Financial Management

ESG503 ESG Accounting and Disclosure

ESG504 Ethics, Law, and Policy for Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG)

ESG505 ESG Management and Strategies

Elective Courses (any 5 courses)
Remarks: The offering of elective courses is subject to sufficient demand and faculty availability.

ESG511 Sustainability in Hong Kong and Mainland China

ESG512 ESG Practices in Business

ESG513 ESG Investing

ESG514 Green Innovation and Entrepreneurship

ESG515 ESG in Marketing

ESG516 ESG and Big Data

ESG517 Sustainable Supply Chain Management

ACT615 Introduction to Python Programming

ORM545 Cloud-based Solutions for Digital Business Transformation

ESG522 Carbon Neutrality Strategies

The MScESGM Sustainable Technology Concentration

The MScESGM Sustainable Technology Concentration consists of 12 courses, 6 core courses and 6 elective courses. Each course carries three credits. To graduate, students must complete 30 credits or ten courses with a cumulative grade point average (GPA) of 2.67 or above. They must complete 6 core courses (18 credits) and choose any 4 from the elective courses offered (12 credits) in order to complete 30 credits for the programme.

Core Courses
ESG501The Economics of Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG)

ESG502 Sustainable Financial Management

ESG503 ESG Management and Strategies

ESG521 Conventional Energy and Renewable Energy

ESG522 Carbon Neutrality Strategies

ESG523 Clean Technology Innovation

Elective Courses (any 4 courses)
Remarks: The offering of elective courses is subject to sufficient demand and faculty availability.

ESG531 Renewable Power Technologies

ESG532 Energy Efficiency and Management

ESG533 Green Logistics

ESG534 Sustainable Power and Economic Assessment

ESG535 Sustainable Urban Energy Planning

ESG536 Green Finance and Investment

Admission Requirements

An applicant should:
Hold a bachelor's degree awarded by a recognized tertiary institution or have equivalent qualifications, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.
An applicant whose degree is not from a tertiary institution in Hong Kong or an English-speaking country should obtain a minimum score of 79 (internet-based test) in the Test of English as a Foreign Language (TOEFL), a band score of 6.0 or above in the International English Language Testing System (IELTS), or a minimum score of 450 or above in the College English Test Band 6 (CET-6).

For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are conducted at a test centre and within the validity period, viz. 2 years, from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort.
For CET6 qualification for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are within the validity period, viz. 5 years, from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort.

Pre-entry Course
For MScESGM applicants with no or limited background in accounting, economics, finance, and business in their bachelor’s studies will be required to pass either one or two 30-hour pre-entry courses, Accounting or Introduction to Business, Economics and Finance, based on the applicants’ undergraduate transcripts prior to the start of this programme.
For MScESGM(ST) applicants with no or limited background in economics, finance, and business in their bachelor's studies will be required to pass a 30-hour pre-entry course, Introduction to Business, Economics and Finance, prior to the start of this programme.

Career Prospects

Graduates of the MScESGM programme have a wide range of career options in environmental, social and governance management and sustainable development related fields. Employment opportunities include listed companies, banks (commercial and investment banks), fund companies, asset management companies, accounting firms, think tanks, multinational companies, government units, public administration, non-governmental organizations (NGOs), social enterprises, consulting firms, etc. Related positions include ESG analyst, ESG manager, ESG and sustainability officer, ESG consultant, risk management officer/consultant, chief sustainability officer (CSO), ESG strategy director, CSR director, ESG consulting consultant/manager, etc. ​

In addition, graduates of the Sustainable Technology Specialization can also work as clean technology researchers, environmental consultants, energy analysts, energy managers, etc.

Our programme has taken a significant step forward in fostering industry-academia collaboration by signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with CECEP Environmental Consulting Group Limited, a leading organization in ESG consultancy and green finance. This partnership aims to provide MScESGM students with valuable internship and career opportunities, bridging the gap between academic learning and real-world application.

Committee Related Information

At the programme level, a Programme Curriculum Committee (PCC) is set up for a taught postgraduate programme. The major responsibilities are:

To take charge of the degree programme, including the design, implementation and review of the curriculum, inter alia, the achievement of the intended student learning outcomes.

To liaise with all Department/Units/Divisions concerned on programme matters, such as course offerings for each academic year.

To liaise with Registry/School of Graduate Studies on all academic matters, including review of academic regulations and examination matters.

To review admissions policy for the degree programme.

To deal with all other matters related to the degree programme.

Membership

Chairman
Programme Director

Members
Head of Department of Accountancy
All teaching staff of the programme
At least one student representative

Secretary
To be appointed by the Chairman

A Board of Examiners is set up for a taught postgraduate programme to oversee examination matters. The major duties are:

To maintain the academic standards of the programme at a level appropriate to the type of award.

To approve examination question papers after considering the views and recommendations of External Academic Advisers, where appropriate.

To maintain general supervision of the system of continuous assessment, where it applies.

To maintain the proper conduct of examinations.

To determine students’ examination results.

To assess the progress of students and to report its decisions, including its recommendations for award classification, to the Postgraduate Studies Committee.

To consider appeals referred to it by the Registrar.

Membership

Chairman
Programme Director

Members
Dean of Faculty of Business
Head of Department of Accountancy
All staff concerned with the setting and marking of any part of the examination.

Secretary
To be appointed by the Chairman

The Staff-Student Consultation Committee provides a platform for students to express their views on the programme and individual courses, and for the departments/academic units to solicit student feedback on specific aspects of the programme and courses for continuous improvement. The major responsibilities are:

To promote understanding between students and teaching staff.

To consider feedback from students regarding teaching, learning and course evaluation and issues of importance for enhancing teaching and learning quality.

To consider any other matters of concern to students.

To review and monitor the learning and teaching quality assurance mechanisms and processes within the programme.

To provide feedback to students on changes made to courses and teaching in response to the Course Teaching and Learning Evaluation exercise.

To formulate strategies in helping students to learn more effectively.

Membership

Chairman
Programme Director

Members
At least two staff members teaching for the programme in the current academic year
At least three student representatives

Secretary
To be appointed by the Chairman

The appointment of External Academic Advisers (EAAs) is intended to provide an external and impartial check that internal standards are being fairly and consistently applied and that Lingnan's measures are comparable with those of similar degree programmes at the tertiary level in Kong and outside Hong Kong. The duties of EAAs include:

To assist the programmes in maintaining standards comparable to those in other tertiary institutions of international repute;
To take an overview of the assessment arrangements, including comments on any assessment materials for continuous assessment, examinations papers and assessment/marking schemes of selected courses sent to them. Comments received on constant assessment materials, examination papers, and assessment/marking schemes should be used for future reference and planning purposes;
To receive and assess a sample of student works, including examination scripts, dissertations, project reports, essays, case studies, etc., as appropriate to enable him/her to understand students' academic standards. Comments from External Academic Advisers regarding the appropriateness of markings should be used for future reference and planning purposes;
To satisfy himself/herself that the standards set by the Board of Examiners concerned are appropriate and comparable with the standards of equivalent programmes at other tertiary institutions;
To submit a confidential report to the President by 1st July. The report should cover issues in the following areas with reference to those in programmes at other comparable tertiary institutions:

(1) the structure, organisation, design and marking of all assessments.
(2) academic standards, i.e., examination papers, course work and grading with regard to the quality of outcome, the performance of students, and the level of the award.
(3)development of the programme, with regard to such matters as the curriculum, the assessment scheme and resources.

The role of an Advisory Board is to act as an interface between Government/industry/commerce/the community at large and the programme concerned. An Advisory Board shall normally meet once a year to help plan and keep under review the following aspects of work including future developments :

L evel and length of courses/programme(s) concerned in relation to local and regional needs;

The relevance of the courses/programme(s) in relation to the local and regional needs;

The prospects of local and regional employment for graduates;

The adequacy of the equipment and other resources of the academic units to fulfil local and regional needs;

The development of teaching and other activities carried out jointly by the academic unit(s) and the appropriate sector of the community;

I nvestigation consultancy and other services given by the academic unit(s); and

K eep under review the assistance that the community can give to further the objectives of the University in the subject concerned by way of practical training facilities, the provision of part-time teaching staff and equipment, the award of scholarships, student-fellowships, etc.

Membership

Chairman
To be nominated by the Convener and appointed by the Senate

Convener
Programme Director

Members
To be nominated by the Convener and appointed by the Senate

Secretary
To be appointed by the Convener

Observers and Advisers
The Committee may invite any persons to attend any meetings as observers or advisers

Objectives and Mission

The MScESGM programme is designed to nurture a pool of professionals and specialists in environmental, social, and governance management. It intends to equip the students with a good understanding of the theoretical framework and best practices of ESG management in businesses. This programme seeks to address the talent shortage of expertise and qualified staff in ESG management, an increasingly acute problem facing the business community.

Aims of the Programme

The programme aims to:
Equip students with an understanding of theories and concepts of ESG management and help students develop an appreciation of sustainability;
Enable students to critically analyse the opportunities and challenges of the sustainable community and develop problem-solving skills as effective ESG professionals and specialists;
Provide students with the opportunities to engage with the broad sustainable community and develop practical communication skills with various audiences about ESG;
Nurture the next generation of ethical sustainability leaders for the betterment of the development of ESG in society.

Programme Intended Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of the MScESGM programme, students should be able to:
PILO-1 Demonstrate a mastery of the knowledge and skills required to function effectively as ESG specialists, professionals, and entrepreneurs in for-profit and not-for-profit sustainable establishments;
PILO-2 Synthesise and critically analyse the operational and managerial challenges about ESG faced by a sustainable establishment and formulate strategies for creative problem-solving;
PILO-3 Communicate effectively with a wide range of audiences and stakeholders of the sustainable establishments with collaborative spirit and teamwork;
PILO-4 Make ethical and socially responsible judgments in professional practices of ESG management.

Application Method

1. Application Period

The application period for applying for MScESGM 2026-27 is 1 October 2025 to 30 June 2026.
Admissions will be closed once the quota is reached.

2. Application Fee

A non-refundable and non-transferable application fee of HK$400 is charged for each application.

3. Application Procedures
Applicants can apply online.
For online application, please access the University’s online application system via the Internet at https://www.ln.edu.hk/admission/apply/.

Supporting Documents
Applicants should scan and submit the following documents to the Online Application System for evaluation of their applications:

Identity documents (e.g. Identity card*, passport or other travel document)
Official Transcript (for academic qualifications of undergraduate and/or postgraduate studies):
An official final transcript for a completed programme
The latest transcript if the programme is not yet completed
An official English-translated copy must be attached if the transcript is not in English. The interpretation of grades (in English) is also required.
Degree Certificate/Graduation Certificate/Proof of Study (as appropriate)
Verification proof by third-party authorities (for a completed programme):
Online Verification Report of Higher Education Degree Certificate (中國高等教育學位在綫驗証報告), both Chinese and English versions, issued by the CHSI (中國高等教育學生信息網) for qualification(s) awarded by universities in Mainland China
Online Query of Overseas Credentials Evaluation Reports國(境)外學歷學位認証書 issued by the Chinese Service Center for Scholarly Exchange (CSCSE) (教育部留學服務中心) (applicable to Mainland applicants with qualifications awarded by overseas universities)
Proof of English language proficiency, e.g. IETLS, TOEFL Score Report (applicable to applicants whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent, is not from a tertiary institution where English is used as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the degree programme)
Personal statement
Reference letters

*Do not upload copy of HKID. Local students will be required to present HKID for verification upon registration.

No Dishonest
Any false or misleading information and/or document(s) provided in the application, or any dishonest conduct observed during the application process, e.g. plagiarism, employing surrogate examination-taker, etc., will result in disqualification of application for admission, revocation of admission offer or termination of studies at the University if admitted, and any fees paid will be forfeited.

Falsification of documents or the use of falsified documents is a very serious offence and is absolutely not tolerated by the University. According to the laws of Hong Kong, forgery is a serious criminal offence that a convicted offender may be liable to imprisonment for a maximum of 14 years.

4. Selection

All applicants for admission to the programme will be considered on the basis of academic and professional qualifications, work experience, suitability, and potential. Applicants may be required to attend an interview (via online) conducted by the Director of Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office and one more faculty member.
Applications are processed on a rolling basis until all places are filled. Early applications are strongly encouraged.

FAQS

What is the duration of the Programme?

The normal duration for the Programme is one year full-time and the maximum period of study is three years. Teaching normally begins in late August and ends in May. Students who are required to attend the pre-entry course will normally take the course from late July– prior to the beginning of the new academic year.

When is the application period for next academic year?

The application generally begins on 1 October and closes on 30 April of the following year. Late application may be considered if spaces are still available.

What are the tuition fees per year?

The tuition fees for the academic year 2026-27 is HK$258,000 for both local student and non-local student.

What is the commencement date of the ESGM classes?

Usually the commencement date of the ESGM classes is late August to early September.

What is the time and venue of classes?

The ESGM classes will normally be held on weekdays or Saturdays at Lingnan campus. For the pre-entry courses (which will normally be offered from mid-July to late August), class meeting times and venues will depend on student enrollment. A formal announcement will be made prior to the commencement of the courses.

What is the language of instruction for the courses?

English (unless otherwise indicated in the course descriptions).

What is the course structure?

The full-time ESGM is a credit-based programme. All courses in the programme structure are designed on the basis of three-credit units. The normal duration for the full-time ESGM programme is one year and the maximum period of study will be three years from the date of first registration. Students are required to complete FOUR required courses (congruent to the Postgraduate Diploma in Environmental, Social, and Governance Management curriculum) in the first semester.Upon successful completion of the 15 credits in the first semester, students are eligible to proceed to the second semester. Students are required to complete the five elective courses (15 credits), in the second semester. Upon completion of these five courses and achieve a cumulative GPA of 2.67 or above, students are granted Master of Science in Environmental, Social, and Governance Management degrees, subject to the satisfaction of University graduation requirements.

What is the admission requirements for the ESGM programme?

To be eligible for entry into Master of Science in Environmental, Social, and Governance Managemen, applicants should hold a bachelor’s degree or equivalent from a recognized tertiary institution; or have other qualifications and experience, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

For MScESGM applicants with no or limited background in accounting, economics, finance, and business in their bachelor’s studies will be required to pass either one or two 30-hour pre-entry courses, Accounting or Introduction to Business, Economics and Finance, based on the applicants’ undergraduate transcripts prior to the start of this programme.

For MScESGM(ST) applicants with no or limited background in economics, finance, and business in their bachelor's studies will be required to pass a 30-hour pre-entry course, Introduction to Business, Economics and Finance, prior to the start of this programme.

What is the language requirement?

As teaching will be normally conducted in English, an applicant whose undergraduate degree is not from a tertiary institution in Hong Kong, or an English-speaking country should meet the English Language Proficiency Requirement below:

IELTS: minimum acceptable score of 6.0 or
TOEFL: minimum acceptable score of 550 (paper-based test) or 79 (internet-based test) or other equivalent English qualifications to be considered on a case-by-case basis. Applicants who have graduated from a university using English as a main teaching language do not normally need to present evidence of their language proficiency.

Can I update my TOEFL / IELTS and additional documents after my online submission?

Yes, after completion of your online submission and payment, you still can submit any additional supporting materials by email to: [email protected] and send the original copy of recommendation letters by post on or before the application deadline.

How to submit the application?

Applications to ESGM programme should be submitted via the University's OnlineApplication System here .

Will I be required to attend an interview?

The selection process will begin as soon as we receive your completed application materials and confidential recommendation forms. Applicants may be required to attend an interview (via online) conducted by the Director of Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office and one more faculty member.

I cannot process the online submission or e-Payment and the system has rejected my submission, why?

It may be the firewall problem, if so, you may need to use another computer or other computer network to try again.
Or you may have chosen a wrong “document type” when uploading your ID card/passport or transcript/certificate online. If so, you need to remove the files and re-upload the file again by choosing the correct document type online.
For any questions, please email to [email protected]

How can I apply for the programme scholarship?

No separate scholarship applications are required. Students will automatically be considered for various types of the scholarships based on their academic merits and services.

Can I apply for accommodation in the University?

The Programme Office will inform the students with hostel details in due course.

How can I contact the ESGM Programme Office?

All enquires can be directed to:

Lingnan University
Tuen Mun, Hong Kong

Fax: (852) 2616 5021

Master of Science in Finance

MScFin Programme

The Master of Science (MSc) in Finance is offered by the Department of Finance at Lingnan University. The MSc in Finance Degree will provide a broad coverage of the major areas in finance, with an emphasis on fundamental principles and professional practice. As such, the Degree will cover asset pricing, commercial and investment banking, corporate finance, financial engineering, insurance, investments, risk management, and wealth management.

Special Features
The Programme is ideal for those students who have a strong desire to expand their knowledge of finance and are keen to work in the finance industry in Hong Kong, Mainland China, and internationally.
The Programme will emphasize the important concepts, skills, and theoretical and institutional underpinnings of finance in Term 1 and more advanced topics and practical aspects in Term 2.
The Programme will provide a comprehensive education for students who wish to attempt the major professional qualifications in finance such as the CFA, CIIA, FRM, and CII designations

Learning Outcomes
Demonstrate knowledge of the functions and operation of financial institutions and of how financial services benefit firms and individuals;
Analyse the risk and return profiles of different investments and devise suitable investment strategies;
Demonstrate mastery of quantitative skills in applying financial models and performing financial analysis and valuation;
Develop and apply risk management techniques and financial products to control exposure to risk in a global capital market; and
Explain how compliance to regulation and ethical standards in the business environment are implemented by financial institutions.

---

Admission Requirements

Master of Science in Finance

General qualifications
Hold a relevant bachelor’s degree awarded by a tertiary education institution recognised for this purpose by the University; or
Have obtained an equivalent qualification; or
Have provided satisfactory evidence of academic and professional attainment.

Language requirements
An applicant whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain:
A minimum score of 79 (internet-based test) in the Test of English as a Foreign Language (TOEFL)*, or
A band score of 6.0 or above in the International English Language Testing System (IELTS) * , or
A minimum score of 450 in CET6 (obtained within five years), plus proof of spoken English competency, or
An equivalent score in a recognised test or an equivalent qualification to prove his/her English language proficiency which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

* For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are conducted at a test entre and within the validity period, viz. 2 years, from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort.

Remarks
A recent GMAT or GRE test score, while not required, will be helpful in determining admittance into the Programme.

Pre-entry courses
Applicants with no or limited background in finance, accounting, and statistics in their bachelor's degree will be required to pass the below pre-entry courses prior to the start of this Programme.
1. Financial Management (18 hours)
2. Statistics (24 hours)

Master of Science in Finance (Dual Degree)

Hold a relevant bachelor’s degree awarded by a recognized tertiary institution, with a cumulative GPA of 3.0 or higher (on a 4.0 scale) .
TOEFL ibT score 80/ IELTS overall score of 6.5 or above.
A recent GMAT or GRE test score, while not required, will be helpful in determining admittance into the Programme.
An admission interview conducted by LU may be required.
Applicants with no background in accounting, finance and statistics in their first degree (or equivalent academic qualification) will have to complete and pass a pre-entry course.

About Us

Our Focus
The degree is ideal for those students who have a strong desire to expand their knowledge of finance and are keen to work in the finance industry in Hong Kong, Mainland China, and internationally.
The degree will emphasize the important concepts, skills, and theoretical and institutional underpinnings of finance in Term 1 and more advanced topics and practical aspects in Term 2.
The degree will provide a comprehensive education for students who wish to attempt the major professional qualifications in finance such as the CFA, CIIA, FRM, and CII designations.
Our undergraduates in BBA in Finance have enjoyed great success in their careers in commercial and investment banking, fund and wealth management, insurance, and in other financial institutions. We fully expect that our graduates in the MSc in Finance will also be able to compete well in the job market. Non-local students have the opportunity to stay in Hong Kong to look for a job upon graduation.

Our Strength
Our full-time professors have many years of experiences in teaching all aspects of finance. All staff have PhDs from major U.S., European, and Hong Kong universities and are active researchers who publish their work in the major journals. Our staff have extensive expertise in China, regional, and international financial markets and institutions.
Our staff have professional qualifications in accounting, actuarial science, financial analysis and investment, insurance, risk management, and tax. This attests to the professional relevance of our teaching and enables us to provide the best guidance to students who want to take the major professional qualifications in finance.
The MSc in Finance is a university affiliation programme of the CFA Institute, which has been acknowledged as incorporating at least 70 percent of the CFA Program Candidate Body of Knowledge (CBOK) and placing emphasis on the CFA Institute Code of Ethics and Standards of Practice within the program.
The Faculty of Business is accredited by the AACSB, the premier international accreditation agency of business schools. This attests to the quality of our programmes and teaching staff.

Our Learning Outcomes
Demonstrate knowledge of the functions and operation of financial institutions and of how financial services benefit firms and individuals.
Analyse the risk and return profiles of different investments and devise suitable investment strategies.
Demonstrate mastery of quantitative skills in applying financial models and performing financial analysis and valuation.
Develop and apply risk management techniques and financial products to control exposure to risk in a global capital market; and
Explain how compliance to regulation and ethical standards in the business environment are implemented by financial institutions.

---

Welcome to our MSc in Finance (MScFin) Programme! Our comprehensive curriculum design equips students with the necessary knowledge, skills, and practical abilities for a successful finance career. Awarded with CFA University Affiliation Programme status and taught by experienced professors and industry experts, our up-to-date programme ensures students receive cutting-edge education and tailored career support. Join us and take the first step towards a successful finance career!

programmeName: Master of Science in Human Resource and Organisational Management
summary: Welcome to MSc in Human Resource and Organisational Management (MScHROM) Programme at Lingnan University, the oldest MSc programme within our Faculty of Business!
suitableBackgrounds: We take pride in offering a curriculum that has evolved with the industry, ensuring that our graduates are equipped with both timeless principles and the latest insights in HR.

We are dedicated to innovation in our curriculum, ensuring our graduates are not only prepared for the future of work but are also positioned to define it as strategic leaders in the evolving field of HR.
We warmly welcome outstanding graduates from around the globe to apply for our programme.
We believe that your diverse academic background and global perspective will bring fresh ideas and innovation to our academic community.
An applicant who holds a bachelor’s degree in business or study Economics, Human Resource Management and Statistics at bachelor’s degree level are considered business-related degree holders.

The preparatory course list for HROM Mainstream Programme includes three courses: Human Resource Management Economics Statistics Depending on applicants’ academic backgrounds, some applicants are required to take three preparatory courses while others might be required to take one or two course(s).
All preparatory course(s) fees paid is non-refundable regardless of whether the applicants attend the course(s) or not (e.g.
Applicants may also satisfy the preparatory course requirements by taking equivalent courses from other recognised undergraduate and postgraduate programmes.
learningObjectives: We take pride in offering a curriculum that has evolved with the industry, ensuring that our graduates are equipped with both timeless principles and the latest insights in HR.

This concentration is designed to equip you with cutting-edge expertise in AI and big data applications, providing the skills necessary to transform HR functions and lead organisational digital change.

Students will also be adept at managing stakeholder expectations and making data-driven decisions that align with organisational goals, preparing them for advanced roles in HR analytics and management.
Throughout this course, students will engage in practical exercises and case studies, applying their theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios.

HRM543 New Compliance of HR Policy Aimed at equipping HR professionals with the knowledge and skills to navigate the complex legal and ethical landscapes of modern workplaces, the course covers essential topics such as the sociocultural influences on Diversity, Equity and Inclusiveness (DEI), strategic DEI approaches, data privacy laws, and the technical aspects of data security within HR systems.

Through a blend of theoretical knowledge and practical application, as well as students will engage in debates, case studies, and projects that emphasize compliance, best practices, and the integration of DEI principles into everyday HR practices.
Students will gain insights into effectively balancing technological advancements with strategic business planning and ethical governance.
Students will explore and critically examine real-life practices and problems related to people management of selected organisations, and recommend possible solutions with action plans.

Master of Science in Human Resource and Organisational Management (HROM)

Welcome to
MScHROM Programme

Welcome to MSc in Human Resource and Organisational Management (MScHROM) Programme at Lingnan University, the oldest MSc programme within our Faculty of Business!



With a rich history of academic excellence, our programme stands out for its deep-rooted commitment to advancing the field of human resource management. We take pride in offering a curriculum that has evolved with the industry, ensuring that our graduates are equipped with both timeless principles and the latest insights in HR.

Apply Here!

Online Application System

Application Start Date: 1 October 2025

Application Deadline: 30 June 2026

Admission Requirements

Click to view more

Programme Leaflet

Click to view more

WHY STUDY WITH US?

Our programme benefits from a strong alumni network and robust corporate partnerships, which provide valuable opportunities for professional growth and networking. Through these connections, students gain access to a wealth of industry expertise and career development resources, enriching their educational experience and paving the way for future success. Join us and become part of a distinguished tradition in HRM education, supported by a community that is dedicated to fostering your career advancement.

WHAT IS DIFFERENT ABOUT US?

We offer a pioneering concentration in Human Resource Analytics and Digital Transformation , the first specialised Master's of its kind in Hong Kong. This concentration is designed to equip you with cutting-edge expertise in AI and big data applications, providing the skills necessary to transform HR functions and lead organisational digital change. We are dedicated to innovation in our curriculum, ensuring our graduates are not only prepared for the future of work but are also positioned to define it as strategic leaders in the evolving field of HR.

LET'S INVEST IN YOUR EDUCATION!

We warmly welcome outstanding graduates from around the globe to apply for our programme. Regardless of your country or region, we look forward to your contribution as we explore the forefront of human resource management together. We believe that your diverse academic background and global perspective will bring fresh ideas and innovation to our academic community. Here, you will not only gain access to world-class educational resources but also forge meaningful connections with peers from various cultures, laying a strong foundation for your future international career.

Preparatory Courses

Preparatory Courses Requirement for Mainstream Programme:

The preparatory courses are designed to help applicants with non-business degrees to get ready for their formal admission to our programme.

An applicant who holds a bachelor’s degree in business or study Economics, Human Resource Management and Statistics at bachelor’s degree level are considered business-related degree holders.

The preparatory course list for HROM Mainstream Programme includes three courses:
Human Resource Management
Economics
Statistics

Depending on applicants’ academic backgrounds, some applicants are required to take three preparatory courses while others might be required to take one or two course(s). Additional tuition fees^ will be charged for each preparatory course. All preparatory course(s) fees paid is non-refundable regardless of whether the applicants attend the course(s) or not (e.g. delay of a valid student visa to attend the registration). Applicants may also satisfy the preparatory course requirements by taking equivalent courses from other recognised undergraduate and postgraduate programmes. The preparatory courses usually start about one month before the commencement of the Programme, which is around the first week of August.

^Applicant(s) who received the programme offer(s) (letter of admission) will be informed individually for the payment notice.

Preparatory Courses Requirement for HRADT Concentration

The preparatory course list for HRADT Concentration includes:
Python
All applicants will be required to attend a Python preparatory course unless they receive special approval from the Programme Director. Such approval will be based on evidence of advanced coding experience or obtainment of relevant certifications.

Human Resource Management
In addition, a Human Resource Management preparatory course will also be provided for HRADT concentration students who do not have a background in Human Resources or related fields for optional study before programme commencement.
