Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies (DAIS)

Programme Booklet

Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies launched by the School of Data Science of Lingnan University is specifically designed to promote the deep integration of artificial intelligence technology and industry. The programme is committed to cultivating liberal arts-type science and technology leaders with global vision and tech-empowered mindset who will lead the frontier of AI technology and drive the industry transformation and upgrading.

The programme aims to enhance students' depth of knowledge and strategic thinking abilities by exploring cutting-edge AI technologies and core knowledge, enabling them to adapt flexibly to the rapidly changing technological development trends and lead the trends of the times.

Programme Overview

Programme Highlights

Curriculum

Application & Admission

Application Procedures

Admission Requirements

About Us

Academic Staff

Contact Us

Source: https://www.ln.edu.hk/sds/dai/dais/programme-overview/curriculum
Home

School of Data Science

Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies (DAIS)

Curriculum

Explore More

Programme Overview

Programme Highlights
Curriculum
Tuition Fees & Scholarships

Curriculum

Curriculum Arrangement

The DAIS programme at Lingnan University requires 57 credits for graduation, including 30 credits from coursework component (10 courses) and 27 credits from research thesis. The coursework includes four core courses, four application-driven courses, and two electives, providing a solid foundation in advanced AI technologies and methodologies. The research thesis involves producing an original thesis with checkpoints at the proposal, mid-term, and final stages.

DAIS offers both Chinese and English modes , differing in language and teaching format. Full-time students typically complete the programme in three years (up to five years), while part-time students take five years (up to seven years). This flexible structure accommodates personal and professional commitments, ensuring continuous progress through regular checkpoints.
Curriculum Outline

Graduation Requirements

For graduation, students are required to accomplish the credit requirement. The minimum CGPA requirement for graduation will be 2.67 for conferment of the DAIS programme.

In addition to fulfilling the core graduation requirements, students must achieve innovative accomplishments including software Innovation or copyright, hardware design or innovation, invention patents, scholarly publications and industry-specific projects or reports, demonstrating the deep understanding and application of artificial intelligence technology.

Apply Now

Application & Admission

Application Procedures
Admission Requirements

Admission Requirements

English-taught Mode (Suitable for Recent/Former Master's Graduates)
Interdisciplinary Pioneer : Driving cross-disciplinary research, fostering industry-academia collaboration to break technological barriers and achieve theoretical innovation.

Future Tech Leader : Visionary scholars passionate about cutting-edge technology exploration, committed to expanding global perspectives and igniting innovative thinking.

Emerging Research Vanguard : Focusing on specialized AI domains, dedicated to delivering groundbreaking research outcomes.

Chinese-taught Mode (Suitable for Professionals with 10+ Years of Work Experience)
Industry Navigator: Key strategists shaping corporate blueprints, leveraging AI to reinvent industrial ecosystems and redefine competitive edges.

Technology Innovation Vanguard: Chief architects of digital transformation, dedicated to deepening technical expertise while constructing unassailable technological moats.

AI-Industry Convergence Pioneer: Seasoned captains of traditional industries, harnessing AI to dismantle sectoral constraints and revolutionize business paradigms.

Admission Requirement for English-taught mode
General Requirements
Hold a master’s degree in STEM related disciplines awarded by a recognized tertiary education institution; or have obtained an equivalent qualification; or have provided satisfactory evidence of academic and professional attainment, which will be considered on case-by-case basis

English Language Requirement
An applicant whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain ：
a minimum score of 79 (Internet-based test) in TOEFL / 6.0 in IELTS, or equivalent.*

1 For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are conducted at a test centre and within the validity period, viz. 2 years, from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort.

Admission Requirement for Chinese-taught mode*
General Requirements
Hold a master’s degree in STEM related disciplines awarded by a recognized tertiary education institution; or have obtained an equivalent qualification; or have provided satisfactory evidence of academic and professional attainment, which will be considered on case-by-case basis.

At least 10 years of work experience, with no less than 5 years of senior level experience.

Demonstrate proficient Chinese language skills through but not limited to one of the followings: previous academic fulfilment in a Chinese-taught programme, relevant work experience in a Chinese environment, or completion of recognized Chinese language courses or certifications

*Subject to change

Programme Highlights

The Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies (DAIS) programme at Lingnan University is a pioneering, self-financed initiative designed to cultivate globally minded and innovative technology leaders. This programme integrates a robust theoretical foundation with practical applications and cutting-edge research, providing students with a comprehensive understanding of AI and its applications.

Programme Core Concepts
Knowledge : Immerse in cutting-edge AI knowledge.
Vision : Overview the AI technology developments and achievements.
Practice : AI for industrial transformation and upgrading.

Programme Aims
Enhance Ability & Widen Vision​:
√ Independent Research​
√ Innovative Applications​
√ Strategic Thinking

Integration of Liberal Arts Education:
√ Critical Thinking
√ Humanistic Literacy
√ Social Responsibility

Cultivating Multidimensional Talents:
√ Original Research
√ Innovation & Initiatation
√ Intellectual Leadership
√ Liberal Arts & Tech

Application Procedures
Admission Requirements

Application Procedures

Application Period for AY2025/26

For the 2025 intake, the application is currently available .
Applications will be processed on a rolling basis until all places have been filled. Therefore, early applications are strongly encouraged.

Application Requirements

All applications must be submitted online along with the necessary supporting documents via the Lingnan University's Online Application System .

Applicants are required to upload:

CV

Graduation Certificate & Degree Certificate

Official Transcript

CHSI Online Verification Report of Higher Education Degree Certificate (for degree obtained in mainland China)

CSCSE verification report (for degree obtained overseas)

Personal Statement

Research Proposal

Recommendation Letter

Proof of Work (if any)

English Test Report (for English-taught mode)

For details, please refer to the Online Application System.

An application is valid and accepted only when the payment procedure is successfully completed. An acknowledgement email with an assigned application number will be sent to the applicant upon each successful online payment transaction.

Application Fee

The application fee for the Doctor of Artificial Intelligence Studies is HKD600.

programmeName: Master of Research in Advanced Data Science
summary: MResADS offers a research-led and industry-oriented curriculum. Students gain advanced knowledge and practical experience in cutting-edge research and innovation, preparing graduates to lead in both academic and industrial settings.
suitableBackgrounds: Source: https://www.ln.edu.hk/sgs/programmes-on-offer/master-of-research-in-advanced-data-science Home School of Graduate Studies Programmes on Offer Master of Research in Advanced Data Science Application Period: 1 Oct 2025 - 30 Jun 2026 --- Source: https://www.ln.edu.hk/sds/dai/ads Master of Research in Advanced Data Science (MResADS) MResADS offers a research-led and industry-oriented curriculum.
Students gain advanced knowledge and practical experience in cutting-edge research and innovation, preparing graduates to lead in both academic and industrial settings.

Research Methods in Data Science (3 credits) This course is an advanced course tailored to meet the increasing demands of research postgraduate students from Lingnan University embarking on research journey in the realm of data science and interdisciplinary research.

Students will be required to carry out independent work on a major project, which can be theorical or practical, under the supervision of individual staff member and another co-supervisor from industry.

Cloud Computing (3 credits) With the rapid development of cloud resources, majority of traditional data centres has been replaced by cloud platforms (such as Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform) due to the limited bandwidth of resource.

Applicants with no or limited background in computer science or statistics will be required to complete non-credit-bearing pre-entry course(s): Introduction to Computing Statistics Student Progression Students are required to achieve a Cumulative GPA (CGPA) of 2.50 or above in order to proceed to the next term.
Students will be awarded a Postgraduate Diploma if they cannot meet the requirement(s) for graduation with a M.

Specifically, to qualify for the award of the Postgraduate Diploma in Data Science issued by Lingnan University, students must complete any 4 required courses with a cumulative GPA that is equal to or greater than 2.33.
learningObjectives: --- Source: https://www.ln.edu.hk/sds/dai/ads/peogramme-overview/course-descriptions Home School of Data Science Master of Research in Advanced Data Science (MResADS) Course Descriptions Explore More Programme Overview Programme Highlights Aims and Learning Outcomes Curriculum Course Descriptions Course Descriptions Required Courses (5 courses) 1.
Additionally, students will develop quantitative analytical skills to interpret data mining models.

By the end of the course, students will have a comprehensive understanding of the theory and practice of data mining and will be equipped with the necessary skills to extract valuable insights from databases.

Students will be required to carry out independent work on a major project, which can be theorical or practical, under the supervision of individual staff member and another co-supervisor from industry.
Under the guidance of a faculty supervisor, students will identify a research question, design and implement a rigorous methodology, analyse results, and present findings in a comprehensive thesis.
Throughout the course, students will develop crucial research skills, including literature review, experimental design, data collection and analysis, and academic writing.

A qualified data scientist needs to equip with cloud computing skills, learning to perform a series of tasks in the data pipeline on the cloud such as data acquisition, data cleansing, data transformation and data mining, as well as model training and testing.
This course aims to examine the latest trends of cloud computing and provide students with the fundamental knowledge of cloud computing.
coreCourses: Research Methods in Data Science (3 credits)
Cut across the core disciplines of AI and Data Science

courseDescriptions.courseName: Research Methods in Data Science (3 credits)

courseDescriptions.description: This course focuses on fundamental concepts, techniques and potential business applications of artificial intelligence. The course provides an overview of waves of AI, intelligent agents, problem solving, planning, reasoning, and learning. It includes topics about search, logic, genetic algorithms, and computational learning and some potential business applications like expert systems, news analysis and so on.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 本课程重点介绍人工智能的基本概念、技术和潜在的商业应用。该课程概述了人工智能、智能代理、问题解决、规划、推理和学习的浪潮。它包括有关搜索、逻辑、遗传算法和计算学习的主题以及一些潜在的商业应用，如专家系统、新闻分析等。

studentPerspectives.value: 这门课的价值不只是“学过”，而是能否产出一个可解释的课程项目、数据分析案例或 AI 应用思路。
studentPerspectives.suggestedFocus: 优先把课程作业整理成简历项目，说明问题、方法、工具、结果和个人贡献。

studentPerspectives.value: 如果基础较弱，不要一开始追求所有技术细节，先补核心概念、常见工具和基础数据/编程能力。
studentPerspectives.suggestedFocus: 课前补关键词、基础概念和入门工具；课中重点记录自己不熟悉的前置知识。

studentPerspectives.profile: 有工作经验或管理背景的学生
studentPerspectives.value: 重点不一定是转码，而是理解技术如何改变业务流程、组织决策、风险管理和行业判断。
studentPerspectives.suggestedFocus: 把课程案例映射到自己熟悉的行业场景，思考管理问题、组织落地和风险边界。

preparationAdvice: 提前熟悉 Python、Excel/表格处理、基础统计或课程涉及的软件环境。
先了解机器学习、生成式 AI、模型评估和 AI 伦理的基本概念。
复习基本商业分析、市场/运营/管理问题拆解方式。
正式选课前仍需核对官网、课程大纲和项目办公室最新说明。

Source: https://www.ln.edu.hk/sds/dai/ads
Master of Research in Advanced Data Science (MResADS)

MResADS offers a research-led and industry-oriented curriculum. Students gain advanced knowledge and practical experience in cutting-edge research and innovation, preparing graduates to lead in both academic and industrial settings.

---

Source: https://www.ln.edu.hk/sds/dai/ads/peogramme-overview/course-descriptions
Home

School of Data Science

Master of Research in Advanced Data Science (MResADS)

Course Descriptions

Explore More

Programme Overview

Programme Highlights
Aims and Learning Outcomes
Curriculum
Course Descriptions

Course Descriptions

Required Courses (5 courses)

1. Statistics for Data Science (3 credits)

This course covers statistical techniques for inferential statistics, such as estimation and hypothesis testing for population parameters. The topics covered include point estimate and interval estimate of population parameters, comparing means, non-parametric techniques and the use of statistical software for data science.

2. Database Management (3 credits)

This course introduces students the fundamental concepts and principles of databases and data warehousing. Various types of databases will be covered in this course, such as objected-oriented, relational, document-oriented, graph, NoSQL, and New SQL. Popular database management systems such as Microsoft SQL Server and/or Oracle will be described and implemented. Topics include data models (ER, relational, and others); query language (Structured Query Language); implementation techniques of database management systems (index structures and query processing); management of semi-structured and complex data; distributed and NoSQL databases; the dimensional modelling technique for designing a data warehouse, and data warehouse architectures, OLAP and the project planning aspects in building a data warehouse.

3. Data Mining (3 credits)

This course teaches the core principles and ideas of data mining. It covers a range of data mining approaches used to extract knowledge from vast amounts of valuable databases in diverse fields such as business, finance, urban planning, and medicine. Additionally, students will develop quantitative analytical skills to interpret data mining models. The course also covers current IT skills that are essential for working with large databases, such as database management and programming. By the end of the course, students will have a comprehensive understanding of the theory and practice of data mining and will be equipped with the necessary skills to extract valuable insights from databases.

4. Practical Application of Deep Learning (3 credits)

This course aims at providing an intensive understanding and hands-on experience of the existing deep learning approaches. The topics will cover how to select deep neural networks, how to design deep neural networks, and how to train and optimize the neural networks for practical applications. The course will cover deep neural network models including convolutional neural networks, recurrent neural networks, long short-term memory networks, deep residual networks, generative adversarial networks, attention-based models, adversarial learning models and training techniques including dropout, batch normalization, selection of activation functions and so on. TensorFlow, Pytorch or other state-of-the-art deep learning tools will be introduced and applied to solve different classes of problems with huge datasets in business domains.

5. Machine Learning for Business (3 credits)

This course covers the concepts, techniques, and business applications of machine learning. It will cover the supervised, semi-supervised, unsupervised, transfer, and reinforcement learning paradigms. The techniques include regression, gradient descent, probability generative model, logistic regression, neural networks, support vector machine, backpropagation, principal component analysis, word embedding, q-learning, policy gradient and so on. The business application examples of machine learning will be included in this course.

6. Research Methods in Data Science (3 credits)

This course is an advanced course tailored to meet the increasing demands of research postgraduate students from Lingnan University embarking on research journey in the realm of data science and interdisciplinary research. This course will delve into the foundational principles, methodologies, and tools used in data science research, providing students with the skills and knowledge needed to plan, design, execute, and evaluate data-driven research projects effectively.

7. Advanced Data Science Project (6 credits)

This course provides students the chance to demonstrate innovative abilities and initiatives in data science problems. Students will be required to carry out independent work on a major project, which can be theorical or practical, under the supervision of individual staff member and another co-supervisor from industry. The course develops the capability to integrate and apply data science knowledge and data analytical skills to different scenarios. The course also serves as a platform of presenting and sharing novel investigations of academic and/or industrial problems in real-world via data science knowledge.

8. Thesis (15 credits)

This course offers students the opportunity to conduct research-oriented study in an area of advanced data science under supervision of faculty members, allowing students to deeply engage with cutting-edge topics and contribute new knowledge to the field. Under the guidance of a faculty supervisor, students will identify a research question, design and implement a rigorous methodology, analyse results, and present findings in a comprehensive thesis. The course emphasizes the application of advanced data science techniques to solve complex, real-world problems or explore theoretical advancements. Throughout the course, students will develop crucial research skills, including literature review, experimental design, data collection and analysis, and academic writing. Regular progress presentations and peer reviews will hone students' ability to communicate complex ideas effectively.

Elective Courses (Any 2 courses from the followings, 6 credits)*
*The offering of elective courses is subject to sufficient demand and faculty availability.

1. Introduction to Cyber Security (3 credits)

This course introduces fundamental concepts and design principles in cybersecurity as well as highlight different methodologies of protecting information and data in the cyber world. Topics include CIA (Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability); introduction to security; cyber-attacks and threats; cryptographic algorithms and applications; network security and infrastructure.

2. Cloud Computing (3 credits)

With the rapid development of cloud resources, majority of traditional data centres has been replaced by cloud platforms (such as Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform) due to the limited bandwidth of resource. A qualified data scientist needs to equip with cloud computing skills, learning to perform a series of tasks in the data pipeline on the cloud such as data acquisition, data cleansing, data transformation and data mining, as well as model training and testing. This course aims to examine the latest trends of cloud computing and provide students with the fundamental knowledge of cloud computing. Students will learn the best practices in deploying and implementing cloud computing applicable for unique business requirements. The course discusses the conceptual topics of cloud technologies and provide hands-on experience through projects utilizing public cloud infrastructures. Topics include cloud delivery models (SaaS, PaaS, and IaaS); Cloud computing overview; Public cloud infrastructure, On-demand self-service and resource pooling; rapid elasticity; measured service; cloud storage architecture (data distribution, durability, consistency and redundancy); data deduplication; cloud security issues; case studies of current cloud computing platforms.

3. Natural Language Processing (3 credits)

This course is an introduction to Natural Language Processing. It covers a brief overview of the field, including the cutting-edge text processing tasks (e.g., text summarization, named entity recognition, document classification, etc.), their computational problem setting and general thoughts of methodologies. State-of-the-art techniques will also be discussed, including generative sequence-to-sequence models, multimodal data modelling (e.g., image-to-text, video/audio-to-text), chatbot, question-answering system, topic modelling, etc.

4. Computer Vision (3 credits)

This course will introduce the basic techniques for image data processing and analysis. Topics include image processing and analysis in spatial and frequency domains, image restoration and compression, image segmentation and registration, morphological image processing, representation and description, feature description, face recognition, iris recognition, fingerprint recognition, image analysis topics, such as medical image analysis.

5. Operation Management (3 credits)

This course introduces operations management in real-world situations (e.g., manufacturing and service industries). Students will learn how to design, operate, and improve processes to increase efficiency and effectiveness. The course covers key topics such as process design, capacity planning, inventory management, quality control, and supply chain management. Students will learn the importance of operations management, as well as the various techniques and strategies used to optimize processes and improve organizational performance.

6. Data Visualization (3 credits)

This course introduces students with data visualization techniques. It introduces visualization techniques for data in everyday life such as business, scientific computing, social media, medical imaging, etc. Such data can include CT/MRI data, graphs and networks, time-series data, text and documents, Twitter data, and spatio-temporal data, etc.

7. Social Computing (3 credits)

This course introduces social analytics techniques such as (social) information network analysis and engineering. Students will learn both mathematical and programming knowledge for analysing the structures of typical social information networks (e.g. Facebook and Twitter). They will also learn how to use the social metrics to evaluate the quality and structure of social network. It will cover topics such as small world phenomenon; contagion tipping and influence in networks; models of network formation and evolution; the web graph and PageRank; social graphs and community detection; measuring centrality; greedy routing and navigations in networks; introduction to game theory and strategic behaviour; social engineering; and principles of computer system design.

8. Mobile Edge Computing (3 credits)

This course provides a comprehensive introduction of Mobile Edge Computing (MEC), a key enabler of 5G networks. The covered topics may include communication and computation basics for MEC, computation offloading, communication and computation resource management, edge caching, MEC application scenarios (e.g., Internet of Things, AR/VR), MEC hardware platforms and standardization. Edge AI is treated as a key application case, which is to support AI applications on resource-constrained devices with the help of MEC servers.

9. Foundation of Artificial Intelligence (3 credits)

This course focuses on fundamental concepts, techniques and potential business applications of artificial intelligence. The course provides an overview of waves of AI, intelligent agents, problem solving, planning, reasoning, and learning. It includes topics about search, logic, genetic algorithms, and computational learning and some potential business applications like expert systems, news analysis and so on.

10. Big Data Analytics (3 credits)

This course provides an understanding of the concept and challenge of big data. The focus is on the data analytic techniques to tackle the V’s (volume, velocity, variety, veracity, valence, and value) in big data and how these impacts data collection, monitoring, storage, analysis and reporting. Apache Hadoop and Spark are examples of a big data management system to manage and process large-scale data. The course covers topics across the big data domain, such as distributed file systems; similarity search techniques and multimodal data processing. Big data analytics applications in data science will be elaborated. Students will actively participate in the delivery of this course through assignments, portfolio development, and projects.

11. Blockchain (3 credits)

Blockchain, as a decentralized open ledger, has proven to be a phenomenal success. This ground-breaking technique holds a huge promise in various fields, digital identification, data marketing, cryptocurrencies like bitcoin, etc. This course introduces students the fundamentals of blockchain, distributed ledger technology, alternative consensus, smart contracts and security, and cryptocurrencies. Case studies of cryptocurrencies and examples of application (e.g., Bitcoin) will be also elaborated. Students will understand the impact of blockchain technologies on financial services and other industries through assignments and projects.

12. Healthcare Analytics (3 credits)

This course aims to introduce students the power of healthcare analytics, which can enhance the traditional medical system in multiple ways by improving its efficiency, convenience, and personalization. Students will learn about key technologies that underpin smart healthcare, including the Internet of Things (IoT), which enables the collection and transmission of data from various wearable sensors. In addition, students will be exposed to data fusion techniques that integrate healthcare data from multiple sources. The course will delve into data models, data mining, and analytics techniques relevant to forecasting. Practical case studies and examples of application will be provided, helping students to understand how these technologies are being used in practice to improve healthcare.

---

Programme Overview

Programme Highlights
Aims and Learning Outcomes
Curriculum
Course Descriptions

Curriculum

The total credit requirement for the MResADS programme is 45 credits, consisting of eight required courses and two elective courses. These courses can have different combinations of lectures, laboratory sessions, tutorials, and/or seminars. Although the normal duration for the MResADS programme is one and a half year of full-time study, students may extend this up to a maximum duration of three years, subject to the approval of the Programme Director and the Person-in-Charge of the Division.

Programme Curriculum
Required Courses (39 credits)

1. Statistics for Data Science (3 credits)

2. Database Management (3 credits)

3. Data Mining (3 credits)

4. Practical Application of Deep Learning (3 credits)

5. Machine Learning for Business (3 credits)

6. Research Methods in Data Science (3 credits)

7. Advanced Data Science Project (6 credits)

8. Thesis (15 credits)

Elective Courses (Any 2 courses from the followings, 6 credits)*

1. Introduction to Cyber Security (3 credits)

2. Cloud Computing (3 credits)

3. Natural Language Processing (3 credits)

4. Computer Vision (3 credits)

5. Operation Management (3 credits)

6. Data Visualization (3 credits)

7. Social Computing (3 credits)

8. Mobile Edge Computing (3 credits)

9. Foundation of Artificial Intelligence (3 credits)

10. Big Data Analytics (3 credits)

11. Blockchain (3 credits)

12. Healthcare Analytics (3 credits)

*The offering of elective courses is subject to sufficient demand and faculty availability.

Applicants with no or limited background in computer science or statistics will be required to complete non-credit-bearing pre-entry course(s):
Introduction to Computing

Statistics

Student Progression
Students are required to achieve a Cumulative GPA (CGPA) of 2.50 or above in order to proceed to the next term. Students who cannot meet such requirement will normally be put on academic probation in the following term or discontinued.

Graduation
For graduation, students are required to complete a total of 10 courses of 45 credits, as specified in the programme curriculum, including eight required courses and three elective courses. The minimum CGPA requirement for graduation will be 2.67 for conferment of the MResADS programme. Students will be awarded a Postgraduate Diploma if they cannot meet the requirement(s) for graduation with a M. Res. degree. Specifically, to qualify for the award of the Postgraduate Diploma in Data Science issued by Lingnan University, students must complete any 4 required courses with a cumulative GPA that is equal to or greater than 2.33.

Admission Requirements

To be eligible for entry into this MResADS programme, candidates should:
hold a degree of Bachelor of Engineering, Science or other related disciplines, with first- or second-class honours from a recognised tertiary institution; or
have equivalent qualifications and experience, which will be considered on case-by-case basis.

English Language Requirements
In addition to the above, an applicant whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution, is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain:
a minimum score of 79 in TOEFL Internet-Based Test 1 ; or
a band score of 6.0 or above in IELTS 1 ; or
a minimum score of 450 or above in College English Test Band 6 (CET-6), with proof of competency in spoken English 2 ; or
an equivalent score in a recognised test; or
an equivalent qualification to prove his/her English language proficiency, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

1 For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purposes, the University accepts only results from a single test sitting. The test must be conducted at a recognised test centre and fall within the two-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort.

2 For CET-6 results submitted for admission purposes, the University accepts only those obtained within the five-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort. Applicants must also demonstrate competency in spoken English through an oral interview conducted by the Programme and/or obtain grade B or above in the CET-6 Speaking English Test.

Aims and Learning Outcomes

Explore More

Programme Overview

Programme Highlights
Aims and Learning Outcomes
Curriculum
Course Descriptions

Aims and Learning Outcomes

The MResADS programme aims to:
Provide students with advanced knowledge and research skills in data science, encompassing state-of-the-art methodologies, emerging technologies, and innovative applications.
Foster a deep understanding of the theoretical foundations underlying advanced data science techniques, enabling students to contribute to the development of new methodologies and algorithms.
Cultivate strong research capabilities through rigorous coursework, supervised dissertation projects, and engagement with faculty-led research initiatives.
Bridge the gap between academic research and industry application through a unique industry attachment model that combines research rigor with real-world problem-solving.
Develop students' critical thinking and ethical reasoning skills, ensuring responsible and impactful applications of advanced data science techniques.
Prepare students for leadership roles in both academic and industrial settings, equipping them with the skills to drive innovation and guide data-driven decision-making at the highest levels.

Programme Intended Learning Outcomes (PILOs)
On completing the MResADS programme, students will be able to:
Critically evaluate and synthesise the fundamental principles and methodologies of advanced data science and artificial intelligence (AI), demonstrating the potential to contribute to research and development in these fields.
Analyse and apply contemporary AI, data analytics, and other information technology techniques for managing, mining, analysing and visualising data across multiple disciplines.
Design, implement, deploy, and manage the solutions for real-world problems using cutting-edge data science and AI methodologies.
Develop critical thinking and problem-solving skills to identify appropriate research problems, data science methods, tools, and algorithms for specific real-world problems and to evaluate their performance and limitations in different scenarios.
Evaluate and apply ethical principles and responsible practices in the use of data science methods and tools, ensuring security, privacy, fairness, transparency, and accountability in data collection, analysis, and decision-making processes.
Synthesise and communicate complex findings effectively to diverse stakeholders, contributing to the advancement of research fields and demonstrating leadership in interdisciplinary and collaborative environments.

---

Programme Overview

Programme Highlights
Aims and Learning Outcomes
Curriculum
Course Descriptions

Programme Highlights

Cut across the core disciplines of AI and Data Science

Integrates diverse disciplines to expose students to know all the critical aspects of data science and how it is used in the real world. It delivers high-value education through a comprehensive 1.5-year program, making advanced research training accessible to aspiring scholars.

Selective admission and individual supervision

Maintains a selective intake to ensure favourable student-to-faculty ratios, fostering close academic supervision and individual mentoring. Each student is assigned a faculty member and completes their study and thesis under supervision.

Research-centric curriculum for nurturing future scholars

Features a mandatory research methodology course, advanced data science project, and substantial dissertation requirement, providing essential experience through systematic research training and rigorous academic writing.

Industry-academic bridge for real-world problem solving

Offers a unique project component featuring dual supervision mechanism. Students have the chance to receive guidance from academic faculty and industry professionals, developing both scholarly research skills and innovative problem-solving abilities valued in academia.

Application Procedures

Application Period for AY2026/27

2026 intake for MResADS programme is currently open for application.

Applications will be processed on a rolling basis until all places have been filled. Therefore, early applications are strongly encouraged.
Application Procedures

All applications must be submitted online along with the necessary supporting documents via the Lingnan University's Online Application System . An application is valid and accepted only when the payment procedure is successfully completed. An acknowledgement email with an assigned application number will be sent to the applicant upon each successful online payment transaction.

Two separate confidential recommendation forms or letters should be completed by two referees and be returned to the MResADS Programme Office through any of the following methods within 3 weeks of application.

By post in a sealed envelope to following mailing address
MResADS Programme Office
Unit L, 1/F, Lingnan Hub
Lingnan University
Tuen Mun, Hong Kong

By email
Email must be sent to the Programme Office ( mresads [email protected] ) from official university/company email addresses only.
Referees with personal email addresses will not be considered.

Download Recommendation Form

Notes

Screening of an application will start upon receipt of all application materials. Shortlisted candidates may be required to attend an interview conducted by the Admission Panel. All correspondence and notifications regarding admissions matters and results will be made via email. Successful applicants are required to secure their place with the necessary payments by the given date. The offer will be given to another applicant if the payments are not received in time.

Should you have any queries or wish to update personal particulars after an application has been submitted, please email the Programme Office ( mresads [email protected] ) together with the application number and applicant’s full name.

Any false or misleading information given in the application will lead to disqualification of the applicant and any fees paid will be forfeited.

Application Fee:
HK$400

Tuition Fee:
HK$347,400 (both local and non-local students)

Pre-entry Course Fee:
HK$3,000 (subject to adjustment) for EACH pre-entry course

All fees paid are non-refundable and non-transferable no matter whether the student completes the Programme or not. The full amount is to be paid regardless of the number of courses exempted.

A deposit of HK$600 (subject to adjustment) is payable by all admitted students. The amount is used to cover the University’s graduation fee (payable upon approval for graduation).

Scholarships

Scholarships may be awarded to outstanding students on the basis of academic merit. All students admitted to the programme will automatically be the candidates for the scholarships; no application is required.
Financial Assistance

Local students can apply for Extended Non-Means-Tested Loan Scheme offered by the HKSAR Government. For eligibility and details, please visit the website of Working Family and Student Financial Assistance Agency .

programmeName: Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence and Business Analytics
summary: The Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence and Business Analytics (MScAIBA) Programme is offered and managed by Division of Artificial Intelligence , School of Data Science , Lingnan University. The normal duration for this programme is one year full-time and the maximum period of study is three years.
suitableBackgrounds: To graduate, students must complete six core subjects and four electives for a total of 30 credits.

Topics covered include: project definition and start up; project attribute estimation; planning and scheduling; resource selection and allocation, implementation; post-project evaluation; project management as a career; skills and knowledge required by professionals, including decision-making and resource allocation appropriate to project phases; integration with other disciplines, including accounting and finance.

CDS538: Cloud Computing With the rapid development of cloud resources, majority of traditional data centers has been replaced by cloud platforms (such as Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform) due to the limited bandwidth of resource.

Pre-entry Courses Applicants with no or limited background in computer science or statistics will be required to complete the corresponding pre-entry courses below: Introduction to Computing Statistics Progression For progression, students are required to achieve a Cumulative G.P.A.
Applicants must also demonstrate competency in spoken English through an oral interview conducted by the Programme and/or obtain grade B or above in the CET-6 Speaking English Test.

Graduates will be able to meet and exceed the expectations of their current and future employers by working in different roles (e.g., business analysts, data analysts, project managers, AI consultants/experts, and data scientists) for various organisations.

The programme prepares graduates (i.e., AI consultants/experts, data analysts, business analysts, data scientists, and IT professionals) for professional IT examinations and further studies at the doctoral level.
Deliver the findings and communicate with different stakeholders with a variety of diverse backgrounds.
learningObjectives: To liaise with teaching staff regarding programme matters, such as course offerings for each academic year;

To deal with all other matters related to the degree programme.

courseDescriptions.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is a new technical science that studies and develops theories, methods, techniques, and application systems for simulating and extending human intelligence. AI techniques and models have been widely employed in various domain-specific applications due to their promising performance compared to conventional methods. This course focuses on fundamental concepts, techniques, and potential business applications of artificial intelligence. The course provides an overview of waves of AI, intelligent agents, problem-solving, planning, reasoning, learning. It includes topics about search, logic, genetic algorithms, computational learning methods, and some potential business applications like expert systems, news analysis, and so on.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 人工智能（Artificial Intelligence，AI）是研究、开发模拟和扩展人类智能的理论、方法、技术和应用系统的一门新技术科学。与传统方法相比，人工智能技术和模型具有良好的性能，因此已广泛应用于各种特定领域的应用。本课程重点介绍人工智能的基本概念、技术和潜在的商业应用。该课程概述了人工智能、智能代理、问题解决、规划、推理、学习的浪潮。它包括有关搜索、逻辑、遗传算法、计算学习方法以及一些潜在的商业应用（如专家系统、新闻分析等）的主题。

courseDescriptions.description: This course is designed to describe the advanced concepts and principles of data management for business. Various types of databases will be discussed in this course, such as object-oriented, relational, document-oriented, NoSQL, and New SQL. Popular database management systems such as Microsoft SQL Server and/or Oracle will be described. Topics include data models (ER, relational, and others); query language (Structure Queries Language); management of semi-structured and complex data; NoSQL databases. It also covers the essential concepts, options, and best practices for data administration, data protection, privacy control, user security and management, and system configurations. It addresses topics about the general concepts of data disaster recovery, planning, and procedures.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 本课程旨在描述商业数据管理的高级概念和原则。本课程将讨论各种类型的数据库，例如面向对象、关系、面向文档、NoSQL 和 New SQL。将描述流行的数据库管理系统，例如 Microsoft SQL Server 和/或 Oracle。主题包括数据模型（ER、关系模型等）；查询语言（结构查询语言）；半结构化和复杂数据的管理； NoSQL 数据库。它还涵盖了数据管理、数据保护、隐私控制、用户安全和管理以及系统配置的基本概念、选项和最佳实践。它讨论了有关数据灾难恢复、规划和过程的一般概念的主题。

courseDescriptions.courseName: CDS523: Principle of Data Analytics and Programming
courseDescriptions.description: This course provides students with the knowledge of the business data analytics process as well as the fundamental principles of programming for data collection, data preprocessing, data analysis, and data visualisation. It introduces different concepts of Python programming, including the basic Python language syntax, variable declaration, basic operators, program flow and control, defining and using functions, classes, and file and operating system interface. Basic Python packages designed for data analytics will be introduced, such as Numpy, Scipy, Matplotlib, and Pandas. A number of data analytics applications in different business domains will be described.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 本课程为学生提供业务数据分析流程的知识以及数据收集、数据预处理、数据分析和数据可视化编程的基本原理。它介绍了Python编程的不同概念，包括基本的Python语言语法、变量声明、基本运算符、程序流程和控制、定义和使用函数、类以及文件和操作系统接口。将介绍为数据分析设计的基本 Python 包，例如 Numpy、Scipy、Matplotlib 和 Pandas。将描述不同业务领域中的许多数据分析应用。

courseDescriptions.description: Machine learning is a branch and one of the most popular AI techniques in recent years. Machine learning models and techniques have been widely used in many fields, such as natural language understanding, machine vision, and pattern recognition. This course will introduce the concepts, techniques, and business applications of machine learning. The course will cover the supervised, semi-supervised, unsupervised, transfer, and reinforcement learning paradigms. The techniques include regression, probability generative model, logistic regression, neural networks, support vector machine, Q-learning, and so on. The business application examples of these courses will be included and introduced in this course.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 机器学习是人工智能的一个分支，也是近年来最流行的人工智能技术之一。机器学习模型和技术已广泛应用于自然语言理解、机器视觉、模式识别等许多领域。本课程将介绍机器学习的概念、技术和商业应用。该课程将涵盖监督学习、半监督学习、无监督学习、迁移学习和强化学习范式。这些技术包括回归、概率生成模型、逻辑回归、神经网络、支持向量机、Q-学习等。本课程将包含并介绍这些课程的商业应用实例。

ORM505: Mobile Technology and Applications in eBusiness
courseDescriptions.description: This course introduces the foundation of mobile technology and the basics for developing mobile applications. The course is also designed for managers to appreciate the business value of innovations in mobile technology as well as the relevant ethical issues.

courseDescriptions.description: This course is about the fundamentals of social media for e-business and the steps involved in incorporating social technology into an e-business platform. It equips students with a comprehensive understanding of social media applications and their contribution to the formulation and corporate strategies.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 本课程介绍电子商务社交媒体的基础知识以及将社交技术融入电子商务平台所涉及的步骤。它使学生​​能够全面了解社交媒体应用及其对制定和企业战略的贡献。

courseDescriptions.description: The principles of project management, largely developed and tested on engineering projects, are being successfully applied to projects of all sizes and types within the business world. Furthermore, the role of project management in a cross section of applications such as information technology, product development, and construction is now emphasized. This course addresses the fundamental principles of project management, and the tools and techniques at our disposal to help achieve our goals. Topics covered include: project definition and start up; project attribute estimation; planning and scheduling; resource selection and allocation, implementation; post-project evaluation; project management as a career; skills and knowledge required by professionals, including decision-making and resource allocation appropriate to project phases; integration with other disciplines, including accounting and finance. The Microsoft Project software tool will be introduced for project scheduling and management.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 项目管理的原则主要是在工程项目上开发和测试的，现已成功应用于商业世界中各种规模和类型的项目。此外，现在还强调项目管理在信息技术、产品开发和施工等交叉应用领域中的作用。本课程讲述项目管理的基本原则，以及我们可以使用的帮助实现目标的工具和技术。涵盖的主题包括：项目定义和启动；项目属性估算；规划和调度；资源选择与分配、实施；项目后评估；项目管理作为一种职业；专业人员所需的技能和知识，包括适合项目阶段的决策和资源分配；与其他学科的整合，包括会计和金融。将引入 Microsoft Project 软件工具来进行项目调度和管理。

courseDescriptions.courseName: ORM515: Business Decision Making with Software
courseDescriptions.description: Organizations often need to make decisions in their best interests in different situations, and Microsoft Excel is one of the most popular software that business people use to assist their decision making. This course introduces commonly used quantitative analysis techniques that facilitate scientific and systematic decision making. Students will learn how to employ appropriate decision making techniques to obtain the best solutions for a variety of business problems, and learn about the best-practices of spreadsheet modelling for clarity and communication. Through practicing these techniques and Excel functions, students will develop analytical and computer-based problem-solving skills, which can help them improve their performance at work or in daily life.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 组织通常需要在不同情况下做出符合自身最佳利益的决策，而 Microsoft Excel 是商务人士用来协助决策的最流行软件之一。本课程介绍常用的定量分析技术，有助于科学、系统的决策。学生将学习如何采用适当的决策技术来获得各种业务问题的最佳解决方案，并了解电子表格建模的最佳实践，以实现清晰度和沟通。通过练习这些技术和 Excel 函数，学生将培养分析和基于计算机解决问题的技能，这可以帮助他们提高工作或日常生活中的表现。

courseDescriptions.description: In an optimization problem, one seeks to minimise or maximise an objective function or a number of objective functions with real, integer, and/or discrete variables, subject to constraints on the variables. Optimization refers to the study of these problems, their properties, the development and implementation of algorithms to solve these problems, and the application of these algorithms to real-world problems. In this course, advanced artificial intelligence algorithms such as multi/many objectives optimization algorithms, genetic algorithms, evolution strategies, ant colony optimization, particle swarm algorithms, differential evolution, and other meta-heuristic methods will be discussed. These methods are able to find optimal or near-optimal solutions for challenging optimization problems. This course will also describe some real-world applications that use these algorithms to handle difficult real-world problems.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 在优化问题中，人们寻求最小化或最大化一个或多个具有实数、整数和/或离散变量的目标函数，并受到变量的约束。优化是指研究这些问题及其性质，开发和实现解决这些问题的算法，以及将这些算法应用于现实世界的问题。在本课程中，将讨论先进的人工智能算法，例如多/多目标优化算法、遗传算法、进化策略、蚁群优化、粒子群算法、差分进化和其他元启发式方法。这些方法能够为具有挑战性的优化问题找到最佳或接近最佳的解决方案。本课程还将描述一些使用这些算法来处理现实世界中困难问题的实际应用程序。

courseDescriptions.courseName: CDS529: Project for Artificial Intelligence and Business Analytics
courseDescriptions.description: The integrated application of AI techniques and business analytics to solve real-world problems is an essential capability in contemporary professional practice. This course provides students with the opportunity to synthesise and apply knowledge gained in earlier courses through the preparation, analysis, interpretation, reflection, and communication of data in a selected research or application context. Emphasis is placed on the planning, management, and execution of a well-defined group project of appropriate scope and complexity. Projects are undertaken in groups under the guidance of supervisors, who provide the project topics and define the problem context, objectives, and expected scope. Depending on the project, students may work with real-world or experimental data and apply appropriate methods to investigate, design, implement, and evaluate AI- and analytics-driven solutions. Typical project areas may include large language model (LLM) applications in education, business, healthcare, and social wellbeing; retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems for domain-specific knowledge support; agentic AI solutions for decision support and task automation; multimodal AI for text, image, audio, and video analysis; sentiment analysis and topic modelling for customer insight and public opinion analysis; AI-enabled recommendation and personalisation systems; computer vision applications for monitoring, detection, and quality assessment; predictive analytics for finance, operations, and risk management; AI solutions for smart cities and urban analytics; digital health analytics using physiological and behavioural data; and human-centred AI applications that enhance user experience, accessibility, and social impact.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 综合应用人工智能技术和商业分析来解决现实问题是当代专业实践的一项基本能力。本课程为学生提供了通过在选定的研究或应用环境中准备、分析、解释、反思和交流数据来综合和应用在早期课程中获得的知识的机会。重点放在适当范围和复杂性的明确定义的小组项目的规划、管理和执行上。项目在主管的指导下以小组形式进行，主管提供项目主题并定义问题背景、目标和预期范围。根据项目的不同，学生可以使用现实世界或实验数据，并应用适当的方法来调查、设计、实施和评估人工智能和分析驱动的解决方案。 典型的项目领域可能包括教育、商业、医疗保健和社会福祉中的大型语言模型（LLM）应用；用于特定领域知识支持的检索增强生成（RAG）系统；用于决策支持和任务自动化的代理人工智能解决方案；用于文本、图像、音频和视频分析的多模态人工智能；用于客户洞察和舆情分析的情感分析和主题建模；支持人工智能的推荐和个性化系统；用于监控、检测和质量评估的计算机视觉应用；财务、运营和风险管理的预测分析；智慧城市和城市分析的人工智能解决方案；使用生理和行为数据进行数字健康分析；以及以人为本的人工智能应用程序，增强用户体验、可访问性和社会影响。

courseDescriptions.description: This course is an introduction to Natural Language Processing (NLP). It covers a brief overview of the field, including the cutting-edge text processing tasks (e.g., text summarization, named entity recognition, document classification, etc.), their computational problem setting and general thoughts of methodologies. State-of-the-art techniques will also be discussed, including generative sequence-to-sequence models, multimodal data modelling (e.g., image-to-text, video/audio-to-text), chatbot, question-answering system, topic modelling, etc.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 本课程介绍自然语言处理（NLP）。它涵盖了该领域的简要概述，包括前沿的文本处理任务（例如文本摘要、命名实体识别、文档分类等）、其计算问题设置和方法论的一般思想。还将讨论最先进的技术，包括生成序列到序列模型、多模态数据建模（例如图像到文本、视频/音频到文本）、聊天机器人、问答系统、主题建模等。

courseDescriptions.courseName: CDS547: Introduction to Large Language Models
courseDescriptions.description: This course provides an in-depth exploration of large language models (LLMs), focusing on their principles, architectures, training methodologies, and applications. Students will learn about the evolution of LLMs, from early models to state-of-the-art systems like GPT-4. The course covers key concepts such as natural language processing, transfer learning, and ethical considerations in AI. Through hands-on projects and case studies, students will gain practical experience in developing and deploying LLMs across various domains.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 本课程深入探讨大型语言模型 (LLM)，重点关注其原理、架构、训练方法和应用。学生将了解法学硕士的演变，从早期模型到 GPT-4 等最先进的系统。该课程涵盖了自然语言处理、迁移学习和人工智能中的道德考虑等关键概念。通过实践项目和案例研究，学生将获得跨各个领域开发和部署法学硕士的实践经验。

courseDescriptions.courseName: CDS550: Programming with Generative Artificial Intelligence
courseDescriptions.description: This course explores the integration of generative AI tools in learning and applying programming skills. Students will discover how to leverage state-of-the-art generative models to enhance their coding proficiency and develop innovative applications. The curriculum covers foundational programming concepts, practical implementation strategies, and ethical considerations. Through hands-on projects, students will gain experience in using generative AI to assist with coding, debugging, and project development, preparing them for advanced programming challenges.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 本课程探讨生成式人工智能工具在学习和应用编程技能中的集成。学生将发现如何利用最先进的生成模型来提高编码能力并开发创新应用程序。该课程涵盖基础编程概念、实际实施策略和道德考虑。通过实践项目，学生将获得使用生成式人工智能协助编码、调试和项目开发的经验，为他们应对高级编程挑战做好准备。

courseDescriptions.description: This course is about AI agent and large language models (LLMs) infrastructure. Agentic AI focuses on the design, theory, and construction of autonomous, goal-driven AI agents and multi-agent systems powered by LLMs. This course will discuss fundamental concepts such as foundations of LLMs, agent architecture, agent planning and tooling, agent memory and context, reasoning loops, collaboration in multi-agent environments, and agent evaluation and benchmarking. Meanwhile, we will discuss limitations and potential risks of current LLM agents, and share insights into directions for further improvement. The course emphasizes both theoretical foundations and hands-on practical implementations, including building agents using open-sourced modern agentic frameworks.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 本课程是关于人工智能代理和大型语言模型（LLM）基础设施的。 Agentic AI 专注于由法学硕士支持的自主、目标驱动的 AI 代理和多代理系统的设计、理论和构建。本课程将讨论基本概念，例如 LLM 基础、代理架构、代理规划和工具、代理内存和上下文、推理循环、多代理环境中的协作以及代理评估和基准测试。同时，我们将讨论当前LLM代理的局限性和潜在风险，并分享进一步改进的方向。该课程强调理论基础和实践实践，包括使用开源现代代理框架构建代理。

courseDescriptions.courseName: SCI501: Geospatial Intelligence for Sustainable Development
courseDescriptions.description: The United Nations has recognised the use of geospatial data and earth observation in advancing and achieving the SDGs. The aim of this course is to learn about the geographic foundations of GIS, location intelligence and remote sensing. The contents cover how GIS and earth observation facilitate geospatial data analysis and communication to address complex geographic concepts or problems. Understanding how geospatial analytics, combined with AI and IoT technology could practically support professionals, NGO’s and governments to analyse geospatial data from multiple sources to monitor the progress of the SDGs and, empower understanding, insight, intelligent decision-making and prediction. Cutting-edge topics and applications of sustainable development will be introduced. The ethical, legal, and societal issues in the field will also be reviewed and addressed. This course combines classroom teaching and hands-on tutorials to learn GIS analytical and remote sensing skills through practice.
courseDescriptions.descriptionZh: 联合国已经认识到利用地理空间数据和地球观测来推进和实现可持续发展目标。本课程的目的是了解 GIS、位置智能和遥感的地理基础。内容涵盖 GIS 和地球观测如何促进地理空间数据分析和通信，以解决复杂的地理概念或问题。了解地理空间分析如何与人工智能和物联网技术相结合，切实支持专业人士、非政府组织和政府分析多个来源的地理空间数据，以监测可持续发展目标的进展，并增强理解、洞察、智能决策和预测。将介绍可持续发展的前沿话题和应用。该领域的道德、法律和社会问题也将得到审查和解决。本课程结合课堂教学和实践教程，通过实践学习 GIS 分析和遥感技能。

relatedStudentBackgrounds: Topics covered include: project definition and start up; project attribute estimation; planning and scheduling; resource selection and allocation, implementation; post-project evaluation; project management as a career; skills and knowledge required by professionals, including decision-making and resource allocation appropriate to project phases; integration with other disciplines, including accounting and finance.

Pre-entry Courses Applicants with no or limited background in computer science or statistics will be required to complete the corresponding pre-entry courses below: Introduction to Computing Statistics Progression For progression, students are required to achieve a Cumulative G.P.A.
Applicants must also demonstrate competency in spoken English through an oral interview conducted by the Programme and/or obtain grade B or above in the CET-6 Speaking English Test.

A diversified choice of elective subjects is offered to cater for students' interests, abilities and career plans while students may learn more advanced and practical knowledge across various domains.

skillsDeveloped: A diversified choice of elective subjects is offered to cater for students' interests, abilities and career plans while students may learn more advanced and practical knowledge across various domains.

CDS521: Foundation of Artificial Intelligence Artificial intelligence (AI) is a new technical science that studies and develops theories, methods, techniques, and application systems for simulating and extending human intelligence.

CDS523: Principle of Data Analytics and Programming This course provides students with the knowledge of the business data analytics process as well as the fundamental principles of programming for data collection, data preprocessing, data analysis, and data visualisation.
The techniques include regression, probability generative model, logistic regression, neural networks, support vector machine, Q-learning, and so on.

The course will cover deep neural network models, including convolutional neural networks, recurrent neural networks, long short-term memory networks, deep residual networks, generative adversarial networks, attention-based models, adversarial learning models, and training techniques including dropout, batch normalisation, selection of activation functions and so on.
Students will actively participate in the delivery of this course through assignments, portfolio development, and projects.
ORM505: Mobile Technology and Applications in eBusiness This course introduces the foundation of mobile technology and the basics for developing mobile applications.
It equips students with a comprehensive understanding of social media applications and their contribution to the formulation and corporate strategies.

ORM511: Project Management with Software The principles of project management, largely developed and tested on engineering projects, are being successfully applied to projects of all sizes and types within the business world.
Furthermore, the role of project management in a cross section of applications such as information technology, product development, and construction is now emphasized.
careerDirections: A diversified choice of elective subjects is offered to cater for students' interests, abilities and career plans while students may learn more advanced and practical knowledge across various domains.
The course is also designed for managers to appreciate the business value of innovations in mobile technology as well as the relevant ethical issues.

Topics covered include: project definition and start up; project attribute estimation; planning and scheduling; resource selection and allocation, implementation; post-project evaluation; project management as a career; skills and knowledge required by professionals, including decision-making and resource allocation appropriate to project phases; integration with other disciplines, including accounting and finance.

CDS529: Project for Artificial Intelligence and Business Analytics # The integrated application of AI techniques and business analytics to solve real-world problems is an essential capability in contemporary professional practice.

Understanding how geospatial analytics, combined with AI and IoT technology could practically support professionals, NGO’s and governments to analyse geospatial data from multiple sources to monitor the progress of the SDGs and, empower understanding, insight, intelligent decision-making and prediction.

Graduates will be able to meet and exceed the expectations of their current and future employers by working in different roles (e.g., business analysts, data analysts, project managers, AI consultants/experts, and data scientists) for various organisations.

The programme prepares graduates (i.e., AI consultants/experts, data analysts, business analysts, data scientists, and IT professionals) for professional IT examinations and further studies at the doctoral level.
Thereby enhancing the competitiveness of graduates in the job market.
It serves a wide range of professionals, decision and policy makers who need to process and analyse big data.

It develops students' analytical and critical thinking, as well as their problem-solving skills, which enables graduates to pursue careers as business analysts, data analysts, data scientists, and AI consultants/experts across various industries.

MScAIBA

The Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence and Business Analytics (MScAIBA) Programme is offered and managed by Division of Artificial Intelligence , School of Data Science , Lingnan University. The normal duration for this programme is one year full-time and the maximum period of study is three years.
To graduate, students must complete six core subjects and four electives for a total of 30 credits. The core courses allow students to establish a strong foundation in AI and business analytics. A diversified choice of elective subjects is offered to cater for students' interests, abilities and career plans while students may learn more advanced and practical knowledge across various domains.
