Programme Highlights

The rapid advancement in different domains such as data science, along with the growth of data and computing power, is reshaping smart city technologies. The Master of Science in Smart City Technologies and Applications programme (MScSCTA) covers key technologies in areas such as computer science and technology, artificial intelligence, and smart cities, cultivating interdisciplinary professionals.

Uniqueness of the Programme
It covers areas such as urban planning, data analytics, and sustainable development, integrating different disciplines to ensure
students understand all the important aspects of a smart city and how these concepts are applied in the real world.

It considers practical applications and frontier technologies, integrating knowledge of smart cities and various real-world analytics
with fields such as blockchain, healthcare analytics, data mining, and geographical information systems.

It serves a wide range of audiences, suitable for professionals from both the private and public sectors, including professionals
and decision-makers to navigate the complexities of smart city regulations and master urban science, informatics, and technology to
develop smart city solutions.

It develops students’ analytical and critical thinking, as well as their problem-solving skills. This enables graduates to pursue careers as
business analysts, data analysts, data scientists, and AI consultants/experts in various industries.

Student Sharing

Stories from Our Learning Journey

Mr. ZHONG
MScSCTA
Current Student, Class of 2025/26
With an undergraduate background in Civil Engineering, I began to re‑evaluate my long‑term career aspirations upon graduation. As the traditional engineering sector encounters increasing structural constraints, I questioned whether I could position myself within a more forward‑looking field offering broader development prospects.

Motivated by this reflection, I ultimately chose to pursue the Master of Science in Smart City Technologies and Applications (MScSCTA). This programme represents a pivotal opportunity for professional transformation. By integrating cutting‑edge technologies—including artificial intelligence, big data analytics, and the Internet of Things—it provides a pathway for transitioning from conventional civil engineering to the emerging domains of smart construction and smart city development.

As one of Lingnan University’s newest programmes, the MScSCTA offers robust academic resources, an innovative and interdisciplinary curriculum, and an inspiring learning environment dedicated to future‑oriented urban studies. More importantly, the programme has substantially expanded my professional horizons. It enables me to engage with high‑demand areas such as urban digitalization, intelligent transportation systems, and urban big data, thereby opening a wider range of promising career trajectories.

I look forward to further developing my competencies in this dynamic learning environment, integrating technological innovation with urban development, and contributing meaningfully to the advancement of smart city construction in the future.

Ms. YUAN
MScSCTA
Current Student, Class of 2025/26
My undergraduate training in Urban and Rural Planning, I participated in several territorial spatial planning projects at The China Academy of Urban Planning and Design, where I gained substantial hands‑on project experience. These experiences not only deepened my understanding of the operational logic behind urban development, but also sparked my strong interest in smart city development. This motivated me to pursue the Master of Science in Smart City Technologies and Applications (MScSCTA). Through this programme, I hope to integrate data science with urban planning and contribute to the sustainable development of cities.

During my undergraduate training and practical planning work, I developed a profound appreciation for the crucial role of GIS spatial analysis and data‑driven approaches in modern urban governance. I chose the MScSCTA programme because it aligns perfectly with my career aspiration to merge traditional spatial planning with advanced digital technologies. The programme covers essential subjects such as operations research, big data analytics, and artificial intelligence, enabling me to strengthen my abilities in data mining and algorithmic modelling within complex urban environments. It is equipping me to become an interdisciplinary professional capable of supporting digitalised spatial governance.

What distinguishes the MScSCTA programme is its strong emphasis on the integration of theory and practice. The curriculum not only delivers a solid theoretical foundation in data science, but also strongly encourages us to address real‑world challenges using technological solutions. Throughout my studies, I have been able to directly apply what I learned to research projects. For example, I am currently involved in a project on urban video surveillance and intelligent analysis using the VIRAT dataset, and I am leading a study on an optimal outdoor walking route planning and facility accessibility assessment system for smart campuses. Thanks to the programme’s training, the abstract of my related research has been accepted by this year’s postgraduate conference, where I have been invited to present orally. The dedicated guidance of the faculty and the programme’s forward‑looking curriculum have significantly enhanced both my academic competence and practical capabilities.

Ms. NIU
MScSCTA
Current Student, Class of 2025/26
Having completed my undergraduate studies in Information Management and Information Systems, I have developed a dual foundation in both management and data science. During my studies, I primarily conducted data‑driven research using tools such as Python and SQL, and published papers on topics related to digital currency (e‑CNY) and smart campus management. I also actively participated in various competitions to strengthen my ability to tackle real‑world problems. In addition, I led several national‑level and provincial‑level innovation and entrepreneurship projects, gaining valuable experience in teamwork and project implementation.

I have built a solid technical foundation, with proficiency in multiple programming languages and analytical software. Beyond academic and technical pursuits, I have remained committed to social welfare. I founded the “捡星星” platform in an effort to leverage technology to support children with autism. I have also participated in research projects on CIM‑based smart campuses and digital twin systems, which allowed me to build preliminary experience in the field. Nevertheless, I aspire to deepen my understanding of underlying technologies and system‑level applications.

The MScSCTA programme aligns closely with my academic background and represents an ideal pathway for my further development. As an emerging interdisciplinary field, smart city technologies holds tremendous potential for modern urban development. The School of Data Science at Lingnan University is a key strategic faculty of the institution, with particularly strong expertise in artificial intelligence. In the context of AI‑driven technological advancement, I believe this programme will expose me to valuable knowledge and resources.

The curriculum covers core subjects such as deep learning and programming, which have provided us with a robust technical grounding. The strong academic expertise of the faculty ensures that I continue to benefit greatly. Notably, the course projects offer opportunities to engage directly with real‑world datasets. For example, I had the chance to work hands‑on with ECG data processing, an experience that was both highly practical and academically valuable.

Application Procedures

Application Period for AY2026/27

For the 2026 intake, the application is currently available.

Applications will be processed on a rolling basis until all places have been filled. Therefore, early applications are strongly encouraged.

The application deadline: 30 June 2026
Application Procedures
All applications must be submitted online along with the necessary supporting documents via the Lingnan University's Online Application System . An application is valid and accepted only when the payment procedure is successfully completed. An acknowledgement email with an assigned application number will be sent to the applicant upon each successful online payment transaction.
Two separate confidential recommendation forms or letters should be completed by two referees and be returned to the MScSCTA Programme Office through any of the following methods within 3 weeks of application.

By post in a sealed envelope to following mailing address

MScSCTA Programme Office
G09, G/F, Wu Jieh Yee Lingnan Hub
Lingnan University
Tuen Mun, Hong Kong

By email

Email must be sent to the Programme Office ( [email protected] ) from official university/company email addresses only.
Referees with personal email addresses will not be considered.

Download Recommendation Form

Notes
Screening of an application will start upon receipt of all application materials. Shortlisted candidates may be required to attend an interview conducted by the Admission Panel. All correspondence and notifications regarding admissions matters and results will be made via email. Successful applicants are required to secure their place with the necessary payments by the given date. The offer will be given to another applicant if the payments are not received in time.
If you should have any queries or wish to update personal particulars after an application has been submitted, please email the Programme Office ( [email protected] ) together with the application number and applicant’s full name.
Any false or misleading information given in the application will lead to disqualification of the applicant and any fees paid will be forfeited .

Tuition Fees, Scholarships & Financial Assistance

Fees for AY2026/27
Application Fee:

HK$400

Tuition Fee:

HK$289,000 for both local and non-local students

Pre-entry Course Fee:

HK$3,000 (subject to change) for EACH pre-entry course

All fees paid are non-refundable and non-transferable no matter whether the student completes the Programme or not. The full amount is to be paid regardless of the number of courses exempted.
A deposit of HK$600 (subject to change) is payable by all admitted students. The amount is used to cover the University’s graduation fee (payable upon approval for graduation).

Scholarships
Entrance Scholarships:
Scholarships might be considered for outstanding students on the basis of academic merit. All students admitted to the programme will automatically be the candidates for the scholarships. No application is required.

Financial Assistance
Local students can apply for Extended Non-Means-Tested Loan Scheme offered by the HKSAR Government. For eligibility and details, please visit the website of Working Family and Student Financial Assistance Agency .

Double degree option available - Lingnan University (LU) & National Chengchi University (NCCU)

Programme Structure

Students are required to satisfactorily complete a total of 10 courses, including 5 Core Courses (24 credits), 4 Experiential Learning Courses (12 credits), 1 Elective Course (3 credits), and a Policy Studies Project (Individual project, 40,000-50,000 words) (33 credits).

Core Courses
Globalisation, Changing Governance and Policy Analysis

Research Methods in Policy Studies

Professional Writing Workshop

Guided Study in Policy Issues

Policy Dissemination and Strategic Communication

Experiential Learning Courses
Dialogue with Policy Practitioners: Theory and Practice

Seminar on Social Policy and Social Change

Asia Pacific Comparative Development and Policy Symposium

Engaging the Media and Public Communications

Elective Courses
Critical Issues for Culture and Heritage Management*

Cultural Policy and Practice*

China and Regional Studies: Politics, Policy, and International Development

Policy and Resource Management Issues in Health Systems

Principles of Data Analytics

Data Analytics in Health and Health Services

Contemporary Issues in Housing Management*

Gerontechnology: Design for an Ageing Society*

Understanding Metropolitan Indicators and Social Policies

Housing Policy and Development*

Internationalisation and Quality Management in Higher Education

Internationalising Education: Institutional Strategy and Development

Managing Talents and Finance in Education Institutions

Generative AI in Education: Applications and Challenges

Data Analysis and Modelling in the R Statistical Environment for Environmental Issues

Entrepreneurial Leadership for Social Startups

Innovation Management

Social Policy Analysis: Comparative Perspectives

*Subject to relevant Committee's approval.

Policy Studies Project

Student should pass all required core courses, experiential learning courses and one elective course with a cumulative G.P.A. 2.67 or above before they can formally register for the Policy Studied Project.

Progression Requirement
Achieve a CGPA of 2.50 or above in order to proceed to the next term

Doctor of Policy Studies

Career Prospects

Career Prospects

Graduates' Sharing

YIM Long Ho, Samson

The DPS programme has given me an opportunity to acquire the knowledge from various public policy areas in the global and local context, which encourages me to see things in different academic fields from an interdisciplinary perspective. It has been a wonderful learning experience for me to have gradually developed an interest on the field of higher education in Lingnan University.

I am honored to be nominated by Lingnan University to enroll in the dual degree programme with National Chengchi University in the second year. During my study in Taiwan, I have also managed to learn more about education in its entirety. Not only have I been able to understand the rationale behind the design of higher education policies, but also to be passionate about teaching.

I am grateful to have the patience and support of the professors from both universities throughout my research journey. I am also honored to have the opportunity to take part in the research program in the University of Oxford, which broadened my horizons and appreciation of higher education. Upon graduation, I hope I could contribute what I have learnt to the society, and to become a responsible global citizen to the world.

Application Procedures

To apply for the Doctor of Policy Studies programme, please follow the steps below:
Create an account in the Online Application System .
Complete the online application form.
Upload scanned copies of the following documents to the system: Passport, or any other identity document(s) of your home country;

Academic certificates and transcripts;

Proof of English language proficiency (IELTS, TOEFL), if needed;

Personal Statement; and

Other relevant materials.

Settle the application fee (HKD600) by a credit card. Application fee is non-refundable and non-transferable.
Invite two referees to complete two recommendation forms or write two recommendation letters and send the originals of completed recommendation forms/letters in a sealed envelope to Programme Team.

Entrance Requirements

For a Doctoral Degree, an applicant shall normally
• hold a relevant master’s degree awarded by a tertiary education institution recognised for this purpose by the University; or
• have obtained an equivalent qualification; or
• have provided satisfactory evidence of academic and professional attainment.

In addition to the above, an applicant whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain:
• a minimum score of 79 (internet-based test) in TOEFL 1 ; or
• a band score of 6.5 or above in IELTS (Academic) 1 ; or
• an equivalent score in a recognised test; or
• an equivalent qualification

1 For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are conducted at a test centre and within the validity period, viz. 2 years, from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort.

Programme Introduction

Doctor of Policy Studies Programme

Hong Kong’s first Doctorate in Policy Studies aims to reflect on up-to-date research and current practices in the field with an international perspective
Housing, education, ageing, health, welfare, and poverty are some of the key social issues which concern Hong Kong and all other major cities in the world. As a result, there is an increasing interest in advanced studies and analysis of relevant social policies. Therefore, as a leading liberal arts university, Lingnan has launched a Doctor of Policy Studies (DPS) programme, the first of its kind in the Greater China region.

The Doctor of Policy Studies (DPS) programme enables experienced professionals working in a range of relevant fields to develop their policy analysis expertise from international and comparative perspectives.

The programme provides an excellent learning platform with strong interdisciplinary orientation to engage students in reflecting on current research and practices in policy studies through partnerships with leading institutions in the region and overseas.

programmeName: Master of Arts in Arts and Cultural Heritage Management
summary: The focus of this module is the visitors: who they are and what they hope to gain from their experience of interacting with cultural heritage. It is designed to provide students with an opportunity to develop a critical understanding of current approaches to managing the visitor experience.
suitableBackgrounds: This course examines the above key questions, focusing on key themes and debates within exhibition-making and creative producing such as the role of curator, boundary-pushing curatorial concepts such as the AI-driven approaches, curating in and outside the museum and working with communities and other mediums and disciplines.

Courses offered at University of Lincoln (UoL), UK (60 UoL credits) Note: 5 UoL credits = 1 Lingnan credit TOU9017M Engaging Stakeholders in Cultural Heritage Development (15 UoL credits) Vibrant heritage organisations increasingly require the active participation of a range of stakeholders.

Engaged stakeholders can help museums, galleries and heritage sites to attract funding, grow audiences and facilitate negotiations around the repatriation of objects and the decolonisation of collections.

Understanding precisely who the stakeholders are – from employees, audiences and volunteers to charities, accrediting and public bodies - and the different ways in which they relate to heritage sites is crucial to understanding how the sector operates.
Also fundamental to our understanding is an appreciation of the regulatory frameworks and funding environments that shape heritage activity and with which stakeholders must interact.
The module will range over a number of country case studies and students will be encouraged to explore those related to their backgrounds.
Students will further be encouraged to reimagine strategies for more effective stakeholder engagement.

Graduation Requirements To qualify for the award of the dual MA ACHM degree of Lingnan University and University of Lincoln, students, must fulfill all the graduation requirements of the two universities.
learningObjectives: Programme Information

Students will learn critical organisational management skills, including art and culture production, venue and risk management as well as business models in working with heritage and cultural capitals.
Students will also be introduced to international, national and regional cultural heritage regulations and policies.
The module will range over a number of country case studies and students will be encouraged to explore those related to their backgrounds.
Students will further be encouraged to reimagine strategies for more effective stakeholder engagement.

Dissertation (Joint Supervision by both universities) ACH504 Dissertation: Master of Arts in Arts and Cultural Heritage Management (30 UoL credits & 6 LU credits) The dissertations and projects are intended to assess the full range of students' abilities and to apply a range of learning outcomes which the programme enables students to develop.

Students will be assigned one supervisor from the University of Lincoln, and one from Lingnan University, so that students can benefit from the expertise of staff at both institutions and maintain links with them throughout their studies.

Aims & Intended Learning Outcomes Aims of the Concentration In addition to the central aims of the MA ACHM, the Concentration content shall equip students with theoretical and empirical knowledge of heritage tourism and management, while allowing them to design a prototype to address a specific gap related to heritage tourism or conduct a research-based on a critical literature review related to innovation in the heritage tourism sector.
Equip students with fundamental knowledge to manage cultural assets and its application in Hong Kong; and 3.

Course Description

Explore More

Programme Overview

Unique Features
Programme Aims
Programme Information
Programme Structure
Course Description
Heritage Tourism and Management Concentration

Course Description

Courses offered at Lingnan University, Hong Kong (LU)

ACH501 Critical Issues for Culture and Heritage Management (3 LU credits)
The course introduces students to the politics and policies of heritage management in the urban environment. Following completion of the course, students should have a good understanding of what constitutes urban and cultural heritage, and why its conservation is so politicised. The course begins by questioning what cultural heritage is in urban contexts, and examining the pressures that processes of urbanisation and development place on existing urban landscapes and connections to place. It will also examine both academic and professional debates regarding ideas about how and why objects, buildings, landscapes, and traditions should be preserved, protected, and presented to current and future generations. The key emphasis will be on the emergence of urban heritage landscapes in Asian cities. In particular, the course will discuss the similarities and divergencies between arts heritage conservation and management in the UK context with that of Hong Kong and the Greater Bay Area. Emerging trends such as digital heritage and AI in visitor experience will be examined.

ACH502 Cultural Policy and Practice (3 LU credits)
This course will address a range of issues relevant to cultural policy and practice in Hong Kong and other Asian cities. It will discuss the relationship between cultural production and policy and deal with issues of ‘what is culture’ in different cultural contexts and countries. This course will provide students with a deeper understanding of the formation and reformation of cultural policies at the local, national, regional and international levels. The Cultural Policy and Practice will enable students to participate in the process of proposing local cultural policies for their countries and to network for the purposes of the research needed to develop policies conducive to improving the working environment in the arts and cultural sector. The course will also provide some practical, transferrable skills, such as decision-making, data management, community consultations, marketing, policy evaluation and development.

ACH503 Arts Exhibition and Performance Management​ (3 LU credits)
The human progress of civilization and technology constantly contest the idea of curatorial, the process that gives people the context in the art viewing experience. What the present holds and what the future foretold when we talk about curating an exhibition for a museum or producing a performance in the digital age. Can a community be curated? Can we have a collective panel instead of one curator? What does it mean to have a non-hierarchical artful experience? This course examines the above key questions, focusing on key themes and debates within exhibition-making and creative producing such as the role of curator, boundary-pushing curatorial concepts such as the AI-driven approaches, curating in and outside the museum and working with communities and other mediums and disciplines. Students will learn critical organisational management skills, including art and culture production, venue and risk management as well as business models in working with heritage and cultural capitals.

ACH510 History and Heritage of the Greater Bay Area (3 LU credits)
The course History and Heritage of the Greater Bay Area investigates the diverse cultural expressions, intangible heritage, historic sites and artefacts unique to the region. It analyses the intricate interplay between history and heritage, questioning the way historical interpretation informs heritage preservation and vice versa. The course provides an overview of key theoretical, practical and policy debates within the field of heritage management. While the primary focus is on the history and heritage of the Greater Bay Area (GBA), the course also incorporates a comparative perspective by examining international case studies and policy issues from other parts of Asia and the world. The concept of heritage is explored through an inter-disciplinary approach, covering heritage practices such as identification, assessment, research, preservation, interpretation, and promotion of various forms of cultural heritage. Students will also be introduced to international, national and regional cultural heritage regulations and policies.

Courses offered at University of Lincoln (UoL), UK (60 UoL credits)
Note: 5 UoL credits = 1 Lingnan credit

TOU9017M Engaging Stakeholders in Cultural Heritage Development (15 UoL credits)
Vibrant heritage organisations increasingly require the active participation of a range of stakeholders. Engaged stakeholders can help museums, galleries and heritage sites to attract funding, grow audiences and facilitate negotiations around the repatriation of objects and the decolonisation of collections. Indeed, their participation is often a necessary prerequisite to such processes. Understanding precisely who the stakeholders are – from employees, audiences and volunteers to charities, accrediting and public bodies - and the different ways in which they relate to heritage sites is crucial to understanding how the sector operates. Also fundamental to our understanding is an appreciation of the regulatory frameworks and funding environments that shape heritage activity and with which stakeholders must interact. The module will range over a number of country case studies and students will be encouraged to explore those related to their backgrounds. Students will further be encouraged to reimagine strategies for more effective stakeholder engagement.

TOU9143M The Visitor Experience at Cultural and Heritage Attractions (15 UoL credits)
The focus of this module is the visitors: who they are and what they hope to gain from their experience of interacting with cultural heritage. It is designed to provide students with an opportunity to develop a critical understanding of current approaches to managing the visitor experience.

TOU9156M Digitising Cultural and Heritage Collections (15 UoL credits)
This module is designed to address key issues in collections management, with a focus on digital collections. As well as aiming to introduce key theory and concepts, the module contains a strong practical dimension, as students have the opportunity to participate in the production of metadata, the development of a content management system and the application of a content management system to supporting exhibition development.

CON9009 Heritage Interpretation and the Marketing of Usable Pasts (15 UoL credits, optional)
Heritage interpretation is far more than information, as this module will demonstrate, Interpretation allows visitors to connect with the significance and uniqueness of heritage assets through storytelling, immersion and other techniques. This module discusses the wide range of interpretation strategies currently employed in the sector and will tutor students towards developing their own interpretation plan for an online exhibition.

Dissertation (Joint Supervision by both universities)
ACH504 Dissertation: Master of Arts in Arts and Cultural Heritage Management (30 UoL credits & 6 LU credits)
The dissertations and projects are intended to assess the full range of students' abilities and to apply a range of learning outcomes which the programme enables students to develop. In particular it enables assessment of the ability to design, develop and write an advanced research project using primary and/or secondary materials appropriate to the topic and according to the necessary conventions of scholarly work. It requires independent motivation and self-directed learning, under supervision, and enables students to demonstrate competence for the critical analysis and sustained persuasive argument. Students will be assigned one supervisor from the University of Lincoln, and one from Lingnan University, so that students can benefit from the expertise of staff at both institutions and maintain links with them throughout their studies.

Course Description
Heritage Tourism and Management Concentration

Heritage Tourism and Management Concentration

Overview
The Concentration in Heritage Tourism and Management ( 文化遺產旅遊與管理 ) (HTM) is a progressive study programme focusing on the innovative solution to the heritage tourism sector now and in the changing future. It provides a pathway for students who would prefer to gain more in-depth knowledge of heritage tourism and management in the Hong Kong context. Students who opt for this HTM concentration are required to complete all the required courses and will be awarded a single award from Lingnan University.
The Government of Hong Kong SAR has set aside resources to promote arts and culture and to develop green tourism. With the ongoing popularity of Palace Museum and M+ and the future developments of the West Kowloon Cultural District , Hong Kong’s role as an East meets West centre for international arts and cultural exchange will continue to be promoted and strengthened. As specified in the Outline Development Plan for the Guangdong Hong Kong Macao Greater Bay Area and the Culture and Tourism Development Plan for the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area (the CTD Plan), the promotion of cultural heritage and green tourism projects with Hong Kong characteristics, enhancing tourism promotion, and rolling out enticing promotional offers in a timely manner to attract tourists from outside Hong Kong can help consolidate Hong Kong’s position as a core demonstration zone for multi-destination tourism and an international tourism hub.
As a result, there is a need to prepare students with the knowledge of heritage management, tourism, and skills to tackle problems and challenges related to heritage tourism and management, along with design or research methodology to provide innovative solutions. This will be achieved by introducing the Concentration with an interdisciplinary approach, whereby the specialties of the School of Graduate Studies, Department of History, Office of Service Learning, and Science Unit collaborate in offering a unique master’s programme to enhance the heritage tourism sector in terms of visibility and ecological sustainability.

Key Features
1. An innovative curriculum: As with the MA ACHM, courses are designed to combine research with practice in heritage tourism and management, to train highly skilled professionals and critical intellectuals for the heritage tourism sector in Hong Kong.
2. Blended learning methods: High-quality teaching through a variety of mechanisms, including lectures, seminars, workshops, and group research projects with significant levels of individual tutorial support, particularly for the capstone project.
3. An interdisciplinary approach: Courses are constructed to draw insights from different disciplines, including History, Science, Tourism, Management, and Entrepreneurship.
4. Inclusion of diverse stakeholders: As engaging in real-world issues and enhancing the heritage tourism sector requires the inclusion of all stakeholders’ viewpoints, such that academics, policymakers, practitioners, entrepreneurs and NGOs will be invited to contribute to the curriculum and participate in the courses.
5. Sustainable and inclusive solutions: Courses are provided to introduce innovative solutions to heritage tourism and management with an understanding of critical issues faced in the heritage tourism sector now and in the changing future.

Aims & Intended Learning Outcomes
Aims of the Concentration
In addition to the central aims of the MA ACHM, the Concentration content shall equip students with theoretical and empirical knowledge of heritage tourism and management, while allowing them to design a prototype to address a specific gap related to heritage tourism or conduct a research-based on a critical literature review related to innovation in the heritage tourism sector. Its specific aims are to:
1. Provide students with the understanding of critical issues faced in the heritage tourism sector now and in the changing future and the tools to tackle them;
2. Equip students with fundamental knowledge to manage cultural assets and its application in Hong Kong; and
3. Enhance the heritage tourism sector in terms of visibility and ecological sustainability.
Intended Learning Outcomes of the Concentration
For those who have opted for the Concentration, upon graduation, they shall be able to:
1. Explain and assess key theories and frameworks in heritage tourism and management;
2. Apply relevant theories and knowledge to tackle problems and challenges related to heritage tourism and management now and in the changing future;
3. Design and carry out research on heritage tourism and management, and derive recommendations for innovation accordingly; and
4. Apply knowledge of heritage tourism and management in the Hong Kong context.
Careers and Skills
The Concentration will produce a new crop of talents with in-depth knowledge and skills to take up current and future opportunities emerging from the heritage tourism sector in Hong Kong. It will broaden students ’academic and experiential horizons on heritage tourism and management issues.
Career Opportunities
Tourism project management
Community project management
Art administration
Private and public cultural assets management
PhD and professional doctorate degrees in fields such as Tourism, Cultural Management, and Heritage Management and Administration

---

Programme Aims

Aims and Learning Outcomes of the Programme
Programme Aims
The MA ACHM aims to train students to develop career-driven practical and research skills in arts and cultural heritage management through the professional and technical knowledge they will obtain from the programme. Specifically, the programme seeks to help students achieve the following aims:

To enable students to build on their existing experience or interest to develop knowledge and skills within arts and cultural heritage management

To enable students to develop a critical understanding of the cross-sector and interdiscipli-nary nature of both cultural heritage organisations and the infrastructure - both state and commercial that supports them;

To provide the context for the students to develop their own thinking related to the sector in an environment where they will receive expert academic and practitioner supervision;

To enable students to both engage with the key issues of the discipline and provide them with a body of work that can be used to develop a career within the sector;

To enable students to gain experience of a work environment through exposure to relevant arts and heritage organisations, but also to be able to critically analyse the ‘culture of the organisation’ and its management practice; and

To enable students through group-based work, with colleagues from a diversity of cultures/countries to develop an understanding and practice of communication [particularly cross-cultural] and leadership skills.

Programme Intended Learning Outcomes (PILOs)
Upon completion of the MA ACHM programme, students will be able to:

Knowledge & Understanding (K&U):
Illustrate an informed critical understanding of the forms and formation of cultural heritage policy and the value of arts and heritage from an international perspective.

Analyse the complex relationship between theory and practice within the development of cultural heritage policy and its impact on cultural heritage sites in different cultural contexts.

Compare and evaluate contemporary administrative and management practice within arts and heritage organisations and their potential professional practice in relation to them

Develop skills to understand visitor volume, experience and needs, as well as associated marketing techniques.

Cognitive & Thinking Skills(C&T):
Develop a reflective approach to the development of their administrative and management practice.

Analyse political, economic, social, technological, and legal contexts in relation to developing a cultural heritage policy.

Subject-Specific Skills and Professional Behaviours and Attitudes (SSS&PBA):
Apply a management structure, principles, and planning skills to cultural heritage projects and organisations.

Work effectively as a member of a team and/or a leader in developing an administrative/management approach to a heritage project or organisation.

Engage in academic debates related to arts & cultural heritage management and learn the value and application of relevant research and theory throughout their study.

---

Programme Overview

Unique Features

Global Learning Experience
The programme, Master of Arts in Arts and Cultural Heritage Management (MA ACHM), will be delivered by a team of world-class scholars and practitioners affiliated with Lingnan University and University of Lincoln. It will also be strongly supported by M+ Museum, as well as other departments and partner institutions of Lingnan University. Students will benefit from diverse experiential learning in this programme by having internship/placements or other forms of learning activities based in Hong Kong and the UK.

Innovative Curriculum
The MA ACHM is a unique programme that offers a high-quality academic curriculum with opportunities for practicums and study abroad. It enables students to put theory into practice and applies their knowledge to solve real-world problems.

Availability of Local and International Institutional and Professional Partnerships
The combined research expertise of the two host universities, Lingnan University and University of Lincoln will provide an extensive choice of traineeship opportunities for students.

Blended Learning Approach
This programme supports students learning with high-quality teaching through a variety of mechanisms, lectures, seminars, workshops, group research projects with significant levels of individual tutorial support, particularly for dissertations.

Experienced Teaching Team
The MA ACHM will be delivered by a multidisciplinary team comprising of experienced professors, distinguished practitioners and researchers with expertise in the arts and cultural heritage sectors.

Multi-campus Learning
The programme is proud to offer a dynamic multi-campus teaching and learning experience designed to enrich students' academic journey. Students will engage with course work across stimulating environments, including the cutting-edge facilities in M+ and the vibrant Lingnan University campus. The multi-campus format reflects our commitment to a holistic and practical educational approach, ensuring that students gain a robust and well-rounded understanding of the global arts landscape.

Source: https://www.ln.edu.hk/sgs/achm/career-prospects
Home

School of Graduate Studies

Master of Arts in Arts and Cultural Heritage Management

Career Prospects

Career Prospects

The exponential growth of the art market has created a massive demand for art practitioners in Hong Kong, Mainland China, and the region. The Greater Bay Area also holds significant cultural assets that will be important to conserve for future generations. Despite this need, there is currently a sharp shortage of arts and cultural heritage practitioners. The MA ACHM programme therefore aims to train students to develop career-driven practical and research skills in arts and cultural heritage management.

The programme is suitable for students from a variety of academic and career background and is well suited for those who wish to (or currently) assume advanced management positions in the creative art and/or cultural heritage industries. It also provides a strong foundation for those hoping to pursue advanced study in:
Cultural heritage management and conservation,
Creative arts in management and cultural policy,
Leadership in arts and cultural heritage management

---

programmeName: Master of Arts in International Higher Education and Management
summary: SGS IHEM - Master of Arts in International Higher Education and Management (IHEM)
suitableBackgrounds: This Concentration is designed to equip graduates with the skills to navigate the digital era of higher education by integrating emerging technologies while emphasising digital equity and liberal arts values.

The IHEM Programme has historically attracted successful applicants from the education and related sectors who are interested in combining their existing teaching skills with digital literacy and pedagogical innovation.

Collaboration with other stakeholders The Concentration emphasises effective use and proficiency in digital tools for personalised learning, enabling students and educators to adapt to the evolving EdTech and digital landscape and lead pedagogical innovations in the teaching and learning space.

The 2023 AAC&U employer survey, which included 1,010 executives and hiring managers, found that while 8 in 10 employers believe higher education prepares graduates for the workforce, only half believe graduates are “very prepared” in critical skills such as oral and written communication, critical thinking, and complex problem-solving.
However, there was almost no gap in employers’ high estimation of the importance of “utilising technology and digital media platforms” and college graduates’ preparedness in that skill.

A recent survey “AI in the Workplace 2025” conducted by Digital Education Council identified that “the top two barriers to AI adoption in the workplace – lack of governance and training – and the opportunities this presents for higher education… and a sharp disconnect between industry and higher education: only 3% of employers believe higher education is adequately preparing graduates for an AI-driven future”.
This ensures graduates are well-prepared for the demands of the modern workforce in higher education and beyond.
Degree Classification Minimum Cumulative G.P.A.
learningObjectives: The IHEM Programme aims to:
Equip students with contemporary theoretical frameworks and international / comparative perspectives to examine international higher education, policy and governance related issues;
Develop students into critical thinkers and problem solvers who could tackle issues in international higher education management with holistic considerations;
Enable students to evaluate and inform policy debates in international higher education policy and governance;
Build and maintain a network of regional and international scholars and students committed to the study and research of international higher education, policy and governance.
Pogramme Intended Learning Outcomes
Upon successful completion of the IHEM, students will be able to:
Explain and assess key theories and frameworks in international higher education management;
Students will be notified of any such decision as soon as possible.

The IHEM Programme has historically attracted successful applicants from the education and related sectors who are interested in combining their existing teaching skills with digital literacy and pedagogical innovation.

The Educational Technology and Digital Transformation Concentration is therefore positioned to train students for the growing demand for integration of digitally skilled professionals in the Educational Technology and Digital Transformation space in international higher education and the global market.

Collaboration with other stakeholders The Concentration emphasises effective use and proficiency in digital tools for personalised learning, enabling students and educators to adapt to the evolving EdTech and digital landscape and lead pedagogical innovations in the teaching and learning space.

Alignment with industry needs (AAC&U 2023 Employer Survey, Finley, 2023) The curriculum is designed to address skills gaps identified in the latest AAC&U employer surveys, particularly focusing on critical thinking, digital literacy, and ethical reasoning in technology-driven environments.

The 2023 AAC&U employer survey, which included 1,010 executives and hiring managers, found that while 8 in 10 employers believe higher education prepares graduates for the workforce, only half believe graduates are “very prepared” in critical skills such as oral and written communication, critical thinking, and complex problem-solving.
However, there was almost no gap in employers’ high estimation of the importance of “utilising technology and digital media platforms” and college graduates’ preparedness in that skill.

A recent survey “AI in the Workplace 2025” conducted by Digital Education Council identified that “the top two barriers to AI adoption in the workplace – lack of governance and training – and the opportunities this presents for higher education… and a sharp disconnect between industry and higher education: only 3% of employers believe higher education is adequately preparing graduates for an AI-driven future”.
This highlights the importance of the EdTech Concentration in further developing these crucial digital competencies.
Equip students with knowledge and skills to design and implement context-specific, equitable EdTech strategies.

Educational Technology and Digital Transformation, EdTech Concentration

Explore More

Programme Overview

Programme Information
Unique Features
Programme Structure (For 2026-27 Intake)
Educational Technology and Digital Transformation, EdTech Concentration

Educational Technology and Digital Transformation, EdTech Concentration

The proposed Concentration in Educational Technology and Digital Transformation (hereafter, “ EdTech Concentration” or “the Concentration”) is an advanced digital literacy pathway of the existing IHEM programme of LU. This Concentration is designed to equip graduates with the skills to navigate the digital era of higher education by integrating emerging technologies while emphasising digital equity and liberal arts values. The proposed launch is effective from the 2026-2027 academic year.

The IHEM Programme has historically attracted successful applicants from the education and related sectors who are interested in combining their existing teaching skills with digital literacy and pedagogical innovation. The Educational Technology and Digital Transformation Concentration is therefore positioned to train students for the growing demand for integration of digitally skilled professionals in the Educational Technology and Digital Transformation space in international higher education and the global market.

Key Features
Interactive, technology-assisted teaching and learning
Students will learn and apply educational technologies and the use of digital tools in transforming teaching and learning through practical demonstrations, preparing them for high-demand roles in the EdTech and digital transformation space.

Interdisciplinary approach
Students will learn about EdTech and digital transformation from different perspectives, such as liberal arts, emerging technologies, digital literacy, and management. A broad range of professionals within the IHEM Faculty staff, the Teaching and Learning Centre, and other guest speakers will facilitate teaching and learning for students to gain a strong understanding of working in the EdTech and digital transformation space, which includes higher education institutions, etc.

Collaboration with other stakeholders
The Concentration emphasises effective use and proficiency in digital tools for personalised learning, enabling students and educators to adapt to the evolving EdTech and digital landscape and lead pedagogical innovations in the teaching and learning space.

Alignment with industry needs (AAC&U 2023 Employer Survey, Finley, 2023)
The curriculum is designed to address skills gaps identified in the latest AAC&U employer surveys, particularly focusing on critical thinking, digital literacy, and ethical reasoning in technology-driven environments. The 2023 AAC&U employer survey, which included 1,010 executives and hiring managers, found that while 8 in 10 employers believe higher education prepares graduates for the workforce, only half believe graduates are “very prepared” in critical skills such as oral and written communication, critical thinking, and complex problem-solving. However, there was almost no gap in employers’ high estimation of the importance of “utilising technology and digital media platforms” and college graduates’ preparedness in that skill. A recent survey “AI in the Workplace 2025” conducted by Digital Education Council identified that “the top two barriers to AI adoption in the workplace – lack of governance and training – and the opportunities this presents for higher education… and a sharp disconnect between industry and higher education: only 3% of employers believe higher education is adequately preparing graduates for an AI-driven future”. This highlights the importance of the EdTech Concentration in further developing these crucial digital competencies. This ensures graduates are well-prepared for the demands of the modern workforce in higher education and beyond.

Aims of the EdTech Concentration

Critically evaluate and synthesise EdTech policies and technologies utilising tools like generative AI, CRM systems, and analytics platforms.
Equip students with knowledge and skills to design and implement context-specific, equitable EdTech strategies.
Help students to assess and address ethical and scalability challenges in EdTech deployment.

Admission

Admission

Application Deadline for 2027 - 2028 Intake:
15 July 2026 - 30 June 2027*
* The Programme Office reserves the right to change
the application closing date without prior notice based on the actual admission situation.

Admission Requirements (AY2027/28) :
To be eligible for entry into this programme, an applicant shall normally
hold a relevant bachelor’s degree awarded by a tertiary education institution recognised for this purpose by the University; or

have obtained an equivalent qualification; or

have provided satisfactory evidence of academic and professional attainment.

English Proficiency
In addition to the above, an applicant whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution, is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain:
a minimum score of 79 (internet-based test) in TOEFL 1 ; or

a band score of 6.0 or above in IELTS 1 ; or

a minimum score of 450 or above in the College English Test Band 6 (CET-6), with proof of competency in spoken English 2 ; or

an equivalent score in a recognised test; or

an equivalent qualification

1 For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purposes, the University only accepts results from a single test sitting. The test must be conducted at a recognised test centre and fall within the two-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort.
2 For CET-6 results submitted for admission purposes, the University accepts only those obtained within the five-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort. Applicants must also demonstrate competency in spoken English through an oral interview
conducted by the Programme and/or obtain grade B or above in the CET-6 Speaking English Test.
* Interviews may be conducted if deemed necessary by the Admissions Coordinator and the Programme Director.
Tuition Fees (AY2026-27)
Tuition Fee: HK$198,000
Application Fee: HK$400

Programme Introduction

Explore More

About us

Programme Introduction
Teaching Team
Committees

Programme Introduction

Short Description of IHEM

The Master of Arts in International Higher Education and Management programme (國際高等教育與管理文學碩士課程, IHEM, hereafter) has a global approach in keeping up with the latest trends in international higher education. It combines education and management, theory and practice into one effective mix. IHEM is designed to equip students with theoretical knowledge to investigate issues concerning international higher education, policy and governance, and the management and leadership of contemporary higher education institutions. Students will achieve and practise their management skills to pursue or advance their careers in education-related fields.

If you are working or aspire to work in the management or policy-making positions in the higher education sector and other education-related organisations, IHEM will be a perfect platform for you to lay the foundations for a promising career.

Programme Aims

The IHEM Programme aims to:
Equip students with contemporary theoretical frameworks and international / comparative perspectives to examine international higher education, policy and governance related issues;

Develop students into critical thinkers and problem solvers who could tackle issues in international higher education management with holistic considerations;

Enable students to evaluate and inform policy debates in international higher education policy and governance;

Build and maintain a network of regional and international scholars and students committed to the study and research of international higher education, policy and governance.

Pogramme Intended Learning Outcomes

Upon successful completion of the IHEM, students will be able to:
Explain and assess key theories and frameworks in international higher education management;

Apply relevant theories and knowledge to tackle problems in international higher education management;

Evaluate and inform debates on international higher education and governance;

Design and carry out research related to education and management issues, and derive recommendations accordingly.

From the programme, I have learnt… 💬The programme inspired innovative thinking💡 and practical skills, equipping students with the ability to transform design concepts into implementable products or projects, and to further develop them into sustainable business operation models.
This program equipped me with both the theoretical knowledge and practical tools to drive meaningful social change.
Through dynamic coursework and real-world projects, I learned to design sustainable business models that balance social impact and financial viability.
The experience sharpened not only my strategic thinking and leadership skills but also, enabling me to pivot my career towards the social impact sector.
More importantly, with a flexible mindset, I am now confidently applying these insights to develop innovative solutions for complex social challenges.
This ability to embrace change is the program's most enduring gift.

Source: https://www.ln.edu.hk/sgs/seim/courses-description/adsi-concentration
Home

School of Graduate Studies

MA in Social Entrepreneurship and Innovation Management

ADSI Concentration

Explore More

Programme Overview

Programme Structure
ADSI Concentration
Programme Information

ADSI Concentration

The SEIM Concentration in Art and Design for Social Impact (ADSI) is designed for students who want to innovate within the creative industries sector and make a positive impact through art and culture. Students will be taught the underlying skills necessary to manage everything from the development of new methods for the presentation of exhibits in museums, to the use of technologies, such as AI, in visual advertisements and the movie industry. They will also learn how to use both innovative management techniques, and an entrepreneurial mindset, to ensure their projects have a positive social impact.

Students opting for the ADSI Concentration will take 2 courses which focus on applying design and innovation know-how in the context of both the creative industries and of intangible cultural heritage. The Interactive Arts and Technology course will provide students with hands-on experience of using digital and other technologies relevant to the creative arts and industries. The Design Methods for Arts and Culture course will teach similar problem-solving concepts and methods as the SEIM equivalent, but will look at them in the context of revitalising aspects of our intangible cultural heritage and of innovation within the creative industries.

The programme for SEIM students who choose the ADSI Concentration looks as follows:

School of Graduate Studies

MA in Social Entrepreneurship and Innovation Management

Graduate Sharing

Graduate Sharing

✨Voices of SEIM Graduates🌈✨



Students of the Master of Arts in Social Entrepreneurship and Innovation Management (SEIM) programme, offered by the School of Graduate Studies at Lingnan University, experienced a fulfilling and unforgettable academic journey during the 2024/25 academic year🎊! Our graduates 🎓 recently returned to campus for the 56th Congregation, where they shared their reflections on the SEIM programme🌻.



From the programme, I have learnt…

💬The programme inspired innovative thinking💡 and practical skills, equipping students with the ability to transform design concepts into implementable products or projects, and to further develop them into sustainable business operation models.



What attracts me most about the programme…

💬The programme emphasises practical application🌟. By integrating innovative ideas with technology, students designed smart furniture, smartphone applications and more for older adults👵🏻🧓🏼, enhancing their quality of life in meaningful ways.



The most memorable moment in the programme…

💬The care and guidance of dedicated instructors made students feel supported throughout their learning journey💖. Orientation and a variety of activities also helped students quickly become part of the SEIM family, leaving them with precious memories.

Alumni

Stories

CHENG Yu

Wealth Management Manager, Manulife

2024 - 2025 Graduate

'My journey in the Master of Social Entrepreneurship and Innovation Management (SEIM) at Lingnan University has been transformative. This program equipped me with both the theoretical knowledge and practical tools to drive meaningful social change. Through dynamic coursework and real-world projects, I learned to design sustainable business models that balance social impact and financial viability. The experience sharpened not only my strategic thinking and leadership skills but also, enabling me to pivot my career towards the social impact sector. More importantly, with a flexible mindset, I am now confidently applying these insights to develop innovative solutions for complex social challenges. Learning to pivot and adapt strategies has been a game-changer in my career, enabling me to thrive in the dynamic landscape of social innovation. This ability to embrace change is the program's most enduring gift. Highly recommended for those passionate about creating a better future! '

Andy QI

Media Company Owner

2024 - 2025 Graduate

SEIM, as an interdisciplinary graduate program bridging social sciences and business, has been a delightful surprise from the very start. During the interview, the warm demeanor of the two professors eased my nerves. After classes began in September, most sessions were held in the M+ Art Center office building adjacent to the Forbidden City. During breaks, gazing at the shimmering Victoria Harbour with a gentle breeze caressing my face, I truly felt the joy of thinking, "This tuition is worth every penny."



The subsequent three-day icebreaker camp at the Jockey Club Campground was packed with excitement. Activities like rock climbing and barbecues were interspersed with workshops and mini-lessons on design methodologies. Guided by professors, classmates from diverse backgrounds spoke freely, not only quickly building closer relationship but also gaining clear insights into the program's direction. The most striking aspect of the project-based teaching was its emphasis on practice over paperwork. We visited nonprofit organizations for real-world observation, explored commercial tech exhibitions to broaden our horizons, listened to successful entrepreneurs share practical experience, and learned tools like modeling and apps to refine business concepts. In the latter half of the semester, groups conducted in-depth field research on their chosen topics, applying the methodologies learned to complete their final projects. This immersive learning experience made every effort feel truly worthwhile.

Application Procedure

Submission of Application
Applicants may submit applications for admission through the University's Online Admission System at https://apply.ln.edu.hk/ . Upon successful submission of the application, applicants will receive an application acknowledgement by email.
Please refer to the Programme List for information about the application period of individual taught postgraduate programmes.

Supporting Documents
Applicants should scan and submit the following documents to the Online Application System for evaluation of their applications:
Identity documents (e.g. Identity card*, passport or other travel document)
Official Transcript (for academic qualifications of undergraduate and/or postgraduate studies):
An official final transcript for a completed programme
The latest transcript if the programme is not yet completed
An official English-translated copy must be attached if the transcript is not in English. The interpretation of grades (in English) is also required.
Degree Certificate/Graduation Certificate/Proof of Study (as appropriate)
Verification proof by third-party authorities (for a completed programme):

Online Verification Report of Higher Education Degree Certificate (中國高等教育學位在綫驗証報告), both Chinese and English versions, issued by the CHSI (中國高等教育學生信息網) for qualification(s) awarded by universities in Mainland China
Online Query of Overseas Credentials Evaluation Reports國(境)外學歷學位認証書 issued by the Chinese Service Center for Scholarly Exchange (CSCSE) (教育部留學服務中心) (applicable to Mainland applicants with qualifications awarded by overseas universities)

Proof of English language proficiency, e.g. IETLS, TOEFL Score Report (applicable to applicants whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent, is not from a tertiary institution where English is used as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the degree programme)
Personal statement
Reference letters (if any)

*Do not upload copy of HKID. Local students will be required to present HKID for verification upon registration.

Entry Requirements

For a Master’s Degree, an applicant shall normally:
hold a relevant bachelor’s degree awarded by a tertiary education institution recognised for this purpose by the University; or
have obtained an equivalent qualification; or
have provided satisfactory evidence of academic and professional attainment.

English Language Requirements
In addition to the above, an applicant whose degree, or exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent, was not awarded by a tertiary institution where English was used as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain
• a minimum score of 79 in TOEFL (Internet-Based Test) 1 ; or
• a band score of 6.0 or above in IELTS 1 ; or
• a minimum score of 450 or above in the College English Test Band 6 (CET-6), with proof of competency in spoken English 2 ; or
• an equivalent score in a recognised test; or
• an equivalent qualification to prove his/her English language proficiency, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

1 For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purposes, the University only accepts results from a single test sitting. The test must be conducted at a recognised test centre and fall within the two-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort.
2 For CET-6 results submitted for admission purposes, the University accepts only those obtained within the five-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort. Applicants must also demonstrate competency in spoken English through an oral interview conducted by the Programme and/or obtain grade B or above in the CET-6 Speaking English Test.
*Interviews may be conducted if deemed necessary by the Admissions Coordinator and the Programme Director.

learningObjectives: Diversified programme structure reflecting contemporary societal trends:

The programme takes into account the trend of globalisation and offers a wide range of up-to-date professional electives. Students can choose from a wide range of subjects aligned with their interests and career aspirations.
Extensive Teaching Mode
Emphasis on practical learning experiences and extensive professional collaboration curriculum:

Part of the programme is delivered through experiential learning, exchange sojourns, guest lectures, and international conferences. Students will be asked to attend and present at various international academic conferences. The programme also provides students with opportunities for overseas study trips and exchanges in the UK, South Korea, etc. In addition, some courses collaborate with different overseas and local universities and professional institutions, and students are able to connect with different experts in the field and other post-graduate students around the world, thereby broadening their global perspectives.

Programme Structure

China ’ s growing influence on the international stage has made its role in global governance increasingly important. China ’ s “ One Belt, One Road ” initiative has not only reshaped the region ’ s economic and trade routes but also fostered cooperation and exchanges between different countries. On the other hand, entering the post-pandemic era, the changing global geopolitical tensions and the national development plans of China provide another set of opportunities and challenges for the country. The Master of China and Regional Development Studies (CRDS) programme at Lingnan University aims to provide students with knowledge in various aspects of the relationship between China and Asian and African countries as well as a thorough understanding of both the country ’ s internal and external affairs and to help them to excel in this field of study and to find multi-dimensional career opportunities. Students can choose from four speciali s ations:

Master of China and Regional Development Studies (China-Africa Studies, CAS)

Master of China and Regional Development Studies (China and East Asia Studies, CES)

Master of China and Regional Development Studies (Greater Bay Area Studies, GBAS)

Master of China and Regional Development Studies (for those who prefer not to specialise in either CAS, CES or GBAS)

Entry requirements

Entry requirements

For a Master’s Degree, an applicant shall normally:
hold a relevant bachelor’s degree awarded by a tertiary education institution recognised for this purpose by the University; or
have obtained an equivalent qualification; or
have provided satisfactory evidence of academic and professional attainment.

English Language Requirements

In addition to the above, an applicant whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution, is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain:

a minimum score of 79 (internet-based test) in TOEFL 1 ; or
a band score of 6.0 or above in IELTS 1 ; or
a minimum score of 450 or above in the College English Test Band 6 (CET-6), with proof of competency in spoken English 2 ; or
an equivalent score in a recognised test; or
an equivalent qualification to prove his/her English language proficiency, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

1） For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purposes, the University only accepts results from a single test sitting. The test must be conducted at a recognised test centre and fall within the two-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort.

2） For CET-6 results submitted for admission purposes, the University accepts only those obtained within the five-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort. Applicants must also demonstrate competency in spoken English through an oral interview conducted by the Programme and/or obtain grade B or above in the CET-6 Speaking English Test.

Application Procedures

Submission of Application

Applicants may submit applications for admission through the University's Online Admission System at https://apply.ln.edu.hk/ . Upon successful submission of the application, applicants will receive an application acknowledgement by email.

Programme Leaflet

Programme Overview

As an emerging global power, China exerts significant political and economic influence across the Asia-Pacific region. This is exemplified by its Belt and Road Initiative and the Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership (RCEP) Agreement, which have had a profound impact on the economies of many Asian countries, particularly in the infrastructure sector. Given China's importance to global development and geopolitics, its engagement in Asia and Africa merits in-depth study. However, current academic programmes and research lack specialised courses focusing on China's involvement in East Asia. The CRDS programme has been established to fill this gap, providing opportunities for those who wish to deepen their understanding of China – East Asia relations and to pursue careers in this field.

Unique Features

International Vision

Cultivating students’ global perspective to nurture talent with an international vision: This programme aims to broaden students’ horizons and develop their abilities for objective analysis. The CRDS programme enables students to face challenges in a rapidly changing environment with confidence and nurtures future pillars who contribute to the development of society.

Diversified Content

Diversified programme structure reflecting contemporary societal trends:
The programme takes into account the trend of globalisation and offers a wide range of up-to-date professional electives. Students can choose from a wide range of subjects aligned with their interests and career aspirations.

Extensive Teaching Mode

Emphasis on practical learning experiences and extensive professional collaboration curriculum:
Part of the programme is delivered through experiential learning, exchange sojourns, guest lectures, and international conferences. Students will be asked to attend and present at various international academic conferences. The programme also provides students with opportunities for overseas study trips and exchanges in the UK, South Korea, etc. In addition, some courses collaborate with different overseas and local universities and professional institutions, and students are able to connect with different experts in the field and other post-graduate students around the world, thereby broadening their global perspectives.

Current Student Sharing

Career Development in Mainland China

PENG Zijian (Graduate of 23-24)

Lingnan University has a strong academic atmosphere with many opportunities to participate in various academic activities. I often take part in these activities, whether they are academic lectures or exchanges with other universities in Hong Kong. The teachers in the CRS program have also been very helpful to me; they are very willing to discuss different issues with me. Each specialization in CRS has its own distinct characteristics. The research direction I chose, the Greater Bay Area studies, provided me with a valuable opportunity to visit and exchange ideas with South China Normal University. CRS has given me a completely new understanding of the discipline and specialization, allowing me to have a more comprehensive view of my research direction and life planning.

CHEN Xu (Graduate of 23-24)

A year at Lingnan CRDS has been a time of simultaneous growth in academic abilities and career goals. The rigorous curriculum, attentive and responsible mentors, and training with an international perspective have allowed me to progress more solidly on the road to public service. For students aspiring to enter the public service sector, this place not only provides a professionally accredited 'stepping stone' but also cultivates genuinely useful policy thinking—this is what I most want to share.

QIN Weijing (Graduate of 23-24)

My time studying CRDS at Lingnan University in Hong Kong was an incredibly valuable and fulfilling experience. The program's curriculum is distinctive, integrating interdisciplinary knowledge, allowing me to comprehensively and deeply understand issues related to China and the region. Participating in international academic conferences enabled me to listen to scholars from around the world share their latest research findings and engage in discussions with them, which not only kept me at the forefront of academia but also enhanced my academic communication skills.

YANG Junling (Graduate of 23-24)

The one-year master's program has made me more competitive and allowed me to be certified in political science, which has reduced some of the competitive pressure in participating in civil service examinations. The all-English instruction in the program has laid a solid foundation for my potential future work related to foreign affairs. Moreover, the presentations interspersed throughout the course have boosted my confidence in civil service interviews and enhanced my ability to think structurally and express complex ideas clearly. I consider it fortunate to study at Lingnan University in the CRDS program, as I have learned a great deal of new knowledge and broadened my international perspective. I believe this is an excellent platform for students who aspire to enter public service, allowing us to leverage our 'cross-sector' backgrounds to contribute unique value to regional development!

CRDS Career Prospect
Alumni Sharing

Caree prospects for graduates

Upon completion of the programme, students will be equipped with critical analytical skills in a wide range of areas, particularly related to regional development, enriching their career prospects. It will be of great value to graduates who aspire to careers in the civil service, journalism and publishing, international private or public sector, non-governmental organisations, education, tourism, cultural organisations and public relations.

CRDS Alumni Career Sharing

We recently held the CRDS Alumni Career Sharing event, where distinguished alumni shared their career experiences and insights with our community. The event covered several key topics. Alumni provided advice on helping attendees navigate and avoid common pitfalls. Participants learned how our guests chose their current industries, including their thought processes, industry outlooks, and how they aligned personal interests with career goals. Alumni shared details on their job search journeys, including resume and interview preparation, overcoming challenges, and key lessons learned. Additionally, they offered valuable advice on career planning, academic development, internships, and networking from seasoned professionals. The event was a great success, providing valuable career information and inspiration to help attendees build a solid foundation for their future careers.

Current Student Sharing

Current Student Sharing (AY2024-25 Intake)

YANG Ruopu （class of 2025）

My year in CRDS at Lingnan University has been transformative, building versatile skills that open doors to diverse careers and further studies. I will carry these valuable "CRDS qualities" forward into a broader future. I chose CRDS for practical reasons—its classification under Law provides an edge in public sector exams. At the same time, its interdisciplinary approach, integrating politics, economics, and regional studies, acts like a master key, offering exceptional flexibility in my career path. Simply put, CRDS has given my professional future room to adapt and grow.

LIU Wenzhong（class of 2025）

The learning journey over the past year has left a deep imprint on me. The CRDS teachers come from different countries and regions, and the broad perspectives and interdisciplinary ways of thinking they bring have inspired me to continually break out of conventional analytical frameworks. In the second semester, we delved into various enterprises, museums, and temples throughout the Greater Bay Area for field studies, bringing the insights gained from these visits into the classroom, where they complemented the classroom knowledge. When knowledge is filled with a sense of reality, it can take root deep within one’s heart.

LI Boya（class of 2025）

This year's learning experience has been rich and fulfilling. The CRDS program integrates knowledge from multiple disciplines, with a curriculum leaning towards political science. The teachers incorporate the latest case studies to provide us with different perspectives for analyzing problems, which greatly aids our understanding and application of theoretical knowledge, especially beneficial for students aiming for public sector exams. Looking back over this past year of study, I believe the greatest gain has been the formation of a more systematic and critical perspective on regional studies. Whether in classroom debates or case analyses, my cognitive framework, personal skills, and teamwork abilities have been continually challenged and refined.
