Brief description of the courses
1. DSM701 Research Methodology in Smart Manufacturing
This course provides a critical foundation in advanced research methodologies for addressing very complex and significant challenges in smart manufacturing and technological innovation. Students will engage with cutting-edge mixed-methods frameworks, data integration techniques, and computational research designs that span computer science, data analytics, operations research, human factors engineering, and sustainability studies. Through rigorous workshops, the course emphasizes the creation and justification of novel methodological approaches capable of generating significant and moriginal contributions to the field. Participants will develop the expertise to innovatively design, creatively execute, and critically evaluate research that integrates diverse epistemological traditions and technological paradigms, while adhering to the highest standards of ethical and scholarly rigor in a cross-disciplinary context. This course feeds into the subsequent courses in the programme, especially where the mixed-methods frameworks and data integration techniques are required.

2. DSM702 Frontiers in Smart Manufacturing and Industry 4.0
This course provides a systematic frontier knowledge to the core theories and practical applications of advanced manufacturing technologies and the smart transformation under Industry 4.0. Key topics include advanced manufacturing processes, intelligent manufacturing systems, the Industrial Internet of Things, Low-altitude technology, robotics and automation, and additive manufacturing. Emphasising data-driven manufacturing, system integration, and intelligent optimization, the course integrates the latest industry trends with case studies and project-based learning. The mixed-methods frameworks and data integration techniques are recommended to enhance the cross-course relationship. It prepares students to understand and apply high-end manufacturing and smart production systems, laying a solid foundation for future careers in smart factories, sustainable manufacturing, and interdisciplinary innovation sectors.

3. DSM703 Advanced Materials and Technology
This course offers a substantial and advanced knowledge of advanced functional materials, focusing on their structures, properties, fabrication techniques, and applications. Students will explore the relationship between material structure, properties, and performance across a range of innovative substances and composites. Key topics include nanostructured materials, high-performance alloys, smart and responsive materials, biodegradable polymers, composite reinforcement strategies, surface engineering, and material functionalization techniques. The course also addresses material selection criteria, durability under extreme conditions, and integration of new materials into digital production environments. Through case studies and hands-on projects, students will develop the ability to evaluate, select, and implement advanced materials for use in cutting-edge industrial applications.

4. DSM704 Additive and Advanced Manufacturing
This course offers an in-depth exploration of cutting-edge additive manufacturing technologies and their integration with advanced production systems through technical workshops, case analyses, and expert-led seminars. It examines how processes such as multi-material 3D printing, generative design, and digital thread implementation are transforming product development, customisation, and sustainability across sectors including aerospace, robotics, medical devices, and energy. The curriculum emphasizes technical mastery, innovation in material-process integration, and economic and ecological considerations in advanced manufacturing. Participants will develop the ability to lead the adoption and improvement of additive and advanced manufacturing technologies in
industrial and research settings.

5. DSM705 Robotics and Industrial Automation
This course provides advanced and forefront knowledge of robotics and industrial automation, focusing on the integration of intelligent robotic systems within very complexmanufacturing and service environments. It examines modelling, design, and control of robotic systems, collaborative and autonomous robots, AI-driven automation, and cyberphysicalproduction systems. Topics include kinematic and dynamic modelling, sensorfusion, machine vision, motion planning, human–robot interaction, distributed roboticsystems, and ethical implications of automation. Through lectures, case studies, simulationbasedexperiments, and research-driven projects, students will develop the expertise todesign, evaluate, and optimise robotic and automated systems that enhance productivity,
flexibility, and sustainability in next-generation industries.

6. DSM706 Engineering Management and Optimization
This course provides substantial knowledge of advanced management and optimization of advanced manufacturing systems. It addresses the strategic and operational dimensions of manufacturing, focusing on decision-making under uncertainty, sustainable production planning, supply chain integration, and data-driven optimization. Students will engage with advanced theories and methods from forefront operations research, systems engineering, and industrial analytics to design and evaluate innovative management strategies. Through lectures, modelling workshops, and research projects, students will develop the expertise to critically analyse complex manufacturing environments and propose optimised solutions that advance both academic and industrial practice.

7. DSM707 Modern Industrial Product Design
This course provides a systematic approach to designing and developing innovative products that integrate advanced technology, user-centred principles, and sustainable practices. It covers the entire development process from concept generation to prototyping, emphasising user-centred design, manufacturability, and environmental impact. Key topics include design thinking, product architecture planning, user experience research, design for manufacturability and assembly, rapid prototyping technologies, and sustainability-driven design strategies. Students will learn to transform technological concepts into functional, scalable, and socially meaningful product solutions. This course prepares students to lead product innovation in high-tech industries, equipping them with the skills to create nextgeneration smart products.

8. DSM708 Leadership and Innovation in Smart Manufacturing
This course is designed to cultivate strategic leadership and innovation management capabilities at the highest level. This course moves beyond technical mastery to focus on leading organizational transformation in complex industrial environments. Through a blend of seminars, workshops, and case studies led by academic and industrial leaders, students will critically examine theories of technological change, develop strategies for fostering innovation, and navigate the human and organizational challenges of digital transformation. The course prepares students to assume executive and entrepreneurial roles, driving sustainable and competitive advancements in the global manufacturing landscape.

9. DSM709 Green Energy and Storage Technology
This course introduces innovative energy and storage systems essential for sustainable manufacturing and smart urban infrastructure. Students will analyse the integration of renewable sources and advanced storage solutions into industrial and urban energy networks. Key topics include solar photovoltaics, wind power integration, hydrogen energy systems battery technologies (e.g., lithium-ion, solid-state), fuel cells, smart grid management, energy storage materials, grid stability, and life-cycle assessment of energy systems. The course also covers energy policy, economic feasibility, and system optimization for carbonsensitive environments. Through case analyses and technology evaluation projects, students will develop the skills to design, assess, and implement efficient and scalable green energy solutions.

10. DSM710 Carbon Neutrality and Environmental Governance
This course examines the technological pathways, policy frameworks, and governance strategies for achieving carbon neutrality in industrial and urban contexts. Students will learn to analyse, design, and implement systems that reduce carbon emissions while supporting sustainable development. Key topics include carbon accounting and emission tracking, carbon capture utilisation and storage (CCUS), circular economy models, climate policy and carbon trading mechanisms, environmental impact assessment, ESG compliance, and green transition roadmaps for smart cities and industries. Through case studies, policy simulations, and project-based exercises, students will develop the ability to integrate technological solutions with regulatory and social dimensions, preparing them to lead crosssector initiatives in decarbonization and sustainable governance.

11. DSM711 Digital and Sustainable Industrial Systems
This course offers a comprehensive exploration of the integration of digital technologies and sustainable practices in modern industrial systems. It examines how digital transformation can drive efficiency, resilience, and environmental responsibility across manufacturing and supply chain operations. Key topics include additive manufacturing, smart energy management, circular economy models, green supply chain design, carbon footprint tracking, and ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) compliance frameworks. The course combines theoretical principles with real-world case studies and simulation-based projects to illustrate how digital tools can monitor, optimise, and reduce ecological impacts while maintaining economic viability. Students will learn to design and evaluate industrial systems that are not only technologically advanced but also environmentally sustainable and socially responsible. The mixed-methods frameworks and data integration techniques are recommended to enhance the cross-course relationship. The course prepares graduates to lead in the development and implementation of digitally enhanced sustainable industrial systems.

12. DSM712 Special Topics in Smart Manufacturing
This course is an advanced course designed to explore the most dynamic and emerging research trends within the field of smart manufacturing. Moving beyond conventional manufacturing processes, the course focuses on the convergence of multiple disruptive technological directions. Students will engage with cutting-edge topics, including AI-driven industrial decision-making and optimization, AI-accelerated material discovery and design, the latest breakthroughs in carbon-negative technologies, and the industrialization progress of next-generation energy materials like perovskite photovoltaics. The course adopts a unique seminar-style format, featuring rotating lectures from leading experts within the university and industry, each sharing their latest research findings and unique insights into future trends. Upon completion, students will be positioned at the forefront of academic and industrial application, equipped with the vision and capabilities necessary to lead future manufacturing transformations.

13. DSM721 Doctoral Thesis
The Doctoral Thesis represents the culminating component of the Doctor of Smart Manufacturing programme. It requires students to conceive, design, and execute a substantial body of original research that makes a significant and original contribution to the forefront of smart manufacturing and its interrelated disciplines. Under supervision, students will demonstrate a critical overview of their field, deal with very complex and novel issues, and produce creative and original responses to fundamental challenges in areas such as advanced materials, robotics and automation, industrial AI, and sustainable digital transformation. The thesis (40,000-60,000 words) must demonstrate scholarly rigour, interdisciplinary integration, methodological innovation, and a high degree of autonomy, ultimately advancing both academic knowledge and industrial or societal practice. This course is graded on a pass/fail basis and does not count towards GPA.

Admission

Admission Requirements
Application Procedures
Fees

Admission

Admission Requirements
General Qualifications
Candidates to be admitted to the DSM programme should:
Hold a Master's degree in engineering, sciences, energy, or a related discipline, awarded by a tertiary education institution as recognised for admission purposes by Lingnan; or

Have equivalent qualifications and experience, considered on a case-by-case basis.

English Language Requirements
In addition to the above, applicants whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain:
A minimum score of 79 in TOEFL Internet-Based Test 1 ; or

A band score of 6.0 or above in IELTS 1 ; or

A minimum score of 450 or above in College English Test Band 6 (CET-6), with proof of competency in spoken English 2 ; or

An equivalent score in a recognised test; or

An equivalent qualification to prove his/her English language proficiency, which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

1 For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purposes, the University accepts only results from a single test sitting. The test must be conducted at a recognised test centre and fall within the two-year validity period , counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort.
2 For CET-6 results submitted for admission purposes, the University accepts only those obtained within the five-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort. Applicants must also demonstrate competency in spoken English through an oral interview conducted by the Programme and/or obtain grade B or above in the CET-6 Speaking English Test.

For Part-time Study

Applicants applying for the part-time mode must:
Hold a valid visa or entry permit that authories study in Hong Kong; and

Ensure their visa status permits part-time study for the entire duration of the programme.

Confidential Recommendation
An applicant should invite his/her referee(s) to complete the Confidential Recommendation form and return the form in sealed envelopes directly to the DSM Programme Office stated below by post as soon as possible after you submit your online application (no later than the official application deadline of the programme).

Recommendation Form [Download form]

Address for returning the Confidential Recommendation form:
DSM Programme, Room UG06, Lau Chung Him Building, Lingnan University, 8 Castle Peak Road, Tuen Mun, Hong Kong
嶺南大學劉仲謙樓UG樓UG06室

Application Procedures
To apply for DSM, please submit your application at the Online Application System . Applicants should upload the following application documents to the online application:

Identity card, passport or travel document

Transcripts

Consent form* (Academic awards verification for entry) [Download form]

English proficiency test report (e.g. IELTS/ TOEFL)

MOI statement (if English is the usual medium of teaching and assessment at the university of which your degree is obtained)

Master's degree certificate/ Graduation certificate

China Qualification Verification (CQV) Report downloadable from https://www.chsi.com.cn/en/ (Applicable if you graduated with a degree awarded by a university in Mainland China)

Personal Statement (Please write a personal statement in English outlining your academic achievements, choice of study, career goals and why apply to the DSM programme.)

Supplementary Information (Please provide any further information (e.g. CV), in English, in support of your application)

Other supporting documents to substantiate qualifications in your application. (If you are an applicant from Mainland China, please provide a sheet stating your contact information in Chinese, i.e. name, address and etc.)

Application Deadline
2026 intake: 15 July 2026
*Late application will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Fees
Application Fee: HK$600
Tuition Fee: HK$560,000

programmeName: Master of Liberal Sciences
school: Wu Jieh Yee School of Interdisciplinary Studies
summary: The Master of Liberal Sciences (MLS) programme is a great demonstration of Lingnan University's exemplification on the institution’s long-standing commitment to liberal arts education while adapting to contemporary education market demands.
suitableBackgrounds: The term "liberal sciences" is a combination of "liberal arts" and "sciences”, referring to fields of study that encompass a broad range of disciplines within the humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and formal sciences.

"Liberal sciences" refers to an interdisciplinary educational approach that combines elements of both liberal arts and scientific disciplines, integrating both scientific knowledge and the analytical and reflective skills associated with the liberal arts, aiming to provide a comprehensive and holistic education.

In response to this growing demand and with the goal of equipping students with interdisciplinary knowledge and skills that bridge the gap between traditional disciplines and emerging trends in the workforce, Lingnan University is dedicated to offering the Master of Liberal Sciences (MLS) programme.

The MLS programme integrates technology with traditional disciplines, equipping students with sports, music, media and arts talents, and students with relevant background and experience, with advanced tools include coding and programming, project/event management, product design, AI and data analysis to support their career development, as well as advancement.

By offering a “Liberal Sciences” taught postgraduate (TPg) programme to sports, music, media, and arts talents, and students with relevant background and experience, the MLS programme not only extends the horizon of conventional “sciences” education, but also enriches the content of liberal arts education of Lingnan University.
This concentration is designed to equip sports background students with the analytical skills and technologies to establish a solid career in the related field.
Graduates are prepared for careers in sports coaching, fitness training, health education, and sports events/business management.

Applied Arts in TechMedia (科技新媒體應用藝術) This concentration focuses on the intersection of technology with media and arts, preparing students with relevant backgrounds to navigate and innovate within the rapidly evolving digital landscape.

WJYSIS MIS - Master of Liberal Sciences

Master of Liberal Sciences

Programme Highlights

The Master of Liberal Sciences (MLS) programme is a great demonstration of Lingnan University's exemplification on the institution’s long-standing commitment to liberal arts education while adapting to contemporary education market demands.

The term "liberal sciences" is a combination of "liberal arts" and "sciences”, referring to fields of study that encompass a broad range of disciplines within the humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and formal sciences. The liberal arts traditionally include subjects such as literature, philosophy, history, and the arts, emphasising critical thinking, communication skills, and a broad-based education. "Liberal sciences" refers to an interdisciplinary educational approach that combines elements of both liberal arts and scientific disciplines, integrating both scientific knowledge and the analytical and reflective skills associated with the liberal arts, aiming to provide a comprehensive and holistic education.

Learn More

In recent years, with the advancement of social productivity and the continuous improvement of living standards, public interest has shifted from solely focusing on productivity improvement and technological progress to broader areas including personal wellness, mental health and entertainment. This shift has led to the rapid growth of industries related to sports and training, music and short video production, leisure and travel, beauty and design. The prosperous of these industries has significantly driven the development of research and education in related fields.

Against this backdrop, the Wu Jieh Yee School of Interdisciplinary Studies (WJYSIS) of Lingnan University has recognized the increasing need for master's-level professionals in fields like sports, music, media, arts, and other relevant areas.

In response to this growing demand and with the goal of equipping students with interdisciplinary knowledge and skills that bridge the gap between traditional disciplines and emerging trends in the workforce, Lingnan University is dedicated to offering the Master of Liberal Sciences (MLS) programme. The aim of the programme is to fully utilise Lingnan University's unique resources in sports, music, media and arts education thereby contributing to the diversified development of the university.

Programme Structure

Programme Structure

MLS students are expected to complete 30 credits. Apart from the four core courses (12 credits) and one or two practice course(s) (6 credits), for students who choose to peruse a concentration, they must:

(1) take the three concentration required courses (9 credits) of the respective concentration and one programme elective course (3 credits); and

(2) complete their selected practice course(s), with the field trips, events and/or projects in the practice course(s) associated with their chosen concentration.

For students who choose not to peruse a concentration, they can choose any 4 courses (12 credits) from the concentration required courses and/or programme elective courses to fulfil the graduation requirement.

Core Courses (Four courses, 12 credits)
Programme Elective Courses (Choose only one, 3 credits)

LSC511 Globalisation and Culture Evolution

LSC502 Scientific Study and Research Methods (3 credits)
LSC512 Pedagogy for Advanced Education

LSC514 Leadership and Interpersonal Skills

Practice Courses (Any 6 credits)

LSC506 MLS Event Workshop (3 credits)

About Us

Programme Highlight

The Master of Liberal Sciences (MLS) programme is a great demonstration of Lingnan University's exemplification on the institution's long-standing commitment to liberal arts education while adapting to contemporary education market demands.

The term "liberal sciences" is a combination of "liberal arts" and "sciences”, referring to fields of study that encompass a broad range of disciplines within the humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and formal sciences. The liberal arts traditionally include subjects such as literature, philosophy, history, and the arts, emphasising critical thinking, communication skills, and a broad-based education. "Liberal sciences" refers to an interdisciplinary educational approach that combines elements of both liberal arts and scientific disciplines, integrating both scientific knowledge and the analytical and reflective skills associated with the liberal arts, aiming to provide a comprehensive and holistic education.

As science and technology become crucial areas in modern workplaces, and fields like sports, music, media, arts and relevant fields gain socio-economic significance, the Wu Jieh Yee School of Interdisciplinary Studies (WJYSIS), leveraging its existing specialty, has innovatively extended Lingnan's liberal arts education into these above-mentioned fields. The MLS programme integrates technology with traditional disciplines, equipping students with sports, music, media and arts talents, and students with relevant background and experience, with advanced tools include coding and programming, project/event management, product design, AI and data analysis to support their career development, as well as advancement. The programme reflects global educational shifts and responds to the evolving socio-economic trends.

Advancing research and education in interdisciplinary and cross-disciplinary fields represents a commitment to innovate at the academic frontier and pay special attention to niche areas. By offering a “Liberal Sciences” taught postgraduate (TPg) programme to sports, music, media, and arts talents, and students with relevant background and experience, the MLS programme not only extends the horizon of conventional “sciences” education, but also enriches the content of liberal arts education of Lingnan University. We are confident that such a well-educated group of special talented students and cross-disciplinary scholars will have a solid and rapid career advancement.

The MLS programme is a one-year full time master’s programme with Mandarin Chinese as its medium of instruction.

MLS students are expected to complete 30 credits. Apart from the four core courses (12 credits) and one or two practice course(s) (6 credits), for students who choose to peruse a concentration, they must:

(1) take the three concentration required courses (9 credits) of the respective concentration and one programme elective course (3 credits); and

(2) complete their selected practice course(s), with the field trips, events and/or projects in the practice course(s) associated with their chosen concentration.

For students who choose not to peruse a concentration, they can choose any 4 courses (12 credits) from the concentration required courses and/or programme elective courses to fulfil the graduation requirement.

The MLS programme offers three concentrations:

Applied Sports and Health Science (應用運動及健康科學) [For Fall intake only]
This is a concentration that integrates scientific principles with practical applications in sports, exercise, and health management. It covers key topics such as exercise physiology, sports nutrition, health promotion and development of associate business including event management and business planning. This concentration is designed to equip sports background students with the analytical skills and technologies to establish a solid career in the related field. Graduates are prepared for careers in sports coaching, fitness training, health education, and sports events/business management. With an emphasis on evidence-based practices and interdisciplinary approaches, the concentration enables students to contribute meaningfully to public health initiatives and meet the growing societal demand for healthier lifestyles.

Music and Performance (音樂與表演)
In the era of high technology and AI, music and the performing arts are undergoing significant transformations. Technological innovations have revolutionised music production, performance, appreciation, as well as related education and business practices, moving beyond traditional models. This concentration is designed to equip students with a solid foundation in music and performance education, alongside the integration of traditional music and performance skills, with the latest technological advancements. Students of this concentration will learn how to utilise AI in areas including composition, education, performance, and business operations. They will be well-prepared with a strong academic foundation to pursue careers in profession of education, the music industry, performance arts, and/or commercial entertainment.

Applied Arts in TechMedia (科技新媒體應用藝術)
This concentration focuses on the intersection of technology with media and arts, preparing students with relevant backgrounds to navigate and innovate within the rapidly evolving digital landscape. As one of the most in-demand fields today, new media blends traditional media communication concepts with high-tech content creation, distribution channels, and promotional platforms. This concentration covers topics such as digital media production, interactive design, visual communication, and the integration of traditional artistic techniques with modern technology. Students will learn to create compelling visual and multimedia content, utilising the latest tools and software in digital art and media. The concentration emphasises both creative and technical skills, enabling graduates to apply their knowledge in diverse fields, including digital marketing, media production, entertainment, and virtual reality. By blending artistic expression with cutting-edge technology, this concentration aims to produce versatile professionals who can lead and innovate in the expanding world of digital media and arts.

For students who choose a concentration, they must complete all three concentration required courses for the award of the concentration.

This concentration covers topics such as digital media production, interactive design, visual communication, and the integration of traditional artistic techniques with modern technology. Students will learn to create compelling visual and multimedia content, utilising the latest tools and software in digital art and media. The concentration emphasises both creative and technical skills, enabling graduates to apply their knowledge in diverse fields, including digital marketing, media production, entertainment, and virtual reality. By blending artistic expression with cutting-edge technology, this concentration aims to produce versatile professionals who can lead and innovate in the expanding world of digital media and arts.

For students who choose a concentration, they must complete all three concentration required courses for the award of the concentration.

Minimum Admission Requirements

Academic Qualification(s)
H old a bachelor's degree awarded by a tertiary education institution as recognised for admission purposes by the University; or

H ave equivalent qualifications; or

H ave provided satisfactory evidence of academic and professional attainment, considered on case-by-case basis

English Proficiency
In addition to the above, applicants whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain:
A band score of 5.5 or above in the International English Language Testing System (IELTS) (Academic) 1 ; or

A minimum score of 46 (internet-based test) in the Test of English as a Foreign Language (TOEFL) 1 ; or

An equivalent score in a recognised test; or
An equivalent qualification to prove his/her English language proficiency which will be considered on a case-by-case basis

1 For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are conducted at a test centre and within the validity period, viz. 2 years , from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort .

Concentration-specific Requirements
For students to be admitted to a concentration, on top of the above criteria, the applicant shall have a concentration relevant background. Those applying for admission to specific concentration should provide the following information and documents:
Proof of related academic or work background

Portfolio, award certificates and other supporting materials

Any other supplementary information

Confidential Recommendation
An applicant should invite his/her referee(s) to complete the Confidential Recommendation form and return the form in sealed envelopes directly to the MLS Programme Office stated below by post as soon as possible after you submit your online application (no later than the official application deadline of the programme).

Recommendation Form 2026/27 [Download form]

Address for returning the Confidential Recommendation form:
MLS Programme, Room UG06, Lau Chung Him Building, Lingnan University, 8 Castle Peak Road, Tuen Mun, Hong Kong
嶺南大學劉仲謙樓UG樓UG06室

Application Procedures
To apply for MLS, please submit your application at the Online Application System . Applicants should upload the following application documents to the online application:

Identity card, passport or travel document
Transcripts
Consent form 2026/27* (Academic awards verification for 2026/27 entry) [Download form]

English proficiency test report (e.g. IELTS/ TOEFL)
MOI statement (if English is the usual medium of teaching and assessment at the university of which your degree is obtained)
Bachelor's degree certificate/ Graduation certificate
China Qualification Verification (CQV) Report downloadable from https://www.chsi.com.cn/en/ (Applicable if you graduated with a degree awarded by a university in Mainland China)
Personal Statement (Please write a personal statement in English outlining your academic achievements, choice of study, career goals and why apply to the MLS programme.)
Supplementary Information (Please provide any further information (e.g. CV), in English, in support of your application)
Other supporting documents to substantiate qualifications in your application. (If you are an applicant from Mainland China, please provide a sheet stating your contact information in Chinese, i.e. name, address and etc.)

Application Deadline
Fall 2026 intake: 31 July 2026
*Late application will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Fees
Application Fee: HK$400
Tuition Fee: HK$188,000

Programme Highlight

The Master of Liberal Sciences (MLS) programme is a great demonstration of Lingnan University's exemplification on the institution's long-standing commitment to liberal arts education while adapting to contemporary education market demands.

The term "liberal sciences" is a combination of "liberal arts" and "sciences”, referring to fields of study that encompass a broad range of disciplines within the humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and formal sciences. The liberal arts traditionally include subjects such as literature, philosophy, history, and the arts, emphasising critical thinking, communication skills, and a broad-based education. "Liberal sciences" refers to an interdisciplinary educational approach that combines elements of both liberal arts and scientific disciplines, integrating both scientific knowledge and the analytical and reflective skills associated with the liberal arts, aiming to provide a comprehensive and holistic education.

As science and technology become crucial areas in modern workplaces, and fields like sports, music, media, arts and relevant fields gain socio-economic significance, the Wu Jieh Yee School of Interdisciplinary Studies (WJYSIS), leveraging its existing specialty, has innovatively extended Lingnan's liberal arts education into these above-mentioned fields. The MLS programme integrates technology with traditional disciplines, equipping students with sports, music, media and arts talents, and students with relevant background and experience, with advanced tools include coding and programming, project/event management, product design, AI and data analysis to support their career development, as well as advancement. The programme reflects global educational shifts and responds to the evolving socio-economic trends.

Advancing research and education in interdisciplinary and cross-disciplinary fields represents a commitment to innovate at the academic frontier and pay special attention to niche areas. By offering a “Liberal Sciences” taught postgraduate (TPg) programme to sports, music, media, and arts talents, and students with relevant background and experience, the MLS programme not only extends the horizon of conventional “sciences” education, but also enriches the content of liberal arts education of Lingnan University. We are confident that such a well-educated group of special talented students and cross-disciplinary scholars will have a solid and rapid career advancement.

The MLS programme is a one-year full time master’s programme with Mandarin Chinese as its medium of instruction.

MSc in Cross-disciplinary Technologies+

MSc in Cross-disciplinary Technologies+

Programme Highlights

The future belongs to versatile talents possess cross-disciplinary knowledge.

Nowadays, the mainstream of technology and industry development is the integration of multiple disciplines. Both emerging and traditional industries are rapidly evolving to become interdisciplinary industries. Driven by such industrial transformation, the demand for interdisciplinary talents in different fields are constantly increasing.

Learn More

In the foreseeable future, many jobs that rely on repetitive skills would be replaced by robots and artificial intelligence. At the same time, core positions in various industries will be held by talents with essential competencies. It can be expected that talents who master knowledge and skills in two or more core disciplinaries will be the backbone and leaders of future enterprises, enabling them to achieve greater personal value in their career.

Based on extensive research on the job market in Greater China and global talent market, the Wu Jieh Yee School of Interdisciplinary Studies (WJYSIS) of Lingnan University has identified that the core knowledge and skills needed for the future are: artificial intelligence, computer programming, advanced manufacturing, e-commerce and international e-business, new media and management, energy management, finance and accounting, and enterprise management and innovation.

Therefore, in order to cultivate core talents that meet the needs of future industries, under the leadership Professor Chen Xi, the Dean and internationally renowned scientist, SIS has designed the Master of Science in Cross-disciplinary Technology+ (MScCT+) programme. This practical programme is tailored according to the demand of the current talent market. The aim of the programme is to establish the most solid knowledge and skills foundation for the success and career advancement of every graduate.

Science

Carbon Neutralisation and Sustainability

Computer Science

Programme Highlight

Programme Highlight

The future belongs to versatile talents possess cross-disciplinary knowledge.

Nowadays, the mainstream of technology and industry development is the integration of multiple disciplines. Both emerging and traditional industries are rapidly evolving to become interdisciplinary industries. Driven by such industrial transformation, the demand for interdisciplinary talents in different fields are constantly increasing.

In the foreseeable future, many jobs that rely on repetitive skills would be replaced by robots and artificial intelligence. At the same time, core positions in various industries will be held by talents with essential competencies. It can be expected that talents who master knowledge and skills in two or more core disciplinaries will be the backbone and leaders of future enterprises, enabling them to achieve greater personal value in their career.

Based on extensive research on the job market in Greater China and global talent market, the Wu Jieh Yee School of Interdisciplinary Studies (WJYSIS) of Lingnan University has identified that the core knowledge and skills needed for the future are: artificial intelligence, computer programming, advanced manufacturing, e-commerce and international e-business, new media and management, energy management, finance and accounting, and enterprise management and innovation.

Therefore, in order to cultivate core talents that meet the needs of future industries, under the leadership Professor Chen Xi, the Dean and internationally renowned scientist, SIS has designed the Master of Science in Cross-disciplinary Technologies+ (MScCT+) programme. This practical programme is tailored according to the demand of the current talent market. The aim of the programme is to establish the most solid knowledge and skills foundation for the success and career advancement of every graduate.

Everything is set for our students to become the most sought-after newcomers in the job market. We aim to fulfill the latest demands of the job market and cultivate the most in-demand talents for industries.

When searching for high-paying positions in the most popular industries on the mainstream recruitment websites, it is not difficult to find that most of these positions describe the ideal candidates as the ones who masters knowledge and skills in more than one discipline. The MScCT+ programme is the first of its kind in Hong Kong and one of the few in the world that integrates professional knowledge from multiple fields into a master’s degree.

The MScCT+ programme has three highlights in its curriculum design:
Firstly, the core courses provide students with essential knowledge and skills that are most needed by business and enterprises, laying a solid foundation for graduates’ career development;
Secondly, the elective courses emphasize the application of problem-solving and business operation skills, training its graduate as a hands-on practitioner;
Thirdly, it provides eight concentrations for students to choose from, allowing them to choose their own study path. Regardless of their undergraduate major, students have the autonomy to define their future development direction according to their personal interests and career development plans.

Programme Structure

MScCT+ students are expected to complete 30 credits, six core courses, one required course and three elective courses. Students who wish to pursue a concentration shall choose 3 elective courses related to that concentration. Students can also select courses according to their personal interest without defining their concentrations.

Core courses (6 courses, a total of 18 credits):
CDT501 Computer Systems and Programming Basics
CDT502 Principles of Data Analytics and Decision Making
CDT503 Control System and Artificial Intelligence Applications
CDT504 Product and Process Design and Implementation
CDT505 Project Management: Concepts and Tools
CDT506 Leadership and Interpersonal Skills for Technology Professionals

Elective courses^ (any 3 courses, 9 credits):
CDT511 Material, Processing Technology and Equipment
CDT512 Automobile Construction
CDT513 Modern Electronic Business
CDT514 Supply Chain Management
CDT515 New Media and Video
CDT516 SAAS and Smart Manufacturing System Design
CDT517 Establish Business Mindset for Technology Professionals
CDT518 Economics and Financial Analysis
CDT519 Data-driven Critical and Analytical Thinking (from 2025-26)
ESG521 Conventional Energy and Renewable Energy
ESG535 Sustainable Urban Energy Planning
ESG536 Green Finance and Investment

Required Course* (3 credits):
CDT507 Master of Technology Field Trip
CDT508 Master of Technology Capstone Project

^Upon completion of 3 elective courses of specific concentration, students would be awarded the concentration.
*Depending on the concentration, students must take either one as a required course. For students who do not declare any concentration, they can choose either courses.

Computer Science

Digital Media Business Management

International e-Business

Renewable Energy Management

*Product Design
(from 2025-26)

Minimum Admission Requirements

Academic Qualification(s)
Hold a relevant bachelor’s degree awarded by a tertiary education institution recognised for this purpose by the University; or
Have obtained an equivalent qualification; or
Have provided satisfactory evidence of academic and professional attainment.

English Proficiency
In addition to the above, applicants whose degree or, exceptionally, an alternative qualification deemed equivalent from a tertiary institution is not using English as the medium of instruction (MOI) for the programme should obtain:
A minimum score of 79 (internet-based test) in the Test of English as a Foreign Language (TOEFL) 1 ; or
A band score of 6.0 or above in the International English Language Testing System (IELTS) 1 , or
A minimum score of 450 or above in College Ebglish Test Band 6 (CET-6), with proof of competency in spoken English 2 ;
An equivalent score in a recognised test, or
An equivalent qualification to prove his/her English language proficiency which will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

1 For IELTS and TOEFL qualifications for admission purpose, the University only accepts results of tests that are conducted at a test centre and within the validity period, viz. 2 years , from the time of submission of an admission application for the specific intake cohort.
2 For CET-6 results submitted for admission purposes, the University accepts only those obtained within the five-year validity period, counted from the date of submission of the admission application for the specific intake cohort. Applicants must also demonstrate competency in spoken English through an oral interview conducted by the Programme and/or obtain grade B or above in the CET-6 Speaking English Test.

Confidential Recommendation
An applicant should invite his/her referee(s) to complete the Confidential Recommendation form and return the form in sealed envelopes directly to the MScCT+ Programme Office stated below by post as soon as possible after you submit your online application (no later than the official application deadline of the programme).

Recommendation Form 2026/27 [Download form]

Address for returning the Confidential Recommendation form:
MScCT Programme, Room UG06, Lau Chung Him Building, Lingnan University, 8 Castle Peak Road, Tuen Mun, Hong Kong
嶺南大學劉仲謙樓UG樓UG06室

Application Procedures
To apply for MScCT+, please submit your application at the Online Application System. Applicants should upload the following application documents to the online application:

Identity card, passport or travel document

Transcripts

Consent form 2026/27* (Academic awards verification for 2026/27 entry) [Download form]

English proficiency test report (e.g. IELTS/ TOEFL)

Bachelor's degree certificate/ Graduation certificate

China Qualification Verification (CQV) Report (Applicable if you graduated with a degree awarded by a university in Mainland China)

Personal Statement (Please write a personal statement of with a recommended length of 200-500 words outlining your academic achievements, choice of study and career goals.)

Programme Highlight

The future belongs to versatile talents possess cross-disciplinary knowledge.

Nowadays, the mainstream of technology and industry development is the integration of multiple disciplines. Both emerging and traditional industries are rapidly evolving to become interdisciplinary industries. Driven by such industrial transformation, the demand for interdisciplinary talents in different fields are constantly increasing.

In the foreseeable future, many jobs that rely on repetitive skills would be replaced by robots and artificial intelligence. At the same time, core positions in various industries will be held by talents with essential competencies. It can be expected that talents who master knowledge and skills in two or more core disciplinaries will be the backbone and leaders of future enterprises, enabling them to achieve greater personal value in their career.

Based on extensive research on the job market in Greater China and global talent market, the School of Interdisciplinary Studies (SIS) of Lingnan University has identified that the core knowledge and skills needed for the future are: artificial intelligence, computer programming, advanced manufacturing, e-commerce and international e-business, new media and management, energy management, finance and accounting, and enterprise management and innovation.

Therefore, in order to cultivate core talents that meet the needs of future industries, under the leadership Professor Chen Xi, the Dean and internationally renowned scientist, SIS has designed the Master of Science in Cross-disciplinary Technologies+ (MScCT+) programme. This practical programme is tailored according to the demand of the current talent market. The aim of the programme is to establish the most solid knowledge and skills foundation for the success and career advancement of every graduate.

Everything is set for our students to become the most sought-after newcomers in the job market. We aim to fulfill the latest demands of the job market and cultivate the most in-demand talents for industries.

When searching for high-paying positions in the most popular industries on the mainstream recruitment websites, it is not difficult to find that most of these positions describe the ideal candidates as the ones who masters knowledge and skills in more than one discipline. The MScCT+ programme is the first of its kind in Hong Kong and one of the few in the world that integrates professional knowledge from multiple fields into a master’s degree.

The MScCT+ programme has three highlights in its curriculum design:
Firstly, the core courses provide students with essential knowledge and skills that are most needed by business and enterprises, laying a solid foundation for graduates’ career development;
Secondly, the elective courses emphasize the application of problem-solving and business operation skills, training its graduate as a hands-on practitioner;
Thirdly, it provides eight concentrations for students to choose from, allowing them to choose their own study path. Regardless of their undergraduate major, students have the autonomy to define their future development direction according to their personal interests and career development plans.
